title: Daemon: implement ResizePane IPC + PaneExited event handlers
description:
tn-5ps8 added stub IpcRequest::ResizePane and DaemonEvent::PaneExited variants to therminal-protocol so the GUI's RemotePty backend can compile and round-trip messages. The server-side handlers in therminal-daemon/src/server.rs and session.rs need real implementations:

1. ResizePane: SessionManager::resize_pane(pane_id, cols, rows) should call the existing Pane::resize() method (already exists for the local Term) and propagate to the PTY master.
2. PaneExited: when a Pane's reader thread hits EOF (in DaemonPtyHandler::on_eof), broadcast DaemonEvent::PaneExited { session_id, pane_id, exit_code: Option<i32> } so attached GUI clients can tear down their RemotePty backend cleanly.

Until this lands, the daemon answers ResizePane with Error{ message: 'unimplemented' } and never emits PaneExited — RemotePty panes will not auto-close on shell exit.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
tn-5ps8 added stub `IpcRequest::ResizePane` and `DaemonEvent::PaneExited` variants to therminal-protocol so the GUI's RemotePty backend could compile. The server-side handlers are stubbed — daemon currently answers ResizePane with `Error{ message: "unimplemented" }` and never emits PaneExited. Until this lands, RemotePty panes don't resize and don't auto-close on shell exit.

## Task
1. **ResizePane handler**: in `crates/therminal-daemon/src/server.rs`, replace the unimplemented stub for `IpcRequest::ResizePane { session_id, pane_id, cols, rows }` with a real call into `SessionManager::resize_pane(session_id, pane_id, cols, rows)`. Add that method to SessionManager if it doesn't exist — it should look up the Pane and call the existing `Pane::resize()` (already implemented for local Term). Return `IpcResponse::PaneResized` on success.

2. **PaneExited broadcast**: in `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs`, in `DaemonPtyHandler::on_eof()` (around line 113-172 per tn-kcug's audit), broadcast `DaemonEvent::PaneExited { session_id, pane_id, exit_code: Option<i32> }` via the existing `broadcast::Sender<DaemonEvent>`. Exit code source: check what `PtyPaneCore` / `portable_pty` exposes on EOF — if not directly available, pass `None` for now and file a follow-up.

3. Ensure the GUI's RemotePty subscription (in `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/remote_spawn.rs`) actually sees `PaneExited` and triggers the same teardown path local-mode uses on `on_eof`. May already work if it just listens for the event variant.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/server.rs (ResizePane handler)
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs (SessionManager::resize_pane, DaemonPtyHandler::on_eof)
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/remote_spawn.rs (verify PaneExited handling)
- crates/therminal-protocol/src/daemon.rs (existing ResizePane/PaneExited types — should not need changes)

## When Done
- Daemon resizes remote panes correctly (no more "unimplemented" error)
- Daemon broadcasts PaneExited on shell exit
- RemotePty panes auto-close cleanly when shell exits
- ./scripts/ci.sh green
- Commit
- Close: bd close tn-5rm0 --reason "daemon resize+exit handlers wired"
## Retro
- What worked: SessionManager already had find_pane_mut and Pane::resize; just needed a pane_id-keyed wrapper. DaemonPtyHandler already carried session_id+event_tx so PaneExited broadcast was a one-liner in on_eof.
- What was unclear: portable_pty doesn't surface child exit code through the reader thread on EOF, so exit_code is None for now (follow-up if needed).
close_reason: daemon resize+exit handlers wired

title: Bridge ClaudeStatePoller into SessionManager (per-pane capacity cache)
description:
Today `ClaudeStatePoller` (crates/therminal-daemon/src/claude_state.rs) watches /tmp/{claude,codex,copilot}-state/*.json and emits ClaudeStateUpdate via mpsc. The output flows ONLY into ClaudeJsonlRegistry for the therminal://claude/events resource. The capacity data (context_percent, model, status) never reaches SessionManager, so MCP tools cannot query agent context %.

This unblocks tn-oz25 (find_with_capacity) and the context_percent field in tn-eyjx (get_status). The TODO is documented at claude_state.rs:12-16.

Scope: add a per-pane capacity cache on SessionManager (or a sibling AgentCapacityRegistry), drain the existing mpsc::Receiver<ClaudeStateUpdate> in the daemon main loop, link session_id <-> pane_id (likely via the agent_registry's pid + the poller's working_dir or a registry-side lookup), and expose a snapshot accessor pane_capacity(pane_id) -> Option<{context_percent, model, status}>.

Out of scope: the new MCP tools themselves (those are tn-eyjx and tn-oz25). This issue ONLY plumbs the data; the tools that read it land in follow-ups.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

## Context
`ClaudeStatePoller` (crates/therminal-daemon/src/claude_state.rs) watches /tmp/{claude,codex,copilot}-state/*.json via the notify crate and emits `ClaudeStateUpdate` over an `mpsc::Receiver`. Today only `ClaudeJsonlRegistry` consumes those updates (for the therminal://claude/events resource). The capacity data (`context_percent`, `model`, `status`) never reaches `SessionManager`, so MCP tools cannot resolve "agent X is at 73% context" by pane_id.

This is the foundational plumbing for tn-eyjx and tn-oz25. The TODO is documented at claude_state.rs:12-16.

## Task
1. Add a per-pane capacity cache. Two options — pick the cleaner one:
   - **Option A**: New struct `PaneCapacityCache` (own file or inside session.rs) holding `HashMap<PaneId, PaneCapacityEntry { context_percent: Option<f32>, model: Option<String>, status: Option<String>, session_id: String, updated_at: u64 }>` behind `Arc<Mutex<...>>`. Owned by `SessionManager`.
   - **Option B**: Add the same fields directly onto `Pane` (session.rs:280-306), parallel to `cwd: Arc<Mutex<String>>`.
   Prefer **Option A** unless Pane already has 3+ similar Arc<Mutex> fields (it does — so honestly it could go either way; choose Option A for clean separation).
2. Drain `ClaudeStatePoller::updates()` in the daemon main loop. Where the ClaudePipeline is constructed (search for `ClaudePipeline::run` and `ClaudeStatePoller::new`), add a tokio task that on each `ClaudeStateUpdate::Upserted(state)`:
   - Resolves `state` → `pane_id`. Linkage strategy: the poller writes its state files keyed by claude `session_id` and may include `pid`. Cross-reference against `AgentRegistry` entries — `AgentEntry.pid: Option<u32>`. Match on pid first; fall back to most-recent-detected-agent-of-that-type if no pid. Document the fallback in code comments — it's a known limitation and acceptable for now.
   - Updates the cache.
   - On `ClaudeStateUpdate::Removed(session_id)`: best-effort remove the matching entry (find by session_id stored alongside the entry).
3. Expose accessor on `SessionManager`:
   ```rust
   pub fn pane_capacity(&self, pane_id: PaneId) -> Option<PaneCapacityEntry>
   ```
   (returns a clone — DTO is small).
4. Add a unit test that constructs a `PaneCapacityCache`, inserts an entry, asserts `pane_capacity` returns it. Don't test the poller wiring end-to-end (filesystem-dependent); just the cache + accessor.

## Constraints
- **Do NOT add new MCP tools** in this issue. That's tn-eyjx and tn-oz25.
- The daemon main loop is not allowed to block on the receiver — use tokio::spawn or convert the std::sync::mpsc into a tokio mpsc bridge if needed.
- `PaneCapacityEntry` must derive Clone + Debug + Serialize (Serialize so the next ticket can return it through MCP without re-defining).
- Keep `pane_id ↔ session_id` linkage logic in ONE function with a clear name (e.g. `resolve_pane_id_from_state`) so future improvements have one place to land.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/claude_state.rs (poller, mpsc receiver, ClaudeSessionState)
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs (SessionManager, Pane, list_agents)
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/lib.rs or daemon main runtime file (where ClaudePipeline is wired — grep for ClaudePipeline::run)
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/agent_registry.rs (AgentEntry.pid for linkage)

## Verify
./scripts/ci.sh

## When Done
mcp__beads__close(issue_id="tn-b45r")
## Retro
- What worked: Adding optional StateUpdateObserver callback to claude_pipeline kept the pipeline single-consumer of the std::sync::mpsc updates_rx while letting ensure.rs bridge into a tokio task. Cache lives on SessionManager as Arc so the bridge task doesn't fight the tokio mutex.
- What was unclear: ClaudeStateUpdate::Removed only carries a path, not session_id, so removal handling is best-effort no-op (entries get overwritten by upserts). Documented in code.
close_reason: Added PaneCapacityCache + bridge task in ensure.rs; pipeline observer callback wires ClaudeStateUpdates into per-pane cache via AgentRegistry resolution. CI clean.

title: Auto-spawn therminal-daemon from GUI startup (folds tn-6q3v into the attach epic)
description:
Once the GUI must talk to the daemon at startup (tn-5g41), the friction of "you have to launch therminal-daemon.exe first" becomes the #1 daily-driver papercut. Fold tn-6q3v in here.

Behavior:
1. In `connect_daemon()` (added in tn-5g41), if the connect attempt fails with the platform's "not found" error (ENOENT on Unix, file-not-found on Windows for the named pipe), spawn `therminal-daemon` (Unix) or `therminal-daemon.exe` (Windows) as a detached child process.
2. Resolve the daemon binary by: same directory as the current `therminal[.exe]` executable first, then `PATH` lookup, then a config-overridable path. Log which path was used.
3. Detach properly so that when the GUI exits, the daemon keeps running (so other clients / persisted sessions survive). Use `setsid` on Unix, `DETACHED_PROCESS | CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP` on Windows.
4. Poll-connect with a short retry loop (e.g. 10 × 100ms) before giving up — daemon startup is sub-second but not instant.
5. If spawn itself fails (binary missing, permissions), surface a clear actionable error.

Closes tn-6q3v.</description>
<acceptance>Fresh Windows boot: double-click `therminal.exe`, daemon spawns automatically, GUI window appears with a working pane. No manual `Start-Process therminal-daemon.exe`. Same on Linux from a clean shell. If the daemon binary is missing, the error message tells the user exactly which path was searched. tn-6q3v closed as superseded/done.</acceptance>
<deps>["tn-ytw2"]</deps>
</invoke>
notes:
2026-04-07: bumped P2→P1 per user request after Phase B smoke testing. Manual "launch daemon, then launch GUI" is the source of stale-process / wrong-attach class issues during daily-driver use on Windows. ensure_daemon() already exists in therminal-daemon — wiring it into GUI startup is the long-promised fix.</notes>
</invoke>
## Retro
- What worked: existing connect_daemon was a clean function so retry_connect could just take a closure pointing at it. Probe trait kept resolver unit-testable without touching FS. is_not_running_error walks the anyhow chain so it catches the io::Error wrapped by both connect_client and the surrounding context().
- What was unclear: whether to depend on the which crate vs hand-rolling PATH lookup — went with hand-rolled (no new dep). DaemonConfig.binary_path uses a custom deserializer so empty-string in TOML round-trips to None (matches how the rest of config_text writes commented-out empty strings).
close_reason: Implemented GUI auto-spawn of therminal-daemon: new therminal-app::daemon_spawn module with Probe-trait resolver (config -> next-to-exe -> PATH), detached spawn (setsid / DETACHED_PROCESS), 10x100ms retry_connect, NotFound/ConnectionRefused/ERROR_FILE_NOT_FOUND detection, and a [daemon] binary_path config override. Folds tn-6q3v. Commit 47f53a7.

title: Replace startup pane spawn with daemon attach (Phase A: attach + load)
description:
## Context
Codex reviewed epic tn-382v on 2026-04-07 and found the remote path is still "proof of life", not a real attach: `remote_spawn.rs:93` assumes `remote_pane_id == session_id`, `window/pane_ops.rs` has zero `SetWorkspaceState`/`SplitPane`/`KillPane` IPC calls (all ops still manipulate local LayoutNode trees), and the initial remote pane's `on_exit` callback can't target its local pane id yet because the mapping is implicit.

This ticket is being **rescoped to Phase A only**: attach to existing daemon sessions and load their workspace state into the GUI. The UI op cutover (Phase B) moves to a new sibling ticket, to be filed once Phase A lands.

## Prerequisite work (new dependency tickets)
- **Pane identity strategy** (to be filed): explicit daemon-pane-id ↔ local-pane-id mapping table, OR local-id = daemon-id in remote mode. Must land before this so the `on_exit` mapping gap (init.rs:376) can be fixed cleanly.
- **SetWorkspaceState sync loop** (to be filed): wire the existing `WorkspaceManager::workspace_info()` (currently `#[allow(dead_code)]` at workspace.rs:353) to publish layout snapshots to the daemon on every topology change. Independent of this ticket and can land first.

## Task (Phase A scope)
1. Drop `connect_daemon()` guard + tokio handle into startup as already done in tn-5g41.
2. On startup, call `IpcRequest::ListSessions` — if any sessions exist, proceed to attach; else create new session via daemon (existing `spawn_remote_pane` path, fixed with real pane discovery).
3. **Discover panes on attach**: for each existing session, call `IpcRequest::GetWorkspaces { session_id }` and read `WorkspaceInfo.pane_ids` + `WorkspaceInfo.layout: Option<LayoutSnapshot>`. The protocol ALREADY carries this (daemon.rs:373,379). No new IpcRequest variant needed — use the workspace state as the attach manifest.
4. For each pane in each workspace, build a local `RemotePty` backend subscribed to that pane's `PaneOutput` stream. Use the pane identity strategy from the prerequisite ticket.
5. Reconstruct the GUI's `WorkspaceManager` from the retrieved `Vec<WorkspaceInfo>`: one `Workspace` per entry, `LayoutNode` from `LayoutSnapshot`, focus from `focused_pane`.
6. If no sessions exist, create one via daemon and render it as today, but with the pane identity mapping wired correctly.
7. Keep `mcp.attach_mode` default at `Local` for this phase — do NOT flip the default yet. That happens in Phase B after UI ops are cut over.
8. Fix the `on_exit` gap in init.rs:376 using the new pane identity strategy — the callback should be able to close its own pane.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/init.rs (attach flow insertion)
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/remote_spawn.rs (drop `remote_pane_id = session_id` hack)
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/workspace.rs (reconstruct from `Vec<WorkspaceInfo>`)
- crates/therminal-protocol/src/daemon.rs (already has `GetWorkspaces` + `WorkspaceInfo.layout`)
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs (verify `GetWorkspaces` returns snapshots populated — daemon may currently store empty layouts)

## Risks (from Codex review + explorer audit)
- **Daemon workspace_state may be empty** — check `SessionManager::set_workspace_state()` callers. If nothing has ever written `LayoutSnapshot` to the daemon, this phase discovers nothing useful. That's why the `SetWorkspaceState` sync loop is a prerequisite.
- **Synchronous `block_on`** in remote_spawn.rs:74 freezes the winit event loop. Wrap daemon RPCs with `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), ...)` and fail loud.
- **MCP `terminal.workspaces.get_layout`** currently returns a degraded horizontal cascade (tools.rs:297, tracked as tn-vs0u). That's a separate consumer and doesn't block Phase A, but note it.

## When Done
- Restart daemon with a live session, restart GUI — existing panes render from daemon state, not local spawn
- `remote_pane_id == session_id` assumption removed; explicit mapping in place
- `on_exit` callback can close its own remote pane without the "redraw only" fallback
- CI green on Linux
- Manual smoke: kill shell in a remote pane, GUI closes the pane cleanly
- Close: `bd close tn-ytw2 --reason "Phase A attach works; Phase B cutover tracked separately"`
- Immediately file Phase B (UI ops cutover) as a follow-up
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
The cutover. After tn-5ps8, RemotePty works behind a flag but startup still spawns local PTYs. This ticket flips the default and makes the GUI daemon-first end-to-end. After this lands, MCP and the GUI finally see the same world.

## Task
1. On startup (after `connect_daemon()` from tn-5g41), call `sessions.list` and `panes.list`.
2. If sessions exist, render their panes as workspace tabs / split layout. The persisted-session restore the daemon already does becomes visible to the user.
3. If no sessions exist, create one fresh session + pane VIA THE DAEMON (not via local spawn) and render it.
4. Replace every local-PTY spawn call site identified in tn-kcug with a daemon IPC call. UI ops: split, close, focus, swap, rename, font size, etc.
5. Workspace tabs and layout state should be daemon-managed where the daemon already supports it (per top-level CLAUDE.md, the daemon already handles workspace tabs and pane swap).
6. Flip default `mcp.attach_mode` to `remote`. Keep `local` as escape hatch for one release.
7. Manual end-to-end test: launch GUI, run `terminal.panes.write` from Claude Code → bytes appear in visible pane. Run `terminal.panes.create` from MCP → new visible pane.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/main.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/spawn.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/workspace.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs

## When Done
MCP↔GUI roundtrip verified manually. Persisted sessions visible. Local-mode flag still works. CI green. Update root CLAUDE.md to reflect that the GUI is now a daemon client. Close: `bd close tn-ytw2 --reason "GUI is daemon client by default"`
## Retro (Phase A)
- PaneSnapshot replay: NOT implemented. Attached panes start with a blank local Term and rely on live PaneOutput events going forward; the daemon's PaneSnapshot machinery is left for a follow-up since wiring grid replay through the existing reader_loop interceptor pipeline is non-trivial and orthogonal to attach correctness.
- Empty workspace_state edge case: documented as fall-through to fresh-session spawn. If GetWorkspaces returns an empty Vec we log a warning and return Ok(false) so the existing spawn path runs (rather than bailing out with no GUI). This handles pre-tn-k3yo persisted sessions cleanly.
- Failure modes that fall back to fresh-session spawn: ListSessions timeout/error, GetWorkspaces timeout/error, malformed responses, empty workspace_state, WorkspaceManager::from_workspace_info returning None. All wrapped in 5s tokio timeouts; nothing panics; GUI always starts.
- Surprises: closure borrow checker — make_leaf needed an extra block scope so its mutable borrow of id_pairs released before the post-call read. Used a let-binding shadow trick with an inner block. Also: spawn_remote_pane now returns SessionId in addition to PaneId so the fresh-spawn callsite can populate self.daemon_session_id (previously left None — publish_workspace_state was a no-op for fresh remote sessions). Both fresh-spawn and attach paths now correctly seed daemon_session_id.
- Manual smoke: not run (no live daemon in this sandbox); deferred to Phase B sibling work. Acceptance covered by unit tests + CI.
close_reason: Phase A attach+load complete: GUI reconstructs workspace layout from existing daemon sessions on startup; Phase B (UI op cutover) remains tracked as tn-beez. CI green, unit test for from_workspace_info passes.

title: Add RemotePty PaneBackend variant: stream PTY bytes from daemon over IPC
description:
Introduce a new `PaneBackend::RemotePty` variant alongside the existing `Terminal` variant (do not delete `Terminal` yet — we keep the local-PTY mode behind a feature flag so we can compare). RemotePty owns a `DaemonClient` subscription handle to a remote pane and forwards bytes both directions over IPC.

Subtasks:
1. **Daemon side**: confirm `therminal-daemon`'s native IPC protocol (NOT MCP — the bd protocol from `framing.rs` / `server.rs`) exposes:
   - create pane / destroy pane
   - subscribe to PTY output (streaming bytes, not parsed)
   - send input bytes
   - resize (cols, rows)
   - cwd / title / exit-code notifications
   If any are missing, file a follow-up issue and stub them. Most should already exist for handoff.
2. **App side**: add `RemotePty { pane_id: u64, output_rx: ..., input_tx: ..., resize_tx: ... }` to PaneBackend.
3. Plumb the output stream into the alacritty terminal state machine the same way local PTY bytes go in today (per tn-kcug's audit). The renderer should not need to know whether bytes came from a local Child or a remote socket.
4. Plumb winit input events through the daemon-bound input channel.
5. Plumb winit resize events through the daemon-bound resize channel.
6. Handle remote-pane-exited cleanly: surface the exit code, mark the pane closed, follow the same teardown the local mode uses.

Gate behind a runtime config flag (`mcp.attach_mode = "local" | "remote"`) so we can flip back if something breaks.

Out of scope: replacing first-pane spawn at startup, restoring existing daemon sessions, MCP-driven new panes appearing in the GUI — those are subsequent tickets.</description>
<acceptance>With `attach_mode = "remote"` and a running daemon, manually creating one RemotePty pane (via a dev-only debug command or env var) renders a fully working bash session in the GUI. Keystrokes reach bash, output renders, resize works, exit cleans up. With `attach_mode = "local"` the existing path is byte-identical to today. CI green.</acceptance>
<deps>["tn-5g41"]</deps>
</invoke>
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
The core architectural change in epic tn-382v: introduce a `RemotePty` PaneBackend variant that streams PTY bytes from the daemon over IPC instead of owning a local portable_pty Child. Local mode stays available behind a feature/config flag for one release.

## Task
1. Confirm the daemon's native IPC (NOT MCP — bd protocol from server.rs/framing.rs) exposes: pane create/destroy, output stream subscribe, input write, resize, cwd/title/exit notifications. If anything is missing, file a discovered-from issue and stub it.
2. Add `RemotePty { pane_id: u64, output_rx, input_tx, resize_tx, ... }` to `PaneBackend` in `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/backend.rs`. Do NOT delete `Terminal` variant yet.
3. Plumb the output stream into the alacritty terminal state machine the same way local-PTY bytes flow today — see tn-kcug's audit for the exact seam.
4. Plumb winit input → daemon input channel. Plumb winit resize → daemon resize channel. Handle exit: surface exit code, mark closed, follow local-mode teardown.
5. Add a runtime config flag `mcp.attach_mode = "local" | "remote"` (default `local`). Gate the new variant behind it.
6. Manual smoke test: with `attach_mode = "remote"` and a running daemon, create one RemotePty pane via a dev/debug command and verify a fully working bash session renders. Type, scroll, resize, exit.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/backend.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/spawn.rs
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/server.rs
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/framing.rs
- crates/therminal-core/src/config.rs (new attach_mode field)

## When Done
Local mode byte-identical to today. Remote mode renders a working bash session. CI green. Close: `bd close tn-5ps8 --reason "RemotePty backend works behind flag"`
## Retro
- What worked: PtyPaneCore's reader_loop pattern translated cleanly to a worker thread reading from std::sync::mpsc fed by a tokio task subscribed to DaemonEvent::PaneOutput. The local Term + interceptor + region_index plumbing in the worker is byte-identical to AppPtyHandler::process_bytes — renderer is unaware bytes came from a socket.
- What was unclear: The daemon's IPC protocol had no ResizePane request and no PaneExited event. Stubbed both in therminal-protocol (server returns Error{unimplemented} for ResizePane; PaneExited variant wired through but never broadcast). Filed tn-5rm0 (discovered-from) for the real handlers.
- Per-pane subscription: each RemotePty pane opens its own DaemonClient connection to subscribe to PaneOutput so multiple panes don't contend on the shared event receiver. Suboptimal but unblocks the seam; a multiplexed broadcaster on the primary client is a future cleanup.
- Pane-id mapping: today the GUI assumes the daemon's first pane id == session id (true in current daemon code). A real attach handshake lands in tn-ytw2.
close_reason: RemotePty backend works behind flag

title: Add therminal-daemon client dependency to therminal-app and define attach mode
description:
Wire `therminal-app` to depend on `therminal-daemon` (or a thinner `therminal-daemon-client` crate if we choose to split — decide here). Add a startup mode where the GUI connects to a running daemon over IPC and refuses to start (with a clear error) if no daemon is reachable.

Tasks:
1. Add `therminal-daemon = { path = "../therminal-daemon" }` to `crates/therminal-app/Cargo.toml`. If the daemon's lib pulls in too much (server, persistence, etc.), instead extract `client.rs` + `framing.rs` + `ipc_transport.rs` into a new `therminal-daemon-client` crate and depend on that. Note: tn-kcug should clarify which is cleaner.
2. In `crates/therminal-app/src/main.rs`, add a `connect_daemon()` step before window creation that uses `DaemonClient` to open the daemon socket / named pipe.
3. On connect failure: log a clear error pointing to `therminal-daemon.exe` (Windows) or the runtime socket path (Unix) and exit non-zero. We'll fold auto-spawn into a later ticket — for now, fail loud.
4. On connect success: log daemon version + protocol_version + socket path, store the client handle in app state, and proceed.

Out of scope: actually using the client to manage panes — that's the next ticket. This issue just establishes the connection.

CI must pass: `./scripts/ci.sh` on Linux, plus `./scripts/build-windows.sh` smoke (close therminal.exe first).</description>
<acceptance>therminal-app depends on therminal-daemon (or extracted client crate). GUI startup connects to daemon over IPC, logs success or fails loud with a clear error. CI green on Linux. Windows build deploys cleanly. Existing local-PTY rendering still works (we haven't touched that path yet).</acceptance>
<deps>["tn-kcug"]</deps>
</invoke>
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
First wiring step toward making the GUI a daemon client (epic tn-382v). Today therminal-app has zero references to therminal-daemon outside the mcp_stdio bridge. We need the GUI to open a daemon client connection at startup. No pane behavior changes yet — just establish the connection.

## Task
1. Add `therminal-daemon` (or an extracted thin client crate, your call based on tn-kcug's audit) as a dependency in `crates/therminal-app/Cargo.toml`. If pulling the full daemon lib brings in too much (server, persistence, mcp), extract `client.rs` + `framing.rs` + `ipc_transport.rs` into a new `therminal-daemon-client` crate first.
2. In `crates/therminal-app/src/main.rs`, before window creation, call `connect_daemon()` which uses `DaemonClient` to open the daemon socket / named pipe. On success, log version + protocol_version + path and store the client in app state.
3. On connect failure: log a clear error pointing at `therminal-daemon.exe` (Windows) or the runtime socket path (Unix) and exit non-zero. Auto-spawn comes later (tn-txs8).
4. Existing local-PTY rendering must still work — do NOT touch PaneBackend yet.

Read tn-kcug's design field first for the seam map.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/Cargo.toml
- crates/therminal-app/src/main.rs

## When Done
./scripts/ci.sh green on Linux. Manually verify GUI starts (with daemon running) and logs connection. Stop the daemon, verify GUI fails loud with the new error. Close: `bd close tn-5g41 --reason "GUI connects to daemon at startup"`
## Retro
- What worked: Extracting therminal-daemon-client cleanly via git mv + re-export from therminal-daemon::lib (pub use) kept all internal call sites (crate::framing, crate::client, crate::ipc_transport) compiling without edits. App.daemon_client field threaded through window::run() avoided thread-local hacks.
- What was unclear: Whether to spin up tokio in main() for a sync winit loop — settled on a leaked single-worker runtime so DaemonClient's connection task has somewhere to live. tn-txs8 (auto-spawn) will need to revisit this.

Local PTY rendering untouched. CI green.

title: Audit current PaneBackend lifecycle and document GUI↔PTY seams
description:
Research-only. Map every place the GUI currently touches a local PTY so we know what to redirect through the daemon. Output is a markdown design note (or beads design field on tn-382v) listing each seam.

Files to read:
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/backend.rs` — the PaneBackend enum (Terminal | WebView)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/spawn.rs` — local PTY spawn
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/workspace.rs` — pane lifecycle within a window
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs` — UI-driven pane ops (split, close, focus)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/main.rs` — startup, where the first pane is created
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/pty/` — `PtyPaneCore` and shared PTY lifecycle

For each seam, document:
1. **Owner**: which struct holds the PTY handle / Child process
2. **Read path**: how bytes from PTY reach the alacritty terminal state and the GPU renderer
3. **Write path**: how winit keyboard/mouse events become PTY input
4. **Resize**: how window-resize events propagate to PTY rows/cols
5. **Lifecycle**: spawn, exit, cleanup
6. **Shared state**: anything cross-pane (clipboard, focus, agent registry, OSC 7 cwd updates, OSC 133 marks → semantic regions)

Also document the daemon-side equivalents in `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs` and `client.rs` so the next ticket has a clear translation table.

No code changes — this issue is pure research.</description>
<acceptance>Design note attached to tn-382v listing all PaneBackend seams with their daemon-side equivalents. Sufficient context that the next ticket can be implemented without re-reading these files.</acceptance>
<deps>["tn-382v"]</deps>
</invoke>
design:
## PTY Seam Map & Translation Table

### 1. Owner (PTY Handle Holder)

**GUI**: `PaneBackendKind::Terminal` in `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/backend.rs:40-47`:
- `term: Arc<FairMutex<Term<PaneListener>>>`
- `pty_writer: Box<dyn IoWrite + Send>`
- `pty_master: Box<dyn MasterPty + Send>`

**Daemon**: `Pane` in `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs:280-306`:
- `term: Arc<FairMutex<Term<HeadlessListener>>>`
- `pty_writer: Box<dyn IoWrite + Send>`
- `_pty_master: Box<dyn MasterPty + Send>` (Unix: wrapped in `FdPtyMaster`)

### 2. Read Path (PTY → Term → Renderer)

**GUI**: `therminal-terminal/src/pty_runtime.rs::reader_loop()` spawns reader thread that calls `handler.process_bytes()` (4096-byte chunks). Handler `AppPtyHandler` (`pane/spawn.rs:51-72`) calls `processor.advance_with_interceptor()`, updates `RegionIndex` from OSC 7/633/133/9, updates `PaneStatus` (cwd, last_exit_code, agent_name), wakes event loop. Renderer (`window/render.rs::render_panes_recursive()`) reads `term.lock().grid()` per frame for GPU text via glyphon.

**Daemon**: Same `reader_loop()`. Handler `DaemonPtyHandler` (`session.rs:113-172`) updates `RegionIndex`, feeds bytes to `AgentStateInference`, broadcasts `DaemonEvent::PaneOutput { session_id, pane_id, data }`. MCP `terminal.panes.get_content` reads grid under lock; on attach `PaneSnapshot` snapshots grid+scrollback+cursor.

### 3. Write Path (Winit → PTY)

**GUI**: Winit `KeyboardInput` → `window/keybindings.rs::handle_key_event()` → `therminal-terminal/src/input.rs::encode_key()` → `window/pane_ops.rs::pty_write_to_pane()` → `PaneBackendKind::write_input()` → `pty_writer.write_all()`.

**Daemon**: IPC `SessionCommand` (TBA) → `SessionManager::write_to_pane()` → `Pane::write()` → `pty_writer.write_all()`. Trust tier: Writer (Supervised/Trusted).

### 4. Resize Propagation

**GUI**: Winit `Resized` or separator drag → `pane/state.rs::grid_size_for_rect_with_header()` → `window/pane_ops.rs::resize_all_panes()` → `PaneBackendKind::resize(cols, rows)` (calls `term.lock().resize()` + `therminal_terminal::pty::resize()`).

**Daemon**: IPC `ResizePane { session_id, pane_id, cols, rows }` (TBA) → `SessionManager::resize_pane()` → `Pane::resize()`. No automatic snapshot push to clients.

### 5. Lifecycle (Spawn / Exit / Cleanup)

**GUI Spawn**: `window/pane_ops.rs::split_focused_pane()` → `pane/spawn.rs::spawn_pane()` allocates `PaneId`, builds `AppPtyHandler` w/ callbacks (wake/on_exit/on_bell/on_notification), `PtyPaneCore::spawn()` creates `Term<PaneListener>` + `pty::spawn_shell_with_options()` + reader thread, wraps as `PaneBackendKind::Terminal`, inserts into layout tree.

**GUI Exit**: Reader EOF → `handler.on_eof()` → `UserEvent::PaneExited` → `close_pane_any()` extracts `PaneState`, drops backend (closes FDs, reader exits, term freed), rebalances layout. `AppPtyHandler::on_eof()` also calls `AgentRegistry::unregister()`.

**Daemon Spawn**: IPC `CreateSession`/`CreatePane` → `SessionManager::create_session()` → `Pane::spawn()` allocates PaneId (independent counter), builds `DaemonPtyHandler` w/ broadcast tx, `PtyPaneCore::spawn()` w/ `HeadlessListener`, reader broadcasts `PaneOutput` per chunk, inserted into `sessions[id].windows[0].panes[id]`.

**Daemon Exit**: EOF or `DestroyPane` → `SessionManager::destroy_pane()` removes Pane, drops fds, reader exits, broadcasts `PaneDestroyed`. Last pane in session may trigger session destruction / idle exit.

### 6. Cross-Pane Shared State

**GUI**:
| State | Location | Sync |
|---|---|---|
| Clipboard | `clipboard.rs` (arboard) | Single-threaded event loop |
| Focus | `App::focused_pane: Option<PaneId>` | Event loop |
| Agent Registry | `App::agent_registry: Arc<Mutex<AgentRegistry>>` | Mutex (reader thread + loop contend) |
| OSC 7 cwd | `PaneStatus::cwd` in `Arc<Mutex<PaneStatus>>` | Mutex (reader thread writes) |
| OSC 633 marks | `Arc<Mutex<RegionIndex>>` | Mutex (reader thread writes) |
| Visual bell | `PaneListener::bell_pending: Arc<AtomicBool>` | Atomic swap-after-chunk |

**Daemon**:
| State | Location | Sync |
|---|---|---|
| OSC 7 cwd | `Pane::cwd: Arc<Mutex<String>>` | Mutex |
| OSC 633 marks | `Pane::command_tracker: Arc<Mutex<CommandTracker>>` | Mutex |
| Agent state | `Pane::inference: Arc<Mutex<AgentStateInference>>` | Mutex |
| Event log | `Pane::event_log: Arc<Mutex<EventLog>>` | Mutex (cap 5000) |
| Clipboard / Focus / cross-pane Agent Registry | N/A (headless) | — |

### Translation Table

| GUI Concept | GUI Location | Daemon Equivalent | Daemon Location | Protocol |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| PTY owner | `PaneBackendKind::Terminal` | `Pane` struct | `session.rs:280-306` | Session→Window→Pane hierarchy |
| Term lifecycle | `PtyPaneCore::spawn()` | `Pane::spawn()` / `from_raw_fd()` | `session.rs:309-472` | IPC `CreateSession`/`CreatePane` |
| Reader thread | `PtyReaderHandler` + `AppPtyHandler` | `PtyReaderHandler` + `DaemonPtyHandler` | `pty_runtime.rs` + `session.rs:113-172` | Same `reader_loop()`, different handler |
| Loop wake | `PaneCallbacks::wake` | `broadcast::Sender<DaemonEvent>` | `session.rs:114` | MCP resource subscription → `notifications/resources/updated` |
| PTY write | `PaneBackendKind::write_input()` | `SessionManager::write_to_pane()` | TBA | IPC `SessionCommand` |
| Resize | `PaneBackendKind::resize()` | `Pane::resize()` via `SessionManager::resize_pane()` | TBA | IPC `ResizePane{cols,rows}` |
| Grid read | `term.lock().grid()` | Same, in `PaneSnapshot` | `session.rs:66-80` | MCP `terminal.panes.get_content` |
| Scrollback | `term.lock().grid_history()` | Same | `session.rs:66-80` | resource `terminal://pane/{id}/scrollback` |
| RegionIndex | `Arc<Mutex<RegionIndex>>` on PaneState | Same on Pane | `session.rs:286` | `terminal.semantic.query_history` |
| Cwd | `PaneStatus::cwd` | `Pane::cwd: Arc<Mutex<String>>` | `session.rs:290` | `terminal.panes.list` |
| OSC 633 | `CommandTracker` via interceptor | `CommandTracker` via interceptor | `session.rs:300` | `terminal.semantic.query_commands` |
| Agent state | `AgentRegistry` + `ProcessDetector` | `AgentStateInference` | `session.rs:296` | `terminal.agents.get_details` |
| Live state | Live `Term` per frame | `PaneSnapshot` on attach | `session.rs:64-88` | IPC `SessionSnapshot` |

### Dependency Recommendation: EXTRACT `therminal-daemon-client` crate

Full daemon lib pulls in `rmcp` (entire MCP server stack), `notify` (file watcher), `schemars` (schema gen) — none needed by clients. Lightweight client subset is just `client.rs` + `framing.rs` + `ipc_transport.rs` + protocol types, deps reduced to `rmp-serde`, `anyhow`, `tracing`, `tokio::io`.

Proposed structure:
```
crates/therminal-daemon-client/
  src/{lib.rs, client.rs, framing.rs, ipc_transport.rs}
  Cargo.toml: tokio, anyhow, tracing, therminal-protocol
crates/therminal-daemon/
  Cargo.toml: + therminal-daemon-client
  src/lib.rs: pub use daemon_client::*
  (server.rs, session.rs, mcp/, claude/, etc. unchanged)
```

Allows `therminal-app` to import only the client; tools/headless consumers reuse it; server-side internals stay isolated.

### Critical Seams for tn-382v Implementation

**Remove from GUI**:
- `PtyPaneCore::spawn()` call in `pane/spawn.rs` → delegate via IPC
- `PaneBackendKind::Terminal { pty_writer, pty_master }` → IPC sender
- PTY reader thread + `AppPtyHandler` → subscribe to `DaemonEvent::PaneOutput`
- Local `AgentRegistry` population → subscribe to daemon-side agent events

**Add to IPC protocol** (`therminal-protocol/src/daemon.rs`):
- `CreateSession { shell, cwd, env }` → `SessionId`
- `CreatePane { session_id, split_direction, cols, rows }` → `PaneId`
- `WritePane { session_id, pane_id, data }`
- `ResizePane { session_id, pane_id, cols, rows }`
- `DestroyPane { session_id, pane_id }`
- Events: `PaneOutput`, `PaneExited`, `PaneResized`

**Reuse**: `SessionManager::write_to_pane()`, `Pane::resize()`, `DaemonEvent::PaneOutput` broadcast, `PaneSnapshot` on attach. RegionIndex/CommandTracker/AgentStateInference move to daemon (shared by all clients). All machinery already in `session.rs`; work is primarily rewiring GUI to use IPC.

title: EPIC: Make GUI a daemon client — render daemon-managed panes instead of local PTYs
description:
Today the therminal GUI (`therminal.exe` / `therminal-app`) and the daemon (`therminal-daemon.exe`) are completely independent. The GUI spawns its own portable-pty children locally and renders them with alacritty_terminal in-process. The daemon owns its own session manager, persists sessions to disk, and exposes panes over IPC + MCP. They never meet. As a result, MCP tools operate on invisible "headless" panes while the user sees a parallel set of GUI-local panes — confirmed end-to-end during tn-q882 verification on 2026-04-07: `terminal.panes.write` against pane 1 worked perfectly per `terminal.panes.get_content`, but nothing appeared in the GUI window.

This was supposed to be the architecture from day one (per top-level CLAUDE.md: "persistent multiplexed sessions via daemon, zero-downtime handoff with SCM_RIGHTS FD passing"). The daemon-side machinery is built — `client.rs`, `handoff.rs`, `fd_passing.rs`, `ipc_transport.rs`, `ensure.rs`, session manager, FD passing — but `therminal-app/Cargo.toml` doesn't even depend on `therminal-daemon`. The wiring from GUI startup → daemon attach was simply never built.

This epic tracks the work to fix that gap: the GUI becomes a daemon client. Every pane it renders is a daemon-managed pane, retrieved over IPC. MCP and GUI then see the same world.

Once this lands:
- MCP `panes.create` shows up in your window
- `panes.write` injects into the pane you can see
- tn-6q3v (auto-spawn daemon) becomes a 5-line addition to GUI startup
- tn-q882 issues 2/3 (mcp_stdio half-close) become routine polish, since the GUI is the canonical client and the bridge is no longer the only way in

Strategy: start with the streamed-bytes model (Option A) — GUI subscribes to PTY output over IPC and forwards input over IPC. It's simpler, cross-platform, and unblocks everything. FD/handle passing (Option B) is a later optimization that uses the existing fd_passing.rs machinery for zero-overhead reads.

Related: tn-q882 (MCP schema bug, fixed; issues 2/3 deferred behind this epic), tn-6q3v (auto-spawn daemon — folds into this epic), tn-8trl (docs — already fixed).</description>
</invoke>
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
This epic is 84% complete (11/13 children done). Two remaining: tn-l3hk (latency baseline measurement) and tn-382v.3 (final gate). The GUI is already a daemon client by default.

## Task
This is a coordination task. The epic is nearly done.

1. Execute tn-l3hk: measure remote-attach latency by adding timing instrumentation around `spawn_remote_pane()` and the IPC round-trip in both local and remote modes. Compare. Document whether FD/handle passing is needed now or can be deferred.
2. After tn-l3hk, run the tn-382v.3 gate: full `./scripts/ci.sh` + manual verification that MCP panes.create/write are visible in the GUI.
3. Close the epic.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/remote_spawn.rs (timing instrumentation point)
- crates/therminal-daemon-client/src/client.rs (IPC round-trip timing)

## When Done
Close tn-l3hk with latency numbers and recommendation.
Close tn-382v.3 gate after CI passes.
Close tn-382v epic.
close_reason: Epic complete. GUI is a daemon client by default (attach_mode=remote). All 13 children closed: IPC layer, session manager, remote pane streaming, snapshot replay (tn-zamd), auto-spawn daemon (tn-txs8), zero-downtime handoff, shell integration validation (tn-sojs), latency baseline (tn-l3hk). IPC overhead measured at 64 µs median / 107 µs p99 — streamed-bytes model acceptable, FD passing deferred. MCP panes.create/write are now visible in the GUI window. CI clean.

title: Auto-spawn daemon from therminal.exe on startup
description:
After the Windows daemon port (tn-theo), launching MCP on Windows requires manually starting therminal-daemon.exe before therminal.exe. Make therminal.exe spawn the daemon automatically.

Two options:
A) Add therminal-daemon as a dependency of therminal-app, call ensure_daemon() from window::run() startup. Pros: single binary launch, shared process. Cons: pulls rmcp + daemon dep tree into the GUI binary (~9MB extra).
B) therminal.exe spawns therminal-daemon.exe as a child process at startup if no daemon pipe is reachable. Pros: keeps GUI binary lean, daemon stays a separate process (matches Unix behavior where ensure_daemon already forks). Cons: extra binary to ship + relies on sibling discovery.

Recommend Option B — matches the Unix model and keeps the GUI lean. The build script (scripts/build-windows.ps1) already deploys therminal-daemon.exe alongside therminal.exe.

Implementation:
- In therminal-app/src/main.rs or window::run() startup, before the event loop:
  - On Windows, check if connecting to \\.\pipe\therminal-daemon succeeds.
  - If not, locate sibling therminal-daemon.exe (std::env::current_exe().parent().join('therminal-daemon.exe')) and Command::new it with detached/no-window flags.
  - Wait briefly for the pipe to come up (poll for ~2s).
- On Unix this is unnecessary — ensure_daemon already self-starts.
close_reason: Superseded by tn-txs8 commit 47f53a7 (auto-spawn implemented as part of the same module).

title: MCP tool: get_agent_status(agent_id) — expose ClaudeStatePoller
description:
Therminal already watches /tmp/claude-code-state, /tmp/codex-state, etc. via crates/therminal-terminal/src/claude_state.rs (notify crate), but the data isn't exposed over MCP. Add get_agent_status(agent_id) tool in crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs that returns the poller's snapshot (mode: Idle/Processing/ToolUse/AwaitingInput).

Why: lets sibling agents coordinate via MCP — e.g. 'wait until agent X finishes ToolUse before I spawn Y'. Conductor-mcp does this via tmux + state files at server.py:24-26; we already have the better half (notify-based watcher), just need to surface it. Hook the poller into the daemon's main event loop if the TODO at claude_state.rs:12-13 is still open.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

## Context
Add MCP tool `terminal.agents.get_status(pane_id)` returning a single agent's mode + capacity snapshot. NOTE: the original issue title says `get_status(agent_id)` but `AgentRegistry` keys exclusively by `pane_id` — there is no `agent_id` concept. Use `pane_id` (matches `terminal.agents.get_details`).

Sibling agents will use this to coordinate: "wait until pane 3's agent finishes ToolUse before I spawn another." It's a strict subset of `terminal.agents.get_details` focused on dynamic state.

Depends on tn-b45r (PaneCapacityCache plumbing).

## Task
1. Define types in `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs`:
   ```rust
   struct AgentStatusParam { pane_id: u64 }   // OR reuse PaneIdParam if already defined
   struct AgentStatusResult {
       pane_id: u64,
       agent_type: Option<String>,    // "claude" | "codex" | ...
       status: String,                 // "idle" | "processing" | "tool_use" | "awaiting_input" | ...
       current_tool: Option<String>,   // for ToolUse { tool_name }
       context_percent: Option<f32>,   // from PaneCapacityCache
       model: Option<String>,          // from PaneCapacityCache
   }
   ```
2. Add `handle_get_agent_status(params)` in `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs`:
   - Lock SessionManager
   - Look up `agent_registry().get(pane_id)` for `agent_type`, `status`, `current_tool`
   - Look up `pane_capacity(pane_id)` for `context_percent` + `model`
   - Return `AgentStatusResult`. If neither lookup yields anything, return an error: "no agent for pane_id {n}".
3. Register in `tool_definitions()`, dispatch in `mod.rs::call_tool()`, mark Observer-tier in `trust.rs::tool_category()`.
4. Tests in `mcp/mod.rs` test module:
   - Tool exists in `tool_definitions()`
   - Trust-gated: a Sandboxed-tier caller is permitted; nothing lower (regression-lock the bypass class)
   - Returns error for an unknown pane_id

## Constraints
- Reuse `PaneIdParam` if it already exists — don't duplicate.
- The agent_status string should match the existing `terminal.agents.list` formatting (look at how `AgentInfoResult.status` is serialized — keep it identical).
- No mutation, no side effects.
- Update the tool table in `crates/therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md`.

./scripts/ci.sh

- What was unclear: three separate trust-tool lists in mcp/mod.rs tests had to be updated in lockstep with the count assertion (16/19/20). Easy to miss.

title: Expose agent context % via MCP (port get_workers_with_capacity)
description:
Port conductor-mcp's get_workers_with_capacity concept (server.py:779-832). Cluster agents by remaining context capacity so spawn decisions (incl. AutoTileDebouncer) can reuse idle panes instead of always spawning new ones.

Implementation: new MCP tool find_agents_with_capacity(threshold) in crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs that queries AgentRegistry + ClaudeStatePoller snapshot. Works for claude/codex/gemini state dirs. Wire AutoTileDebouncer::poll() so it can optionally consult capacity before spawning a fresh pane.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

## Context
Add MCP tool `terminal.agents.find_with_capacity(threshold)` returning the list of agents that have at least `threshold` percent of context remaining. Lets coordinators reuse idle/under-utilized panes instead of always spawning fresh ones.

Note: the original issue mentioned wiring AutoTileDebouncer to consult capacity. That part is **out of scope** for this ticket because AutoTileDebouncer is app-side (crates/therminal-app/src/pane/auto_tile.rs), not daemon-side, and would require an app-side MCP round-trip. File a separate follow-up if/when needed; this issue is daemon-only.

Depends on tn-eyjx (file ordering — both edit mcp/tools.rs, mcp/mod.rs, trust.rs, CLAUDE.md).

## Task
1. Define types in `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs`:
   ```rust
   struct FindWithCapacityParam { threshold_percent: f32 }  // threshold of REMAINING capacity, e.g. 30.0 = "agents with >=30% headroom"
   struct AgentCapacityInfo {
       pane_id: u64,
       agent_type: Option<String>,
       status: String,
       context_percent: Option<f32>,    // currently used (from PaneCapacityCache)
       remaining_percent: Option<f32>,   // 100.0 - context_percent
       model: Option<String>,
   }
   struct FindWithCapacityResult { agents: Vec<AgentCapacityInfo> }
   ```
2. Add `handle_find_with_capacity(params)` in `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs`:
   - Lock SessionManager
   - Iterate `list_agents()`
   - For each entry: look up `pane_capacity(entry.pane_id)`. Compute `remaining_percent = 100.0 - context_percent` (None if no capacity data).
   - Filter: include if `remaining_percent.is_none() || remaining_percent.unwrap() >= threshold_percent`. (Treat unknown capacity as "include" so callers can still fall back to those panes — document this in the tool description.)
   - Sort descending by remaining_percent (None last).
   - Return `FindWithCapacityResult`.
3. Register in `tool_definitions()`, dispatch in `mod.rs::call_tool()`, mark Observer-tier in `trust.rs::tool_category()`.
4. Tests in `mcp/mod.rs` test module:
   - Tool exists
   - Trust-gated correctly (same regression test as tn-eyjx)
   - Threshold filtering: build a fake SessionManager with three panes (one at 20% used, one at 80% used, one with no capacity data). Threshold=50 → returns the 20%-used pane and the unknown-capacity pane, NOT the 80%-used one.
   - Sort order verified

## Constraints
- Threshold semantics: REMAINING percent, not used percent. Document explicitly in the tool description string.
- Unknown capacity treated as "include." Document.
- No mutation.
- No AutoTileDebouncer changes — out of scope.
- Update the tool table in `crates/therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md`.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs (AgentInfoResult/AgentStatusResult patterns)
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/trust.rs
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs (list_agents + pane_capacity)
- crates/therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md

## Verify
./scripts/ci.sh

## When Done
mcp__beads__close(issue_id=\"tn-oz25\")
## Retro
- What worked: handle_get_agent_status from tn-eyjx was a clean template; PaneCapacityCache.upsert + register_agent in test setup is straightforward.
- Watch out: sort_by closure must compare a vs b directly (not b vs a) when also branching on Some/None — easy off-by-one to put None first.
- Watch out: rmcp CallToolResult.content access in tests — use .content.first().and_then(|c| c.as_text()) to dodge the AsRef inference error.

title: Wire or remove unwired config fields: osc9_enabled and AgentTrust.allowed_tools
description:
CLAUDE.md mandates "Config fields must be wired: if a config struct has a field, code must read it." Code review 2026-04-06 found two unwired fields in crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs:

1. NotificationConfig.osc9_enabled (~line 627) — declared, documented, in default config text, but never read. OSC 9 desktop notifications are gated only by TerminalConfig.osc_9 (the parser toggle). Setting `notifications.osc9_enabled = false` has no effect.

2. AgentTrust.allowed_tools (~line 538) — Option<Vec<String>>, serialized, shown as example in config_text.rs, but never enforced. trust.rs only checks tier; the per-agent tool allowlist is silently ignored. **This is a security-relevant footgun** — users may believe they've restricted an agent and they have not.

For each: either wire it (preferred for allowed_tools — enforce in TrustEnforcer::enforce_trust by checking tool name against allowed_tools when Some) or delete the field entirely (and remove from config_text.rs).

TODOs were left in the code by the review pass marking both sites.

Priority P1 because allowed_tools is a security misconception, not just dead config.
notes:
## Retro
- Wired osc9_enabled via new NotificationSource tag on UserEvent::DesktopNotification; gated OSC 9 branch in event handler so hot-reload stays correct.
- Wired AgentTrust.allowed_tools in check_tool_access after tier check; None=no restriction, Some=exact match required.
- Added 3 unit tests: allowlist_none_imposes_no_restriction, allowlist_empty_denies_all, allowlist_specific_tool_allows_listed_denies_others.
- Removed both TODO markers; replaced with doc comments pointing to enforcement sites.
- clippy + test --workspace clean.
close_reason: Wired osc9_enabled (gated in window event handler via NotificationSource tag) and AgentTrust.allowed_tools (enforced in check_tool_access after tier check); added 3 allowlist unit tests (None/empty/specific).

title: Preserve pane cwd and shell during daemon session persistence and restore
description:
Code review finding: persisted session snapshotting writes empty `cwd` and `shell` for panes, but restore uses those fields as real spawn inputs. That means restored sessions can come back in the wrong directory or shell after daemon restart.

Impact:
- Session restore loses execution context.
- Behavioral regression on daemon restart / ensure startup path.

Likely files:
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/persistence.rs
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/ensure.rs
- crates/therminal-protocol/src/daemon.rs
acceptance_criteria:
- Persisted pane metadata records the effective cwd and shell used for restore.
- Restored sessions reopen panes with the expected cwd and shell.
- Add an integration test covering persist -> restart/restore -> verify pane context.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: The pane model cleanly accepted cwd/shell fields, and the DaemonPtyHandler already had access to InterceptedEvent::CurrentDirectory -- just needed to wire it through via Arc<Mutex<String>>.
- What was unclear: restore_from_persisted returns pane count not session count -- caused a minor test assertion fix.
close_reason: Session persistence preserves pane cwd and shell

title: Fix zsh login-shell overlay recursion in generated .zprofile
description:
Code review finding: generated zsh overlay `.zprofile` sources `${ZDOTDIR:-$HOME}/.zprofile` while `ZDOTDIR` still points at the overlay directory. For login zsh, `.zprofile` runs before `.zshrc`, so the generated file can source itself recursively.

Impact:
- Login shells using zsh can loop or misbehave before the integration restores the user's original `ZDOTDIR`.
- Existing tests only validate file creation/content, not actual login-shell execution semantics.

Likely file:
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/pty.rs
acceptance_criteria:
- Generated zsh overlay files source the user's real login-shell config without self-recursion.
- Login zsh startup order is handled correctly for `.zshenv`, `.zprofile`, `.zshrc`, and `.zlogin`.
- Add a regression test that would fail on self-sourcing or otherwise validates the intended source paths for login shells.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
The generated zsh `.zprofile` (pty.rs:203-207) sources `${ZDOTDIR:-$HOME}/.zprofile`, but ZDOTDIR still points to the overlay directory at that point. ZDOTDIR is only restored later in `.zshrc` (lines 184-190). For login shells, zsh sources `.zprofile` before `.zshrc`, so the generated `.zprofile` sources itself recursively.

## Task
1. In `pty.rs` at line 203, fix the `.zprofile` generation to use `_THERMINAL_ORIG_ZDOTDIR` (the saved original) instead of `ZDOTDIR`, matching the pattern already used in `.zshenv` (line 166-168)
2. Apply the same fix to `.zlogin` (lines 213-217) which has the identical bug
3. The fix for both files should be: `if [ -f "${_therminal_real_zdotdir}/.zprofile" ]; then . "${_therminal_real_zdotdir}/.zprofile"; fi` — where `_therminal_real_zdotdir` is set from `_THERMINAL_ORIG_ZDOTDIR` the same way `.zshenv` does it
4. Update the existing test `prepare_zsh_zdotdir_creates_config_files` to verify `.zprofile` and `.zlogin` reference `_THERMINAL_ORIG_ZDOTDIR` rather than `ZDOTDIR`

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/pty.rs (lines 148-220, zsh overlay generation)

## When Done
Close issue: `bd close tn-ihp0 --reason "Fixed zsh .zprofile/.zlogin to use saved ZDOTDIR"`
## Retro
- What worked: Clear bug description with exact line numbers made the fix straightforward. The existing pattern in .zshenv provided the template.
- What was unclear: Nothing, the task was well-scoped.
close_reason: Fixed zsh .zprofile/.zlogin to use saved ZDOTDIR

title: Fix clipboard initialization panic in headless or unavailable clipboard environments
description:
Code review finding: therminal-app clipboard access is documented and used as best-effort, but global initialization still panics when no clipboard backend is reachable. Verified by `cargo test --workspace --quiet`, which fails in `clipboard::tests::parse_osc52_recognised` with an X11 timeout panic from `crates/therminal-app/src/clipboard.rs`.

Impact:
- App can crash during OSC52 handling, copy, or paste in headless/remote/broken desktop environments.
- Test suite is currently red in such environments.

Likely files:
- crates/therminal-app/src/clipboard.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mouse.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs
acceptance_criteria:
- Clipboard initialization never panics when the system clipboard is unavailable.
- Copy/paste/OSC52 paths degrade gracefully and log warnings instead of crashing.
- `clipboard::tests::parse_osc52_recognised` passes in headless CI-like environments or is rewritten to avoid real clipboard dependency.
- `cargo test --workspace --quiet` passes for this area.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Simple type change from OnceLock<Mutex<Clipboard>> to OnceLock<Option<Mutex<Clipboard>>> cleanly resolved the panic path while preserving the retry logic.
- What was unclear: Nothing -- the issue description was precise and actionable.
close_reason: Clipboard gracefully degrades in headless environments

title: Fix: Ctrl+Shift+W exits app when other workspaces have panes; shell exit doesn't close pane in non-active workspace
description:
Two related bugs in pane close logic:

1. **Ctrl+Shift+W closes entire terminal when other tabs exist**: `close_focused_pane()` and `close_pane_by_id()` in pane_ops.rs set `self.workspaces = None` when the last pane in the *current workspace* is removed — without checking if other workspaces still have panes. Should remove the empty workspace and switch to another one instead.

2. **Typing "exit" doesn't close the pane**: `PaneExited` event calls `close_pane_by_id()` which uses `get_layout_mut()` — this only searches the *active* workspace's layout. If the exiting pane is in a non-active workspace (or the pane lookup fails for any reason), it silently does nothing.

Root cause for both: pane close operations only operate on the active workspace and have no cross-workspace awareness.
close_reason: Fixed: pane close now searches all workspaces via remove_pane_any(), gc_empty_workspaces() cleans up empty tabs and switches to nearest workspace. App only exits when no panes remain anywhere.

title: Debounce close-pane hotkey to prevent double-close on key repeat
description:
The "two panes closing at once" bug is likely caused by keyboard repeat firing the close-pane hotkey twice before the first close completes. The key repeat interval on most systems is 30-50ms, and close_pane_by_id does layout mutation synchronously — but if two KeyboardInput events queue up in the same winit event batch, both fire against the current layout.

Fix: add a debounce/cooldown on destructive hotkeys (close pane, close window) so that rapid repeats within ~100ms are ignored. This is the same pattern terminals like Alacritty use for destructive actions.

## Files
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs` — hotkey dispatch, add timestamp check before calling close_pane
- Could use `Instant::now()` comparison against a `last_close_action: Option<Instant>` field on the window state
close_reason: Debounce added to close-pane/close-pane-by-id/close-all-panes hotkeys (100ms cooldown via last_close_action field on App struct)

title: Fix directional pane navigation (spatial) and audit close-double-fire
description:
Ctrl+Shift+Arrow keys don't navigate spatially between panes — they're aliased to index-based cycling (FocusNext/FocusPrev). User also reports two panes closing at once occasionally.

## Bug 1: Directional keys aliased to cycling (CRITICAL)

In `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs:690-694`, FocusUp/Down/Left/Right are grouped with FocusNext/FocusPrev:

```rust
KeyAction::FocusNext | KeyAction::FocusRight | KeyAction::FocusDown => {
    self.move_focus(FocusDirection::Next);
}
KeyAction::FocusPrev | KeyAction::FocusLeft | KeyAction::FocusUp => {
    self.move_focus(FocusDirection::Prev);
}
```

Pressing Ctrl+Shift+Right should move to the pane *to the right*, not the next pane in tree order.

## Bug 2: Cycling order is tree-order, not spatial

`adjacent_pane()` in `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/layout.rs:276-287` walks pane IDs in tree traversal order. In a 2x2 grid this causes unpredictable focus jumps.

## Bug 3 (investigate): Two panes closing at once

User reports closing one pane sometimes closes two. Close logic in `pane_ops.rs` looks safe (remove_pane returns early if pane missing, take() guards on_exit callback). May be a visual artifact of focus shifting unexpectedly due to Bug 1/2, or a separate race. Needs reproduction.

## Files involved

- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs` — dispatch (lines 690-694)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/layout.rs` — adjacent_pane() (lines 271-288)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs` — close logic (lines 116-243)
- `crates/therminal-core/src/config/keybindings.rs` — keybinding definitions (correct, not the issue)
design:
1. Add spatial variants to FocusDirection (Up/Down/Left/Right) or a separate SpatialDirection enum.
2. Implement `spatial_adjacent_pane()` on the layout that computes pane viewport rects and finds the nearest neighbor in the requested direction.
3. Separate dispatch: FocusNext/FocusPrev → index cycling, FocusUp/Down/Left/Right → spatial lookup.
4. For the double-close: add tracing/logging around close_pane_by_id to capture if it fires twice, then reproduce and fix if confirmed.
acceptance_criteria:
- Ctrl+Shift+Right moves focus to the pane visually to the right of the current pane
- Ctrl+Shift+Left/Up/Down behave spatially as expected
- FocusNext/FocusPrev (Ctrl+Shift+N/P) still cycle in order
- Closing a single pane never closes a second pane
- Works correctly with 2, 3, and 4+ pane layouts
- Existing pane tests pass, new tests cover spatial navigation
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Clean separation of SpatialDirection enum from FocusDirection kept the cycling logic untouched. The 2x cross-axis penalty in distance scoring correctly handles L-shaped and asymmetric layouts.
- What was unclear: Bug 3 (double-close) -- no reproduction path found. The on_exit.take() pattern in spawn.rs prevents duplicate PaneExited events per pane. Added detailed tracing (pane_count_before/after, focused pane state) to close_pane_by_id to capture future occurrences. The most likely cause is two panes exiting near-simultaneously (e.g., shell group), which is correct behavior.
## Root cause found for double-close bug

No repeat filtering on key events. At `window/mod.rs:1542-1546`, the match filters `ElementState::Pressed` but does NOT check `key_event.repeat`. Winit fires repeated `Pressed` events when a key is held, so holding Ctrl+Shift+W even briefly fires ClosePane multiple times.

Fix: either filter `!key_event.repeat` globally at line 1545, or selectively for destructive actions (ClosePane, CloseAllPanes, CloseWindow). Global filter is simpler and matches how most apps work — keybinding actions don't auto-repeat.
close_reason: Fixed spatial pane navigation: added SpatialDirection enum and spatial_adjacent_pane() using viewport center-point distance with 2x cross-axis penalty. Separated dispatch so FocusUp/Down/Left/Right use spatial lookup while FocusNext/FocusPrev use cycling. Added tracing to close_pane_by_id for double-close investigation. 7 new spatial navigation tests covering 2/3/4/5-pane layouts. Also fixed pre-existing clippy errors in therminal-daemon MCP code.

title: Upgrade render triplet: wgpu 23→28, cosmic-text 0.12→0.15, glyphon 0.7→0.10
description:
Coordinated upgrade of the version-locked render triplet. glyphon 0.10 requires wgpu ^28 and cosmic-text ^0.15 — all three must change together.

Biggest ROI: cosmic-text 0.15 adds ASCII fast-path that skips complex shaping/bidi for ASCII runs — significant terminal rendering perf win.

wgpu 28 changes: deferred command encoding, objects are Clone (drop Arc wrappers), device.poll() API reworked, Vulkan/GLES behind feature flags.

Part of tn-x2zq (dependency upgrade epic).
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
glyphon 0.10 requires wgpu ^28 and cosmic-text ^0.15. These three are version-locked through glyphon and MUST be upgraded together. The biggest win is cosmic-text 0.15's ASCII fast-path — skips complex shaping/bidi for ASCII text, which is ~95% of terminal output.

Target versions: **wgpu 28, cosmic-text 0.15, glyphon 0.10**.

## Task
Coordinated upgrade of the render triplet:

1. Update workspace `Cargo.toml`:
   - `wgpu = "28"` (from "23")
   - `cosmic-text = "0.15"` (from "0.12")
   - `glyphon = "0.10"` (from "0.7")

2. Fix wgpu 23→28 breaking changes in `crates/therminal-core/src/wgpu_context.rs`:
   - v24: Objects are `Clone` (internally refcounted) — remove `Arc` wrappers around Device, Queue, etc.
   - v24: `Instance::new()` takes `&InstanceDescriptor`
   - v25: `device.poll()` uses `PollType` instead of `Maintain`
   - v25: Vulkan/GLES backends now behind feature flags — check wgpu features in Cargo.toml
   - v26: `as_hal` returns guards instead of callbacks
   - v28: Deferred command encoding, stricter validation

3. Fix cosmic-text 0.12→0.15 changes in `crates/therminal-core/src/text.rs` and `font.rs`:
   - v0.13: `ShapeBuffer` moved into `FontSystem`
   - v0.14: Variable font features API
   - v0.15: `set_text`/`set_rich_text` gain alignment param, ASCII fast-path is automatic

4. Fix glyphon 0.7→0.10 changes in `crates/therminal-core/src/text.rs`:
   - Check `TextRenderer`, `TextAtlas`, `Cache`, `Viewport` API changes
   - `TextArea` and `TextBounds` may have new fields

5. Fix downstream in `crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs` and `window/chrome.rs` — they use glyphon types directly

6. Run `./scripts/ci.sh` — full workspace build + test

## Key Files
- `Cargo.toml` — workspace dependency versions
- `crates/therminal-core/src/wgpu_context.rs` — wgpu Device/Surface/Queue setup
- `crates/therminal-core/src/text.rs` — TherminalTextRenderer (glyphon + cosmic-text facade)
- `crates/therminal-core/src/font.rs` — FontSystem setup
- `crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs` — terminal cell rendering
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome.rs` — UI chrome text rendering

## When Done
Close issue: `bd close tn-1gjn --reason "Render triplet upgraded: wgpu 28, cosmic-text 0.15, glyphon 0.10"`
close_reason: Render triplet upgraded: wgpu 28, cosmic-text 0.15, glyphon 0.10. All CI checks pass (fmt, clippy, build, test).

title: Bug: hotspot detection highlights random first words as git branches
description:
GIT_BRANCH_RE in `hotspot_detection.rs` line 75 is `^[* ] {1,2}([A-Za-z0-9_/\-\.]+)` — this matches any line starting with 1-2 spaces followed by a word character. In practice this highlights the first word of every indented paragraph line as a "git branch".

Example: `"  This is a normal line"` → matches `"This"` as a GitRef.

## Root cause
The regex is too broad — it's designed for `git branch` output (`* main`, `  feature/foo`) but triggers on any indented text.

## Possible fixes
1. **Tighten the regex** — require branch name to contain `/` or `-` (most real branch names do), or require the `*` prefix specifically for the "current branch" case
2. **Context-aware detection** — only run git branch detection when surrounding lines also look like git branch output (multiple consecutive matching lines with consistent format)
3. **Require minimum complexity** — branch name must be at least N chars or contain a separator character
4. **Disable GIT_BRANCH_RE entirely** — git hashes still work via GIT_HASH_RE, branch detection has too high a false positive rate without context

Recommend option 1 + 3: require either `*` prefix (current branch) or that the name contains `/` or `-`, and minimum 2 chars.

## Files
- `crates/therminal-app/src/hotspot_detection.rs:74-75` (the regex)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/hotspot_detection.rs:183-195` (where it's applied)
close_reason: fixed in commit d7edd81 — tightened GIT_BRANCH_RE to require * prefix or separator chars

title: Fix: window stays blank after last pane exits (shell exit or close pane)
description:
When closing the last pane via shell `exit` or Ctrl+Shift+W, the window stayed alive as a blank frame instead of exiting. Root cause: `saved_layout` (from a previous CloseAllPanes) on App prevented the exit check from firing.

Fixed by moving `saved_layout` into `WorkspaceManager` so `workspaces == None` is the single exit signal. CloseAllPanes now keeps workspaces alive with an empty layout + saved snapshot inside.
close_reason: fixed in commit acb0531 — moved saved_layout into WorkspaceManager

title: Extract shared PTY runtime into therminal-terminal
description:
Extract the duplicated PTY lifecycle code from `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/spawn.rs` and `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs` into a shared abstraction in `therminal-terminal`.

Both files duplicate ~80 lines of identical boilerplate: `Term::new()` with `TermConfig`, `Arc<FairMutex<Term>>` wrapping, `spawn_shell()`, `try_clone_reader()`/`take_writer()`, thread spawn with `ansi::Processor` reader loop.

Create a `PtyPaneCore` (or similar) in `therminal-terminal` with:
- A `PtyEventHandler` trait with `on_output(&self, data: &[u8])` and `on_eof(&self)` methods
- Core struct owning: `Arc<FairMutex<Term>>`, PTY writer, PTY master (kept alive), reader thread handle
- Constructor that handles Term creation, PTY spawn, reader/writer clone, thread setup
- The reader loop calls the handler trait for output notification

App-specific additions (interceptor, process detector, PaneStatus updates, wake callback) stay in the app's handler impl. Daemon-specific additions (DaemonEvent broadcast, headless listener) stay in the daemon's handler impl.

Depends on pane.rs split (tn-yu08) so spawn.rs is a clean standalone module to refactor.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Clean trait-based abstraction with `PtyReaderHandler` letting each caller control both the `advance` method (plain vs with_interceptor) and post-read side effects. The `take_writer`/`take_pty_master` pattern works well for callers that need to decompose the core into separate owned fields.
- What was unclear: Whether `into_parts` would work with a Drop impl -- it doesn't (can't move out of Drop types), so removed it in favor of the take_ methods.
close_reason: Extracted shared PTY runtime into PtyPaneCore in therminal-terminal/src/pty_runtime.rs. PtyReaderHandler trait lets app and daemon customise reader loop without duplicating Term creation, PTY spawn, and thread management boilerplate. CI passes.

title: Split pane.rs into focused submodules

Split `crates/therminal-app/src/pane.rs` (2077 LOC) into submodules by responsibility:
