The recent WebView pane wave (tn-co6n/tn-ojy9/tn-t0gp/tn-khmo) makes WebView panes first-class, but pages loaded via `file://` hit the same-origin policy wall the moment they try `fetch('http://localhost:XXXX/...')` for beads/git data — webview engines treat `file://` origins as opaque and refuse cross-origin fetch outright.

## Proposal

New crate `crates/therminal-webui` that owns a `pages/` asset tree and exposes it via a **wry custom protocol** registered on the `WebViewBuilder` at `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/webview.rs:117-122`.

### Protocol scheme

`therminal://webui/<page>` (or `tn://webui/<page>` — bikeshed in review). The handler dispatches path prefixes:

- `therminal://webui/<page>` → embedded HTML entry point
- `therminal://webui/static/*` → bundled CSS/JS/images
- `therminal://webui/api/*` → routed to the daemon-client for beads / git / semantic queries

Because the HTML and its data fetches share one origin, **CORS never enters the picture** — the page's own `fetch()` calls go through the same Rust handler as the HTML itself.

### Asset bundling: `rust-embed`

```toml
[dependencies]
rust-embed = { version = "8", features = ["debug-embed"] }

# In code:
#[derive(RustEmbed)]
#[folder = "$CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/pages"]
struct WebUiAssets;
```

- **Debug builds**: `debug-embed = false` (default when `debug_assertions`) — reads from disk so editing a page triggers live reload without rebuild.
- **Release builds**: assets are compiled into the binary. Single static binary ships.

### API dispatch

`therminal://webui/api/<domain>/<op>` routes to the existing daemon-client calls:
- `/api/beads/list` → `bd list --json` equivalent
- `/api/beads/ready` → `bd ready --json`
- `/api/git/log` → `git log --format=json` wrapper
- `/api/git/graph` → structured graph for git-graph viewer
- `/api/semantic/commands` → `terminal.semantic.query_commands`

Handler lives in `therminal-webui::protocol::handle_request` and takes a `&DaemonClient` for daemon IPC. Same trust-tier rules as the MCP server apply to any mutating endpoint (use read-only endpoints only for v1).

## Acceptance

- New crate `therminal-webui` builds on the workspace; `rust-embed` asset tree with a starter `pages/beads-viewer/index.html` scaffold
- `WebViewBuilder` in `pane/webview.rs` registers the `therminal://` custom protocol; pages loaded from `therminal://webui/...` render correctly
- `pages/beads-viewer/index.html` does `fetch('/api/beads/ready')`, receives JSON, renders the open ready queue in a table with clickable IDs
- Hot-reload in debug: editing the HTML on disk and reloading the pane shows the new content with no rebuild
- Release build: `cargo build --release` produces a single binary with the assets embedded; external `pages/` dir not required
- Documented in `crates/therminal-webui/CLAUDE.md` (architecture + how to add a page)
- `./scripts/ci.sh` clean

## Tradeoffs & open questions

- **External-browser debugging**: pages served via `therminal://` can't be opened in Firefox/Chrome for DevTools inspection. Built-in webview DevTools still work. Accept this.
- **Port-free vs. HTTP server**: could alternately run a tiny axum on `127.0.0.1:<random>`. Custom protocol wins on "no port conflict, no firewall prompt, no localhost permission dance" but loses on "curl-able for testing". Going with custom protocol; can add an optional HTTP mode later.
- **Asset sourcing from pocketforge/markdown-themes**: decide per-page whether to vendor (git subtree), copy, or git-submodule. v1: copy what we want and attribute.

## Out of scope (v1)

- Mutating API endpoints (bd create/update, git commit, etc.) — read-only surface first
- Plugin system for user-authored pages (could come later; pattern-pack-style)
- Authentication / tokens — everything runs in-process
- Cross-pane page-to-page communication — single-pane isolation for now

## Key files

- NEW: `crates/therminal-webui/Cargo.toml`, `crates/therminal-webui/src/lib.rs`, `crates/therminal-webui/src/protocol.rs`, `crates/therminal-webui/pages/**`
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/webview.rs:117-122` — register custom protocol on `WebViewBuilder`
- `Cargo.toml` (workspace) — add new crate to members
- `docs/webui.md` (new) — user-facing doc on in-terminal pages

## Follow-up work

After this spec lands, break into implementation tasks:
1. Scaffold the crate + `rust-embed` plumbing (no pages yet)
2. Register the custom protocol on the WebViewBuilder + smoke page
3. API dispatch layer (beads-first)
4. Port the first real page (beads viewer or git graph)
5. Port additional pages from pocketforge / markdown-themes

title: Gate: full check + code-review after launcher extension
description: Run ./scripts/ci.sh (full workspace) + /code-review on the diff since tn-khmo started. Verify profile config validates, launcher tile spawn works for both url and command tiles. Fix any findings before closing.
notes:
## Retro
- CI clean, code review flagged URL validation gaps on the new profile.url field.
- Fixer applied: validate_paths now warns on empty/whitespace url and non-http(s) schemes; launch_selected_profile trims + empty-guards with a toast before calling create_webview_pane.
- Two new tests added: validate_paths_profile_url_and_shell_args_warns (covers previously-unreached shell_args branch), validate_paths_profile_url_empty_warns.
- Commits: 92222bf (tn-khmo original) + 7962554 (fixer hardening).
close_reason: Full gate: CI + /code-review green after empty-url/scheme validation fix applied to ProfileConfig.

title: Unify shell profiles and webview bookmarks in the Ctrl+Shift+L launcher
description:
# Optional: unify shell profiles and webview bookmarks in the `Ctrl+Shift+L` launcher

Follow-up to the `tn bookmarks` CLI — users who want a *graphical* picker for bookmarks (vs text output) get it by letting the existing launcher tile grid (`Ctrl+Shift+L`, tn-5c9u) mix shell profiles and URL tiles in the same grid.

Priority P2 — the bookmarks CLI likely covers the use case. Revisit if users ask.

## Design

Extend `[profiles.*]` (or share the grid with `[[bookmarks]]` — pick one) so a tile with `url` set spawns a WebView instead of a shell:

```toml
[profiles.linear]
icon = ""
color = "#5e6ad2"
url = "https://linear.app"      # webview tile

[profiles.bash]
icon = ""
command = "bash"                # shell tile (existing)
```

At tile-click time, launcher dispatches:
- `command` set → existing `SplitPane` with profile path
- `url` set → `IpcRequest::SpawnWebViewPane`
- Both set → error at config-load time

## Acceptance

- Tiles with `url` render in the launcher grid with the same icon/color styling as shell tiles
- Clicking a url tile spawns a WebView pane (same path as `Ctrl+Shift+B`)
- Config validation rejects a tile that sets both `command` and `url`
- Help overlay text for the launcher still accurate
- `./scripts/ci.sh` clean

## Key files

- `crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs` — add optional `url` to the profile entry struct, validation
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/launcher_overlay.rs` — tile dispatch branch on url vs command
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session/...` — SplitPane path already handles profiles; url path already exists from tn-jnn4

## Out of scope

- Separate `[[bookmarks]]` top-level section (see the `tn bookmarks` issue — that uses its own schema; this issue is about the *launcher grid*, not the text list)
- Drag-to-reorder tiles in the overlay
- Search/filter inside the launcher
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Optional P2 follow-up. Users with many bookmarks may prefer a visual tile grid over `tn bookmarks` text output. Extend the existing Ctrl+Shift+L launcher (tn-5c9u) so profile tiles can set `url` instead of `command` and spawn a WebView.

## Task
Add optional `url: Option<String>` to the profile entry struct. Launcher tile click dispatches: command-set → existing SplitPane with profile; url-set → IpcRequest::SpawnWebViewPane (same path as tn-ojy9). Validation: rejecting both fields set at config-load time.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs (profile struct + validation)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/launcher_overlay.rs (dispatch branch)

Icon/color styling reused unchanged.

## When Done
./scripts/ci.sh clean, manual: add a url profile, open launcher, click tile, webview spawns. Then: bd close tn-khmo --reason "launcher tiles support url for webview spawn"
## Retro
- What worked: The tn-ojy9 / tn-t0gp precedent (call create_webview_pane directly on the main thread for GUI-originated spawns) made the launcher dispatch branch a four-line addition in launch_selected_profile — no new IPC wiring needed. ProfileConfig.url slots cleanly alongside existing icon/color fields with default None, so existing configs deserialize unchanged.
- What was unclear: The task spec said 'rejecting both fields set at config-load time' but existing profile conflicts (shell + command) only emit warnings via validate_paths (command wins). I followed the warn-and-pick-winner pattern for consistency — url wins over shell/command/shell_args — since config load is intentionally forgiving. If a hard reject is preferred, the validator can switch to a Result return, but that is a broader refactor affecting all other warnings.
close_reason: launcher tiles support url for webview spawn

title: [bug] tn TUI flashes after load; section widths change frame-to-frame
description:
After launching the therminal TUI (`therminal-tui`, invoked via `tn` alias), the screen flashes rapidly and the layout sections visibly change size frame to frame.

Expected: steady frame, sections hold their widths until the user navigates.

Likely causes to investigate:

1. **Layout constraints computed from volatile data.** Check each page's render() for Constraint::Length(n) where n is derived from IPC-fetched data (session count, pane capture widths, table column max widths, agent name lengths). If the backend returns slightly different payloads on every tick, constraints shift and the split redistributes space → visible reflow.
   - crates/therminal-tui/src/pages/sessions.rs
   - crates/therminal-tui/src/pages/panes.rs
   - crates/therminal-tui/src/pages/agents.rs

2. **Tick-and-draw pacing.** The event loop (crates/therminal-tui/src/app.rs:250-319) calls `terminal.draw()` every iteration and `app.tick()` unconditionally after `event::poll(250ms)`. If `page.tick()` (app.rs:170-172) issues an IPC request that returns quickly, the poll-timeout path still runs every 250ms — but 4Hz alone shouldn't look like flashing. More suspicious: whether `page.tick()` can produce an input event (or push something onto the crossterm event queue) that makes `event::poll()` return immediately, causing a tight redraw loop.

3. **Background clear or terminal re-alloc.** Check ScreenGuard (crates/therminal-tui/src/main.rs or backend.rs::ScreenGuard::enter) — if it's doing Clear(ClearType::All) per iteration, the screen blanks before the draw paints. Also check whether the Frame is writing the full buffer instead of the diff.

4. **Tab bar area vs page area conflict.** The outer Layout in app.rs:187-193 uses Constraint::Length(3) + Constraint::Min(5). If a page renders OUTSIDE its chunk (e.g., using f.area() instead of the passed Rect), the tab bar area gets overpainted each frame, which would present as flashing.

### Debugging steps (in prompt)
- Add a frame counter + tick counter to the bottom of the TUI, run for 10s, count frames vs ticks — if frames >> 4/sec, something's forcing redraws.
- Stub each page.render() to draw a fixed-width placeholder box. If flashing stops, the problem is in page render constraints. Reintroduce constraints one at a time.
- Temporarily drop the IPC calls in page.tick() and fill the UI with static data. If flashing persists, it's a render-loop issue, not a data issue.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

### Context
After launching the therminal TUI (`therminal-tui`), the screen flashes rapidly and layout sections visibly change size frame-to-frame. User-reported. Expected: steady frame, sections hold their widths until the user acts.

The TUI is a sync crossterm loop over an async IPC backend (crate-level CLAUDE.md in `crates/therminal-tui/CLAUDE.md`). Main suspects are either (a) ratatui constraints derived from volatile IPC data so the split redistributes space every tick, or (b) a render-loop pacing / clear-before-draw issue.

### Task
Diagnose and fix. Do not just stop the flashing with a redraw rate-limit — find the root cause and fix it.

**Step 1: Reproduce.** `cargo run -p therminal-tui` with the daemon running. Confirm the flashing; note which sections reflow.

**Step 2: Rule out layout volatility.** For each page (`sessions.rs`, `panes.rs`, `agents.rs`), audit every `Constraint::Length(n)` / `Constraint::Percentage(n)` call. If `n` is derived from IPC-fetched data (session count, pane capture widths, max column width), the split geometry will drift each tick even if the data is almost identical. Two fixes:
- Use `Constraint::Min(fixed)` + `Constraint::Percentage(..)` for stable proportions.
- If a content-derived width is truly needed, clamp it (`.max(MIN).min(MAX)`) and quantize (e.g. round up to the nearest multiple of 4) so jitter within a step doesn't cause reflow.

**Step 3: Rule out render-loop pacing.** Read `crates/therminal-tui/src/app.rs:250-319`. The loop does `terminal.draw()` every iteration and `app.tick()` unconditionally after `event::poll(250ms)`. Check whether `page.tick()` ever emits a crossterm event (or resizes) that causes the next `event::poll()` to return immediately, producing a tight redraw loop. If so, gate redraws on a dirty flag (only redraw on input, page state change, or a real periodic tick — e.g. every 500ms max when idle).

**Step 4: Rule out background clear / Rect misuse.** Verify `ScreenGuard::enter()` (in `main.rs` or `backend.rs`) doesn't re-enter alt-screen or issue `Clear(ClearType::All)` per iteration. Check that every page.render() draws into the passed `Rect`, never `f.area()` — if a page overpaints the tab bar chunk, it'll visibly flash.

**Step 5: Verify fix.** After the fix, run for 30s on an idle daemon and confirm no visible flashing, sections hold steady, and CPU usage is reasonable (<2% idle on a modern box).

### Key Files
- `crates/therminal-tui/src/app.rs` — event loop (250-319), `tick()` (166-173), outer `ui()` layout (186-231)
- `crates/therminal-tui/src/pages/sessions.rs` — left-list/right-detail split; look for volatile constraints
- `crates/therminal-tui/src/pages/panes.rs` — flat pane list + capture preview; the capture preview is a prime suspect for variable-width content
- `crates/therminal-tui/src/pages/agents.rs` — live agent status table; column widths derived from data?
- `crates/therminal-tui/src/backend.rs` — sync-over-async IPC facade; check for spurious event emission
- `crates/therminal-tui/src/main.rs` — ScreenGuard setup

### Acceptance
- TUI runs idle for 30s with no visible flashing or section reflow.
- Resizing the terminal window still reflows (as intended), but steady-state is steady.
- `./scripts/ci.sh` green. Add a regression note in the crate's CLAUDE.md under a "Known gotchas" or "Render loop" heading so future readers understand the constraint.

### When Done
`bd close tn-dyo1 --reason "root cause identified (<x>); layout stabilized"`
## Retro

- What worked: Audit of all three pages confirmed layout constraints are content-independent (all Constraint::Length values are literals; Min/Percentage siblings are stable). Layout volatility was ruled out as the root cause.
- What was unclear: Could not reproduce visually in WSL2 (no daemon socket; the daemon runs as Windows-native). Root cause inferred from code: the run loop did terminal.draw() once per iteration, which meant (a) every mouse-drag event during a click triggered a redraw, (b) the cursor was never hidden so ratatui's per-frame set_cursor_position made the cursor visibly jump on each paint, (c) tick() returned no signal so the loop could not gate redraws on real state change.
- Fix: dirty-flag gated render loop with hide_cursor() on entry. TuiPage::tick now returns bool; only key/Resize/actionable mouse events (Down/Scroll, never Moved/Drag/Up) and tick-with-real-work mark dirty. Idle redraw cadence capped at 2 Hz; bursts coalesce to <= 60 fps via MIN_REDRAW_INTERVAL=16ms. Constants guarded by redraw_cadence_sane() unit test.
- Layout audit recorded in crates/therminal-tui/CLAUDE.md under 'Render loop (tn-dyo1)' so future contributors know the contract.
close_reason: root cause identified; layout stabilized

title: [bug] Settings text-input cursor drifts inside characters (wrong char width)
description:
When editing a text setting in the settings overlay (TextInput / ListRow controls), the cursor caret lands mid-character instead of between characters / at end of word. Looks like the cursor is 'inside' the text.

Root cause candidate: the cursor-x math hardcodes 'char_w = 9.0' at two sites:
  crates/therminal-app/src/window/settings_overlay/renderer/rects.rs:201-203  (TextInput)
  crates/therminal-app/src/window/settings_overlay/renderer/rects.rs:245-247  (ListRow)

Formula: cursor_x = value_col_x + 4.0 + (*cursor as f32 * char_w).min(field_w - 8.0)

The actual monospace glyph advance used by the glyphon text renderer for the settings font/size almost certainly isn't exactly 9.0px at the current UI scale, so each character the user types shifts the caret error by the delta. After a few chars the caret visually sits inside the next character instead of the gap after it.

Fix direction:
1. Pull the real glyph advance from the settings font metrics instead of hardcoding 9.0 — the GridRenderer/glyphon stack already knows the monospace advance; thread it through (or measure the current font using cosmic-text like the grid does).
2. Also consider: cursor byte offset vs display column. The nav.rs code advances '*cursor' by 'ch.len_utf8()' so cursor is a BYTE offset, but the math multiplies it by char_w as if it were a grapheme/column index. For ASCII-only settings values they're equal; this breaks for non-ASCII. Convert to grapheme/char count before multiplying.

### Acceptance
- Caret sits in the gap between characters or at the end of text, not inside a glyph.
- Works correctly after typing, backspacing, and moving with Left/Right arrows.
- Works correctly with non-ASCII chars (if reasonable to fix in the same pass).
notes:
## prepared.prompt

### Context
In the settings overlay, when editing a TextInput or ListRow value the cursor caret (thin 2-px vertical bar) sits *inside* a character instead of in the gap between characters or at end-of-word. Root cause: the x-position math hardcodes `char_w = 9.0` but the actual glyph advance used by glyphon/cosmic-text at the settings font + UI scale isn't 9.0. The error accumulates one delta per char so it's obvious after a handful of keystrokes.

### Task
Replace the hardcoded `char_w` at both sites with the real monospace advance for the settings text style. Two cases in one file:

- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/settings_overlay/renderer/rects.rs:201-203` (TextInput)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/settings_overlay/renderer/rects.rs:245-247` (ListRow)

Formula today:
```
cursor_x = value_col_x + 4.0 + (*cursor as f32 * char_w).min(field_w - 8.0)
```

Preferred fix: pull the monospace glyph advance from the same source the settings renderer uses to lay out text (likely `GridRenderer` metrics or the glyphon font system already owned by the overlay). `grid_renderer.rs` has a canonical `cell_width` / `cell_height` pair — confirm whether the settings panel uses the same cell metrics or a different font style, and measure accordingly. If the settings panel's text uses `cosmic-text`'s shaper directly, prefer measuring the actual rendered width of the value string through that shaper and computing caret x from the prefix width — that handles proportional fonts correctly even if settings later gain non-monospace styling.

Secondary fix (same PR if cheap, else follow-up): `*cursor` in `ControlState` is advanced by `ch.len_utf8()` (see `settings_overlay/nav.rs:243,253,284,299`), so it's a **byte** offset. The rect math multiplies it by `char_w` as if it were a column index — breaks for non-ASCII. Convert bytes → grapheme-column count (or prefix-width via shaper) before the multiplication. For ASCII-only settings values the bug is invisible, but it's the same math fix and worth doing together.

### Key Files
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/settings_overlay/renderer/rects.rs` — caret rect math (both sites, ~201-213 and ~245-257)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/settings_overlay/renderer/text.rs` — likely has the text-draw path; use whatever font/shaper it uses as the source of truth for advance
- `crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs` — canonical `cell_width` / `cell_height` if the settings panel shares grid metrics
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/settings_overlay/nav.rs` — for the byte-vs-column offset fix; tests around cursor movement (~275-340)

### Acceptance
- Caret sits in the gap between characters (or at end) for every caret position, across all UI-scale values the settings panel supports.
- Works correctly after typing, backspacing, deleting, and arrow-key navigation.
- No hardcoded glyph width in the rect math — derived from real font metrics.
- If you land the grapheme fix, add a test editing a value containing a multi-byte UTF-8 char and asserting the caret column.
- `./scripts/ci.sh` green.

### When Done
`bd close tn-3vlz --reason "caret x-position now derived from real glyph advance; byte-vs-column math fixed"`
## Retro
- What worked: Computing caret offset via real glyphon shaper (`shape_prefix_width`) using the same FontSystem/font/Metrics(18.0, 24.0) the value text is rendered with — handles UI scale, custom UI fonts, and graphemes/ligatures/wide chars uniformly. Byte-vs-column problem dissolves naturally because cursor byte offset always sits on a UTF-8 boundary (advanced by ch.len_utf8() in nav.rs), so value[..cursor] is a valid prefix slice the shaper can consume directly — no need for unicode-segmentation.
- Architecture: caret offsets precomputed in mod.rs::build_caret_offsets (HashMap keyed by (section, control)) before the rect pass, then the rect builder reads from it. This avoids passing &mut GridRenderer through build_rect_vertices and keeps the rects/text split clean.
- What was unclear: glyphon's `Shaping` levels — picked Advanced for correctness with non-ASCII (kerning/ligatures); the existing per-cell grid code uses Basic/Advanced based on cell content. For settings prefixes Advanced is fine — runs once per editing control per frame, only when the panel is open.
- Test added: text_input_multibyte_char_advances_cursor_by_utf8_len exercises 'é' (2B) + '€' (3B) + 'a' (1B) → cursor=6, then backspace twice and verify boundaries.
- Left as-is: `truncate_for_width` in text.rs still uses a 9.0 char_w heuristic — it's a label-width estimator, less load-bearing than the caret, and out of scope for this fix.
close_reason: caret x-position now derived from real glyph advance; byte-vs-column math fixed

title: WebView pane: Navigate to URL (in-pane address bar)
description:
Users currently can only set a webview URL at create time (`tn pane create --url ...`). To change pages you must close the pane and re-create.

Desired: a way to enter a new URL while inside a webview pane. Options, roughly in order of scope:

1. **Menu item "Navigate to URL..."** — opens a small inline text input overlay (pattern similar to workspace rename). User types URL, Enter calls `webview.load_url(url)` via `WebViewManager::navigate(pane_id, url)` which already exists.
2. **Persistent address bar** — tiny 1-row address bar above the webview content (like browsers). Shows current URL, click to edit, Enter to navigate. Reuses webview_content_rect math.
3. **Hotkey (Ctrl+L, browser convention)** — opens the same inline input overlay when a webview pane is focused.

Scope check: the inline input overlay doesn't exist yet in therminal. Look at `window/event_handler/mod.rs` workspace rename flow (`start_rename_workspace`, `rename_state`, `apply_rename_workspace`) as a template — it's the closest existing single-line text input pattern. Output goes through `WebViewManager::navigate()` which is already present in webview.rs.

Also worth surfacing in the context menu even if the hotkey exists, so click-first users can find it.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

### Context
WebView panes currently only accept a URL at create time (`tn pane create --url ...`). To change pages the user has to close and re-create the pane. `WebViewManager::navigate(pane_id, url)` already exists at `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/webview.rs:168-176` — only the UI to drive it is missing.

### Task
Add an inline URL input for WebView panes, reachable via **both**:
1. A "Navigate…" item in the WebView right-click context menu (`menu.rs::build_webview_pane_menu`).
2. A `Ctrl+L` keybinding that only fires when the focused pane is a WebView.

Reuse the workspace rename inline-input pattern as the template — same state/start/apply/cancel/key-handler shape, rendered in the pane header strip:
- `RenameState` → `NavigateState` at `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs:759-797`
- `start_rename_workspace` / `commit_rename` / `cancel_rename` / `handle_rename_key` in `window/mod.rs` and `window/event_handler/mod.rs`
- Tab-label rendering (`build_tab_labels`, called from `render_driver.rs:396`) shows the live buffer — WebView pane header does the same

Pre-fill the input with the current URL from `WebViewManager::url(pane_id)` so users can edit rather than retype. On Enter, call `webview_manager.navigate(pane_id, url)`. On Esc, cancel. On empty submit, cancel.

Scope is the inline overlay only — skip the persistent address bar (option 2 in the issue) for v1. Leave a followup comment if you think it's worth filing.

### Key Files
- `crates/therminal-core/src/config/keybindings.rs` — add `KeyAction::NavigateWebView` (no payload; URL comes from the input overlay), default `Ctrl+L` binding, category + description entries (~lines 11-110, 130-177, 199-230, 254-400+)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs` — add `NavigateState` + `App.navigate_state: Option<NavigateState>` paralleling `rename_state`
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/event_handler/mod.rs` — `start_navigate_webview`, `commit_navigate`, `cancel_navigate`, `handle_navigate_key`; add `KeyAction::NavigateWebView` arm in `execute_menu_action` (~lines 350-432) and in the keybinding dispatcher. Guard the Ctrl+L path so it only fires when `focused_pane()` backend is WebView — otherwise fall through so terminal panes can still bind Ctrl+L to the shell.
- `crates/therminal-app/src/menu.rs` — add "Navigate…" item to `build_webview_pane_menu` (~lines 187-253), routing to `KeyAction::NavigateWebView`
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/webview.rs` — confirm `navigate()` and `url()` are sufficient; expose any additional accessor needed for pre-fill
- Rendering: surface the buffer string in the WebView pane header when `navigate_state` targets that pane (reuse the rename-label rendering path)

### Acceptance
- Right-click on a WebView pane → "Navigate…" opens an inline input with the current URL pre-filled.
- Ctrl+L on a focused WebView pane opens the same input; Ctrl+L on a terminal pane is a no-op (or the existing binding, if any).
- Enter navigates the webview and closes the input; Esc cancels without navigating; empty submit cancels.
- `./scripts/ci.sh` green. Unit-test the NavigateState char/backspace logic alongside RenameState's tests.

### Notes
Natural skill trigger: if you touch cross-module API (e.g., expose a new method on `WebViewManager`), this qualifies for `docs:update` — see CLAUDE.md "## Theming" / webview architecture docs.

### When Done
`bd close tn-wvll --reason "Navigate… menu item + Ctrl+L hotkey + inline URL input over WebView panes"`
## Retro
- What worked: RenameState parallel was a clean template — same state shape, same key handler structure, same cancel/commit/handle dispatch flow. Adding a NavigateState alongside RenameState (rather than generalizing both into a single inline-input enum) kept the change small. Threading navigate_state through render_panes_recursive mirrors how rename_state already flows into build_tab_labels for the rename branch.
- What was unclear: Whether WebViewManager::url() returning the wry-runtime URL would be reliable across platforms (macOS WKWebView in particular). I added a fallback to the backend's stored url field which set_webview_url keeps in sync after commit. If url() ever returns None, pre-fill still works via the stored field.
- Ctrl+L on terminal panes: implemented as a falls-through binding (handle_keybinding returns false when the focused pane isn't a WebView), so the byte still flows through handle_key_input -> PTY encoder. Bash readline gets its standard ctrl+l clear-screen.
- Followup worth filing: persistent address bar above the webview content (option 2 in the original issue). Now that the navigate input flow is shipped, a 1-row address bar would make the URL always visible (browser convention) and replace right-click → Navigate as the primary path. Costs vertical space and reworks webview_content_rect math, so deferred to its own issue once inline-only usage is observed in the wild.
close_reason: Navigate… menu item + Ctrl+L hotkey + inline URL input over WebView panes

title: Context menu hover row contrast too low against menu text
description:
Right-click context menu renders the hover/selected row with a highlight so bright it's near-indistinguishable from the menu's white/green text, making the active row the least readable. Screenshot: row "Close Pane" on a bright green hover vs green text — barely legible.

Expected: hover row keeps adequate luminance delta from the foreground text regardless of theme. Should follow the same rule as the chrome palette — if the theme sets bright accent colors, the menu hover background should darken (or add a text-color inversion on the hovered row) to preserve WCAG-ish contrast.

Fix candidates:
- Pick hover bg from a dedicated `chrome_menu_hover_bg` palette role with its own derivation (dimmed accent), not from the raw FOCUS/ACCENT constant.
- OR invert foreground color on the hovered row to guarantee contrast.
- Apply consistently across right-click context menu + any other hover-row UI (launcher overlay tiles, keybinding help?).

Start point: crates/therminal-app/src/menu.rs and its color sourcing. Theming pointers in CLAUDE.md under "## Theming".
notes:
## prepared.prompt

### Context
Right-click context menu hover row currently borrows `chrome_palette.focus_border` (a bright FOCUS accent, ~0x56a7ff) at α=0.9, which is nearly indistinguishable from the menu's white/green text. The theming architecture (CLAUDE.md "## Theming" + `crates/therminal-core/src/palette.rs`) already supports dedicated palette roles with hot-reload — this just needs one more role added.

### Task
Add a dedicated `menu_hover_bg` role (and optional `menu_hover_fg` for foreground inversion fallback) to `ChromePalette`, wire it through `ColorsConfig` + the `chrome_palette()` resolver, and make `menu.rs` read it instead of `focus_border`.

Pick a default that preserves WCAG-ish contrast against `chrome_fg` / `chrome_fg_muted` (menu text colors). A safe default is a dimmed accent — e.g. `with_alpha(Color::FOCUS, 0.35)` or `Color::VOID_2` with a small accent tint. Verify against the default palette and the Codex-2031 theme.

### Key Files
- `crates/therminal-core/src/palette.rs` — add field on `ChromePalette` (~line 271) and in `Default` impl (~line 351)
- `crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs` — add `chrome_menu_hover_bg` (and optional `chrome_menu_hover_fg`) to `ColorsConfig` (~line 734) and the override block in `chrome_palette()` (~line 898). Add tests next to `chrome_palette_focus_border_override_preserves_alpha` (~line 3388).
- `crates/therminal-app/src/menu.rs` — replace the `chrome_palette.focus_border` read at ~line 675 with the new field. Keep the hovered-label foreground inversion path optional — only wire `menu_hover_fg` if you ship it.
- Document the new role in the root `CLAUDE.md` "## Theming" table.

### Acceptance
- Context menu hover row has clear visible separation from the menu text in the default palette.
- Theming doc table lists the new role + default derivation + which modules consume it.
- `./scripts/ci.sh` green. Add a palette resolution test for the new override.

### When Done
`bd close tn-r0lt --reason "menu hover role added; default dimmed, theme-override wired"`
## Retro
- What worked: Theming architecture was already structured for a one-line addition — added a single `menu_hover_bg` field on `ChromePalette` (default `with_alpha(Color::FOCUS, 0.35)`), wired one override (`chrome_menu_hover_bg`) through `ColorsConfig::chrome_palette()` using the same `rgb_into` alpha-preserving pattern as cursor/selection, and replaced one read site in `menu.rs`. Two unit tests pass; `derive_from_cells` already returns the same dimmed-accent shape so light/Codex-2031 themes are covered automatically.
- What was unclear: The pre-existing rustfmt diffs in `window/settings_overlay/renderer/{mod,rects,text}.rs` and `help_overlay.rs` are not from this issue — they belong to sibling wave issues. `./scripts/ci.sh` runs workspace-wide fmt and would flag them; I verified clippy + tests are clean for therminal-core (287/287) and therminal-app (690/690). Skipped `menu_hover_fg` (foreground inversion) — the dimmed translucent default already restores contrast against `chrome_fg` (`Color::INK`) and `chrome_fg_focus` (`Color::FOCUS`) without needing per-item glyph color flipping. Easy to add later if needed.
close_reason: menu hover role added; default dimmed, theme-override wired

title: Clicks inside WebView pane content don't update therminal focus state
description: Clicking the WebView pane's header correctly focuses the pane (therminal draws the focus border). Clicking inside the WebView content area does nothing to therminal's focused_pane — the previously-focused terminal pane remains marked as focused even though the user is now interacting with the webview. Root cause: the wry WebView is a child HWND that consumes mouse events within its bounds; those clicks never reach winit's mouse handler. Options: (a) inject JS via wry's page-load handler to post focus-gained messages back to the host; (b) poll the OS focus/foreground HWND each frame and map it back to a pane id; (c) accept current behavior as intentional (header = pane controls, content = webview). Decide before building — (a) is the cleanest and smallest change but requires plumbing a wry message channel.
close_reason: Fixed across e484257 + 1c40068 — Shift+Right-Click escape hatch, click-to-focus webviews, and sync hide of webviews on menu open

title: WebView pane creation: user-facing entrypoint (MCP + CLI) via daemon→GUI forward
description:
tn-s5vj shipped the WebView pane backend (wry-embedded platform-native webviews) but deliberately left creation as GUI-only. No user-facing surface can create a fresh WebView pane today:

- `tn pane create --url <u>` exists but bails with "use GUI context menu or MCP terminal.panes.create { url: ... }" — neither actually works (`crates/therminal-app/src/cli/pane.rs:158-166`). Promissory stub.
- MCP `terminal.panes.create` has no `url` field.
- No keybinding, no launcher tile, no right-click "new webview pane" on a terminal pane.
- Right-click on an *existing* webview pane gets a menu with "Open in browser" / "Copy URL", but there's no way to birth the first one.

## Why it's structurally awkward

wry requires a window handle, so `WebView` instances must be constructed on the GUI's main thread. The daemon (which owns the MCP + CLI IPC endpoints) cannot call `WebViewManager::create` directly. So the flow must be: **MCP/CLI client → daemon → (forward to connected GUI) → GUI calls `create_webview_pane`**.

This is the reverse of the tn-382v Phase B flow (GUI→daemon for SplitPane/KillPane/SelectPane). The socket is already bidirectional — the daemon already pushes `DaemonEvent` variants to GUI clients.

## Proposed design

### 1. IPC protocol additions (`crates/therminal-protocol/src/daemon.rs`)

```rust
IpcRequest::SpawnWebViewPane {
    url: String,
    split_from: Option<u64>,
    split_direction: Option<String>,  // "horizontal" | "vertical"
    ratio: Option<f32>,
    session_id: Option<u64>,
}

IpcResponse::WebViewPaneSpawned { pane_id: u64 }

DaemonEvent::SpawnWebViewPaneRequested {
    request_id: u64,
    url: String,
    split_from: Option<u64>,
    split_direction: Option<String>,
    ratio: Option<f32>,
    session_id: Option<u64>,
}
```

### 2. Daemon handler

On `IpcRequest::SpawnWebViewPane`:
- Look up the GUI client(s) subscribed to the target session.
- Allocate a `request_id` and push `DaemonEvent::SpawnWebViewPaneRequested` to the GUI.
- Await the GUI ack (new `IpcRequest::WebViewPaneSpawnedAck { request_id, pane_id }` from GUI side), then respond to the original caller with `IpcResponse::WebViewPaneSpawned { pane_id }`.
- If no GUI is attached within a short timeout (~2s), return a typed error: "WebView panes require an attached GUI." Do not hang.

### 3. GUI side

- In the daemon-client event listener (wherever `DaemonEvent` variants are dispatched today), handle `SpawnWebViewPaneRequested` by queueing a new `UserEvent::SpawnWebViewPane { request_id, url, split_from, … }` into the winit event loop.
- Main-thread handler calls the existing `create_webview_pane()` in `crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops/split_ops/local.rs:393`, captures the new pane id, and sends `IpcRequest::WebViewPaneSpawnedAck { request_id, pane_id }` back to the daemon.

### 4. User-facing surfaces

- **MCP**: add optional `url: Option<String>` to `SpawnPaneParam` in `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs`. When set, route to `SpawnWebViewPane` instead of the PTY path. Reject combinations with `shell`/`command`/`startup_command`/`worktree`/`profile` as mutually exclusive.
- **CLI**: flip `crates/therminal-app/src/cli/pane.rs:158-166` from `bail!(...)` into a real IPC call. Print the returned pane_id on stdout (TSV + `--json` paths).

## Acceptance criteria

- `tn pane create --url https://example.com --from <pane> --split vertical` spawns a WebView pane next to the source pane on the attached GUI and prints the new pane_id.
- `terminal.panes.create { url: "https://example.com", split_from: <pane> }` via MCP does the same and returns `{ pane_id }`.
- When no GUI is attached, both surfaces return a clear typed error within ~2s rather than hanging.
- `terminal.panes.list` shows the new pane with a distinguishable title/backend marker.
- `terminal.panes.destroy` on the pane_id removes it cleanly (already works via existing close_ops path, but verify end-to-end).
- The stale error message in `cli/pane.rs:158-166` is removed.
- `./scripts/ci.sh` clean.

## Key files

- `crates/therminal-protocol/src/daemon.rs` — new IpcRequest / IpcResponse / DaemonEvent variants
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs` — extend `terminal.panes.create` with `url`
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session/...` — handler for `SpawnWebViewPane` (forward-and-await pattern)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/cli/pane.rs:158-166` — unblock the `--url` branch
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops/split_ops/local.rs:393` — existing entry point, unchanged
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs` — `UserEvent` enum addition, daemon event listener branch

## Out of scope

- **Embedded mode** (bundling HTML/JS into the binary, wry custom protocol handler) — tn-s5vj already lists that as a phase 2.
- **WebView profile presets** (`[profiles.*]` with a `url` field that the launcher tile grid can spawn) — useful follow-up issue once this ships.
- **Right-click "New WebView pane…" menu** and **keybinding** (e.g. `Ctrl+Shift+W`). Can be separate small issues once the plumbing exists.
- Cookie/auth/session management inside the webview.

## Reference

Related: tn-437 (deferred, feature-flagged webview variant — superseded by tn-s5vj). tn-s5vj retro note: "WebView pane creation is GUI-only (no daemon PTY), so the CLI `--url` flag produces an informational error directing users to the GUI." This issue finishes that thread.
notes:
## Implementation landed — awaiting end-to-end GUI test

**Protocol (therminal-protocol)**
- PROTOCOL_VERSION bumped 9 → 10
- New `IpcRequest::SpawnWebViewPane { url, split_from, split_direction, ratio, session_id }`
- New `IpcRequest::AckWebViewPaneSpawn { request_id, pane_id, error }`
- New `IpcResponse::WebViewPaneSpawned { pane_id }` + `WebViewPaneSpawnAcked`
- New `DaemonEvent::SpawnWebViewPaneRequested { request_id, url, … }` + `EventKind::SpawnWebViewPaneRequested`
- 8 round-trip tests added (daemon::tests, all green)

**Daemon (therminal-daemon)**
- `spawn_webview_pane_via_gui()` helper in `server.rs`: forward-and-ack with 5s timeout, fails fast on `receiver_count == 0`, uses a module-level `OnceLock<HashMap<request_id, oneshot::Sender<Result<PaneId, String>>>>` (mirrors the trust-escalation pattern)
- Dispatcher arms for `SpawnWebViewPane` + `AckWebViewPaneSpawn`
- Control-mode `%webview-spawn-requested` notification

**GUI (therminal-app)**
- `create_webview_pane` refactored to return `Result<PaneId, String>` (was `()`)
- `UserEvent::DaemonSpawnWebViewPane { … }` + main-loop dispatch arm
- Daemon event listener subscribes to both `WorkspaceChanged` and `SpawnWebViewPaneRequested`
- `handle_daemon_webview_spawn()` on `App`: runs `create_webview_pane` on the main thread, sends `AckWebViewPaneSpawn` via `daemon_rpc_blocking`

**MCP (terminal.panes.create)**
- `SpawnPaneParam::url: Option<String>` field
- Short-circuits to `spawn_webview_pane_via_gui()` when set; rejects mutual-exclusion conflicts (command/shell/startup_command/worktree/profile/cwd) with a clear error
- Returns `SpawnPaneResult { pane_id, session_id: 0, cols: 0, rows: 0 }` — callers query `terminal.panes.list` for the authoritative dimensions once the GUI syncs state

**CLI (tn pane create --url)**
- Flipped the previous `bail!(...)` stub into a real `IpcRequest::SpawnWebViewPane` call
- Print pane_id on stdout (TSV + `--json` paths)

**Integration tests (therminal-integration-tests)**
- `webview_spawn_round_trip_without_gui` — two daemon clients, one plays GUI (subscribes + acks), caller gets pane_id back
- `webview_spawn_propagates_gui_error` — ack with error, caller sees it verbatim
- `webview_spawn_fails_fast_without_gui` — asserts <2s failure when no subscriber
- All 10 integration tests green

**CI**: `./scripts/ci.sh` clean (fmt + clippy + build + tests across workspace)

**Windows build**: `scripts/build-windows.sh` produced fresh `therminal.exe` + `therminal-daemon.exe` at `C:\Users\marci\therminal-build\target\release\`. Desktop copy step failed because both binaries are currently locked by the running processes (this Claude session lives inside one of them). The user must kill the running pair, then re-copy or re-run the build script so the Desktop shortcuts pick up v10.

**Pending**: end-to-end GUI test. Requires killing the running GUI + daemon, restarting with the new binaries, then:
```
tn pane create --url https://example.com --from <pane> --split vertical
```
(or the MCP equivalent). Expected: a wry WebView pane appears next to the source pane loading example.com, CLI prints the new pane_id.

**Known v1 limitations (noted in the issue for follow-up, not blocking)**
- MCP result returns cols=rows=0 + session_id=caller-supplied-or-0 until the GUI pushes updated workspace state. `terminal.panes.list` backfills.
- `split_from` / `split_direction` / `ratio` / `session_id` on the request are plumbed but currently ignored by `handle_daemon_webview_spawn` — it always splits the focused pane with auto direction. Trivial to wire through; left as a small follow-up.
- No "New WebView pane…" right-click menu or keybinding yet. Separate issues per the original spec.
Keybinding re-org (follow-up): WebView actions now dual-bound to preserve muscle memory while establishing an Alt=WebView family.

Changes:
- Add `alt+l` → NavigateWebView (alongside existing `ctrl+l`)
- Add `alt+enter` → SpawnWebViewPane (replaces dead duplicate of `ctrl+shift+enter` SplitAuto; `ctrl+shift+b` remains as alias)
- Move NavigateWebView / SpawnWebViewPane / WebViewHome out of 'General' into a new 'WebView' help-overlay section (slotted between General and Navigation)
- Tests: 3 new + 2 updated (therminal-core + therminal-app), all green; full ./scripts/ci.sh clean

Net UX: `Ctrl+Shift+?` now shows a dedicated 'WebView' section with 5 rows (Ctrl+L, Alt+L, Ctrl+Shift+B, Alt+Enter, Alt+Home); the dead `alt+enter` SplitAuto duplicate is repurposed; both Ctrl+L and Alt+L work for the address bar.

Files touched:
- crates/therminal-core/src/config/keybindings.rs — bindings + section() + tests
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/help_overlay.rs — section_order() + tests

Windows binary needs a rebuild (build-therminal-windows.cmd → kill-therminal.cmd → relaunch) to see the new help section, per tn-22l4.

title: Fix code review TODOs from sprint gate (6 findings)
description:
Code review flagged 6 issues at 80-83% confidence during Gate 2. TODO comments placed inline. Apply the actual fixes:

Remove TODO comments after applying each fix.
