description: After minimizing and restoring the therminal window on Windows, the terminal grid renders at the wrong dimensions — text wraps at incorrect column widths, appearing as garbled single-syllable fragments. Slightly resizing the window fixes it. The winit Restored event (or equivalent) is not triggering relayout_and_redraw(). Likely need to handle WindowEvent::Occluded(false) or the platform-specific restore event and call resize_all_panes + request_redraw.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Clear issue description with architecture pointers made the fix straightforward. The existing `relayout_and_redraw()` method was the exact right hook.
- What was unclear: Nothing significant — the bug description and fix guidance were precise.
close_reason: Restore from minimize triggers relayout. Added App.minimized flag, set on Resized(0,0), cleared on non-zero Resized with forced relayout_and_redraw(). Also added Occluded(false) handler as fallback.

title: Fix F11 focus mode: hide CSD title bar, add hover hint at top edge
description:
F11 focus mode hides status bar, pane headers, and tab labels — but the CSD title bar strip persists (empty except for close/min/max buttons) because `render_driver.rs:281` gates on `tab_bar_visible || use_csd`. On Windows (CSD always on), this leaves a pointless empty bar.

Fix: true fullscreen — hide the CSD bar entirely in focus mode. Add a subtle hover hint when the mouse reaches the top edge of the screen: translucent bar showing "F11 to exit focus mode" (and optionally the close/min/max buttons). Fades out when mouse leaves. Similar to browser/video player fullscreen behavior.

Right-click context menu still available for pane close/management.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
F11 focus mode hides status bar, pane headers, and tab labels — but the CSD title bar strip persists on Windows because render_driver.rs line 281 gates on \`tab_bar_visible || use_csd\`. This leaves a pointless empty bar with just close/min/max buttons.

## Task
Make F11 true fullscreen with a hover-reveal hint.

1. **Render driver** — crates/therminal-app/src/window/render_driver.rs:
   - Line 281: Change \`if tab_bar_visible || use_csd\` to also check \`!self.focus_mode\` so the CSD bar is hidden in focus mode
   - The status bar block at line 161 already correctly checks \`!self.focus_mode\`

2. **Layout geometry** — crates/therminal-app/src/pane/geometry.rs:
   - \`effective_tab_bar_height_csd()\` (line 79) needs a \`focus_mode: bool\` parameter — return 0.0 when focus_mode is active
   - Update \`content_area_rect_csd()\` (line 104) to pass focus_mode through
   - Update all call sites to pass the focus_mode flag

3. **Mouse hit-testing** — crates/therminal-app/src/window/mouse.rs:
   - CSD button hover (lines 512-538): skip CSD hit-testing when focus_mode is active
   - \`edge_resize_hit()\` (line 1028): should still work in focus mode for window resizing
   - \`header_hit_test()\` (line 223): already respects focus_mode at line 236

4. **Hover-reveal hint** — when focus_mode is active and mouse Y is within 4px of the top edge:
   - Show a small translucent overlay bar with text "F11 to exit focus mode"
   - Optionally show close/min/max buttons alongside
   - Use OverlayLayer with Modal tier for the hint
   - Fade out when mouse leaves the top edge zone
   - Track hover state in App struct (e.g. \`focus_mode_hint_visible: bool\`)

5. Run ./scripts/ci.sh to verify.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render_driver.rs (CSD render gate)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mouse.rs (CSD hit-testing, hover detection)
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/geometry.rs (layout height computation)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs (App struct, focus_mode flag)

## When Done
Close issue: bd close tn-sfn9 --reason "F11 now hides CSD bar; hover hint shows at top edge"
## Retro
- What worked: Following the toast.rs pattern exactly for the overlay text rendering avoided API mismatch issues. The geometry function signature change propagated cleanly through all call sites.
- What was unclear: The original CSD-stays-visible-in-focus-mode was an intentional design choice (for window close/min/max access). The hover hint replaces that affordance.
close_reason: F11 now hides CSD bar; hover hint shows at top edge

title: fix: settings overlay Select fields reset on every keystroke + no active theme indicator
description:
Two settings overlay issues:

1. **Select fields (UI Text Scale, New Pane CWD) were unusable** — `sync_toggle_values()` called after every key event rebuilt section controls from scratch, resetting `expanded` and `selected` state. Users could never cycle through options in a Select dropdown.

2. **No visual indicator for active theme** — Theme presets were plain Action controls with no state tracking. After applying a theme, all presets looked identical.

Fix: snapshot/restore in-progress Select and TextInput editing state across section rebuilds; track `active_theme` on `SettingsOverlayState` and render `(active)` label in accent color next to the applied preset.
close_reason: Fixed: snapshot/restore preserves Select/TextInput editing state across sync_toggle_values rebuilds; active_theme tracked and rendered with (active) label. 3 new tests added, all 30 settings overlay tests pass, CI green.

title: Hotspot context menus do not respect theme colors
description:
The right-click / click-to-open hotspot context menus always render with the default blue theme colors, regardless of the active theme. Like the settings overlay (tn-v4iq), these menus are not reading from `ChromePalette` and don't update on theme hot-reload.

All overlay/popup UI should derive colors from `renderer.chrome_palette.*` so they re-skin consistently with the rest of the chrome.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Straightforward pattern following tn-v4iq settings overlay fix. ChromePalette is Copy so extracting it before the &mut borrow was trivial.
- What was unclear: Nothing significant. The borrow checker caught the &mut + & conflict on renderer, resolved by copying the palette (it's Copy) before the call.
close_reason: Context menus now read from ChromePalette; colors update on theme hot-reload

title: Terminal selection highlight color makes overlaid text unreadable
description:
The default terminal text selection/highlight color (light blue, `Color::ACCENT_COOL` @ α=0.35) overlays on top of white text in certain TUI applications (e.g. McFly header menu), making the text nearly unreadable — white-on-light-blue with insufficient contrast.

Screenshot evidence: McFly's header bar shows white text on a light blue highlight where options like "ESC - Exit | ↵ - Run | TAB - Edit | F1 - Rank Sort | F2 - Delete | F3 - Alt Directories" are barely legible.

Additionally, this highlight color does not update when the theme changes — it stays the same even after switching themes via hot-reload.

Two sub-problems:
1. **Contrast**: the default selection alpha/color doesn't provide enough contrast against white text in the default theme
2. **Hot-reload**: selection color is not being refreshed from `ChromePalette` on theme change (should follow `chrome_palette.selection`)
notes:
## Investigation

Selection highlight IS wired to ChromePalette — `resolved_selection_color()` at grid_renderer.rs:784-788 reads `self.chrome_palette.selection`. Hot-reload SHOULD propagate. If it doesn't in practice, the issue is either the config override not being parsed, or the TUI app setting its own BG that blends poorly with the selection overlay.

The primary issue is the default alpha value: `Color::ACCENT_COOL @ α=0.35` (palette.rs:373) doesn't provide enough contrast over white/light text.

## files_touched
- crates/therminal-core/src/palette.rs (selection default, line 373; derive_from_cells line ~487)
- crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs (chrome_palette() resolver, selection override handling)
- crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs (resolved_selection_color, line 784)

## prepared.prompt

## Context
The terminal selection highlight uses `Color::ACCENT_COOL` at α=0.35, which is a light blue overlay. When TUI apps like McFly render white text on a colored background, the selection overlay creates white-on-light-blue which is nearly unreadable. The selection color IS properly wired to ChromePalette (grid_renderer.rs:784-788), so hot-reload should work — but the default needs better contrast.

## Task
Improve the default selection highlight contrast so it works well over both dark and light text.

1. In `crates/therminal-core/src/palette.rs`:
   - Line 373: Change the default selection alpha from 0.35 to a higher value (try 0.45-0.50) so the overlay is more opaque and provides better contrast
   - Line ~487 (`derive_from_cells`): Apply the same alpha change to the derived palette path
   - The goal is: selection should be clearly visible without completely obscuring the text underneath

2. Verify the hot-reload path works:
   - In `crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs`, check that `ColorsConfig::chrome_palette()` applies the `selection` override from `[colors]` config. The existing `selection` field in `ColorsConfig` should map to `ChromePalette.selection`.
   - If the override path is broken, fix it so `[colors] selection = "#rrggbb"` properly overrides the selection color.

3. Run the existing palette tests to make sure nothing breaks.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-core/src/palette.rs
- crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs (just for reference)

## When Done
Run `./scripts/ci.sh` to verify build + clippy + tests pass.
Close issue: bd close tn-hh3d --reason "Selection highlight alpha increased for better contrast over light text; hot-reload path verified"
## Retro
- What worked: The issue description was well-prepared with exact line numbers and clear instructions. Both the default path (ChromePalette::default) and the derived path (derive_from_cells) were identified upfront, and the hot-reload path was already correctly wired.
- What was unclear: The working tree had pre-existing uncommitted changes from other work (settings overlay, help overlay, session_pty tests) that caused a compile error unrelated to this task. Had to investigate and work around those.
close_reason: Selection highlight alpha increased from 0.35 to 0.45 for better contrast over light text; hot-reload path verified working via rgb_into() in ColorsConfig::chrome_palette()

title: Settings overlay does not update colors on theme change
description:
The settings overlay menu does not adjust its colors when the theme changes, unlike the rest of the chrome (tab bar, status bar, pane headers, etc.). When hot-reloading `[colors]` or switching themes, the settings panel keeps its original colors while everything else re-skins.

This is inconsistent with the tn-g7oo theming architecture where `ChromePalette` is rebuilt on every `apply_color_overrides` call and all chrome modules read from `renderer.chrome_palette.*`.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: ChromePalette is `Copy`, so snapshotting it before the mutable renderer borrow in pass 2 was clean and zero-cost. The existing pattern of `renderer.chrome_palette.<role>` in other chrome modules made the mapping obvious.
- What was unclear: Nothing significant. The task spec mapped each hardcoded color to the right palette role clearly.
close_reason: Settings overlay now reads from ChromePalette; colors update on theme hot-reload

title: Settings overlay: arrow key navigation gets stuck on dropdown/slider fields
description:
In the settings overlay, navigating with up/down arrow keys gets stuck on certain field types:

1. **Shell section → "New pane cwd"** — arrow keys stop working after navigating to this field
2. **Accessibility → "UI text scale"** — same issue

The user has to Esc out of the settings modal and reopen it to recover. These fields are likely dropdowns or sliders that capture the arrow key input instead of passing it back to the settings navigation handler.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: The bug was clearly caused by Select controls always capturing Up/Down arrows via active_control_is_select(). The fix mirrors the existing TextInput editing/expanded pattern exactly. Adding a new test (select_up_down_navigate_when_not_expanded) directly verifies the reported bug is fixed.
- What was unclear: Nothing significant -- the pattern was well-established by the TextInput precedent.
close_reason: Arrow key navigation no longer gets stuck on dropdown/slider fields in settings overlay

title: WSL path resolution chain tests (state files, JSONL tailer, process detector)
description: Table-driven tests for the three tn-966s translation layers: (1) state.rs rewriting /tmp/claude-code-state/ to \\wsl.localhost\distro\tmp\..., (2) jsonl_tailer.rs resolving ~/.claude/projects/ against the WSL user's home via detect_wsl_home(), (3) process_detector.rs parsing wsl.exe -e ps output through the classifier. Each layer should be testable with injected/mocked wsl.exe output (distro name, home dir, ps output) so tests run on any platform. Cover: multiple distros, spaces in paths, missing wsl.exe fallback, pure-Windows panes bypassing WSL translation, UNC path edge cases.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: The three WSL translation layers already had clean pure-function separation (linux_to_unc, expand_home_to_unc, parse_wsl_ps, classify_wsl_process) that made table-driven testing straightforward without needing mock injection or cfg(test) setters. The OnceLock shell-out wrappers are thin enough that testing the pure functions underneath gives high coverage.
- What was unclear: is_valid_session_id lived in jsonl_tailer.rs not state.rs -- had to move the test to the correct module after a compile error.
close_reason: Added table-driven WSL path resolution tests for state.rs, jsonl_tailer.rs, and process_detector.rs. 12 new test functions covering all three translation layers with injected mock data, running on any platform.

title: End-to-end render pipeline tests (Term → hotspot → RenderCell assertions)
description: Create tests that exercise the full chain from PTY bytes to rendered cells without GPU. Feed bytes into an alacritty Term, extract row text, run hotspot detection + pattern engine, call apply_hotspots_to_cells, and assert on the resulting RenderCell grid — which cells have hotspot set, correct start_col/end_col, correct kind. This catches bugs like tn-mnlo where data flows across engine.rs → hotspot_detection.rs → render.rs → grid_renderer.rs and no single crate's unit tests cover the chain. Key scenarios: pattern hotspot with highlight group, overlapping hotspots (URL suppresses file path), wrapped-line hotspots across row boundaries, alt-screen enter/exit preserving primary screen state.
notes:
## files_touched
- crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs (RenderCell struct)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs (apply_hotspots_to_cells, extract_row_text_from_cells, compute_wrap_continuation)
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/hotspot_detection.rs (detect_hotspots_from_text_with_wrap)
- crates/therminal-integration-tests/tests/scenarios.rs (existing integration tests)

## prepared.prompt

## Context
Bugs like tn-mnlo cross four crates through data (PatternMatch.byte_end → TextHotspot.end_col → RenderCell.hotspot → underline rect) — no single crate's unit tests catch them. We need integration tests that exercise the full chain from terminal content to render cells without needing a GPU.

## Task
Create integration tests in `crates/therminal-integration-tests/` that exercise the render pipeline end-to-end:

1. Create a new test file `crates/therminal-integration-tests/tests/render_pipeline.rs`
2. Write a test helper that: creates an alacritty Term, feeds bytes into it via `advance_with_interceptor`, extracts cells as RenderCell structs, runs `extract_row_text_from_cells` + `compute_wrap_continuation`, runs `detect_hotspots_from_text_with_wrap`, calls `apply_hotspots_to_cells`, returns the final cell grid
3. Write test scenarios:
   - **Pattern hotspot with highlight group**: feed a line containing "PTY" with a glossary-style pattern, assert only the 3 PTY cells have `hotspot = Some(...)`, not trailing context
   - **Overlapping hotspots**: feed a line with a URL and a file path in the same region, assert URL suppresses file path
   - **Wrapped-line hotspot**: feed a file path that wraps across two rows, assert hotspot spans both rows with correct start_col/end_col per row
   - **Alt-screen round-trip**: feed content, enter alt screen (ESC[?1049h), feed alt content, exit alt screen (ESC[?1049l), assert primary screen content is restored correctly

Note: `RenderCell`, `apply_hotspots_to_cells`, `extract_row_text_from_cells`, and `compute_wrap_continuation` are currently private to therminal-app. You'll need to either make them pub(crate) and re-export from a test-support module, or add the tests as `#[cfg(test)]` within therminal-app itself (e.g. `crates/therminal-app/src/window/render_tests.rs`). Prefer the latter to avoid leaking internal types.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs:138 — RenderCell struct
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs:36 — apply_hotspots_to_cells
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs:125 — extract_row_text_from_cells
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/hotspot_detection.rs — detect_hotspots_from_text_with_wrap

## When Done
Run `cargo test -p therminal-app -- render_pipeline` (or the relevant test filter). Then:
bd close tn-75kq --reason "Added N render pipeline integration tests covering hotspot, wrap, and alt-screen scenarios"
## Retro
- What worked: The existing test patterns in remote_spawn.rs and the daemon session tests provided a clear template for creating headless Term instances. The cell extraction logic in render.rs was straightforward to replicate in the test helper.
- What was unclear: Nothing major -- the only stumbling blocks were API differences (Dimensions trait import, tempfile deprecation).
close_reason: Added render pipeline integration tests covering hotspot highlight group, overlap, wrap, and alt-screen scenarios

title: Unignore and stabilize daemon PTY session tests
description: 9 tests in crates/therminal-daemon/tests/session_pty.rs are ignored: create_session_shell_running, attach_snapshot_has_content, write_to_pane_echo_output, destroy_session_cleans_up, capture_pane_content, split_and_kill_pane, split_pane_horizontal/vertical, kill_pane_cascades_resize. These cover the exact attach/snapshot/write lifecycle where bugs are hardest to diagnose. Either make them reliable in CI (may need a PTY harness or timeout tuning) or replace with equivalent stable coverage.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Replacing fixed `thread::sleep` with polling helpers (`wait_for_prompt`, `wait_for_output`) that retry every 50ms with a 10s deadline was the right pattern. All 11 tests pass reliably across 6 consecutive runs (3+3). The 10s timeout handles the worst case where 11 PTYs spawn in parallel on a loaded CI runner.
- What was unclear: The initial 5s timeout was marginal -- one failure in 3 runs under parallel load. Bumping to 10s eliminated it. The `require_pty` function was also a no-op (returned from itself, not the caller) -- converted to a macro for correct early-return semantics.
close_reason: Unignored and stabilized all 11 PTY session tests. Replaced fixed thread::sleep with polling helpers, 10s timeout, require_pty macro. Verified stable across 6 consecutive runs.

title: Expand test suite: cross-crate integration tests and WSL path coverage
description:
Unit test coverage is strong (422 in therminal-terminal, 581 in therminal-daemon, etc.) but bugs increasingly cross crate boundaries through data rather than APIs. The tn-mnlo hotspot underline bug traversed engine → hotspot_detection → render.rs → grid_renderer across four crates — no single crate's tests could catch it. The ignored daemon PTY tests (tn-htuh) leave the attach/snapshot/write lifecycle untested. WSL cross-boundary path resolution has multiple translation layers that compound edge cases.

Three areas to address:

1. Unignore and stabilize daemon PTY session tests (tn-htuh) — the attach/capture/write/split/kill lifecycle paths
2. End-to-end render pipeline tests — feed bytes into a Term, run full hotspot/pattern detection, assert on RenderCell grid (which cells have hotspot set, underline extent, etc.) without needing GPU
3. WSL path resolution chain tests — table-driven tests that feed path shapes through the full state file → JSONL tailer → process detector resolution chain, covering UNC paths, distro detection, and home directory translation
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Pattern hotspot highlight group: underline covers full match instead of keyword
description:
Glossary pattern pack regexes like `\b(?P<term>PTY)\b(?P<ctx>.{0,200})` use a trailing context capture for event payloads. The pattern engine used the full match span (group 0) for the hotspot's visual extent, causing the dotted underline to stretch from the keyword across all trailing text to the right edge of the terminal.

Fix: added `highlight` field to `[pattern.hotspot]` TOML schema. When set, the engine narrows `PatternMatch.byte_start/byte_end` to the named capture group's span instead of the full match. Updated all glossary hotspot patterns to set `highlight = "term"`.
close_reason: Fixed: added highlight field to hotspot schema, updated engine to narrow byte span, fixed glossary patterns, added tests.

title: Derive ChromePalette from cell colors when chrome roles unset
description:
`ChromePalette::Default` falls back to the compile-time Codex 2031 constants (`Color::VOID_0`, `Color::INK`, `Color::FOCUS`, etc.) when a chrome role field is unset. This means any user who sets only `[colors] background = ...` in `therminal.toml` — or picks a theme preset that only overrides terminal cells — gets a mismatched dark Codex chrome on top of their chosen palette.

The real fix is to make chrome roles derive from the *resolved terminal cell palette* (background, foreground, ANSI indices) when not explicitly overridden. Then any theme change — preset, hand-edited `[colors]`, or hypothetical color scheme import — drags the chrome along with it automatically, and explicit chrome role fields become true overrides for users who want decoupled control.

Complements tn-2xwr (which ships the mechanical per-preset fix). This issue is the deeper architectural change.
design:
**Files**:
- `crates/therminal-core/src/palette.rs` — `ChromePalette::Default` impl
- `crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs` — `ColorsConfig::chrome_palette` resolver

**Current behavior**: `chrome_palette()` builds a `ChromePalette::default()` (compile-time Codex constants) and then selectively overrides fields whose config keys are `Some`.

**Target behavior**: derive a `ChromePalette` from the *resolved cell palette* — the background/foreground/ansi values that the renderer actually uses after all overrides apply — and then layer explicit `chrome_*` / `hotspot_*` overrides on top.

**Derivation rules (first pass — adjust in review)**:

Given resolved `bg` (window background), `fg` (window foreground), and the 16-entry `ansi` array:

- `header_bg` = bg shifted toward fg by ~6–10% luminance (darker on dark themes, lighter on light). Use HSL or OKLCH luminance, not raw RGB mixing, to stay perceptually uniform.
- `header_bg_dim` = bg shifted by half of that (~3–5%).
- `status_bar_bg` = `header_bg_dim`.
- `focus_border` = `ansi[4]` (blue) with alpha 0.92. Fallback: `ansi[6]` (cyan) if blue luminance collides with bg.
- `separator` = bg shifted toward fg by ~20%.
- `chrome_fg` = fg.
- `chrome_fg_muted` = fg blended 50% with bg.
- `chrome_fg_focus` = same as `focus_border` sans alpha.
- `chrome_fg_warn` = `ansi[3]` (yellow).
- `chrome_fg_alert` = `ansi[1]` (red).
- `csd_close` = `ansi[1]`.
- `exit_ok` / `exit_error` = `ansi[2]` / `ansi[1]` @ α=0.90.
- `selection` = `ansi[4]` @ α=0.35 (or fall back to explicit `colors.selection` if set — already handled).
- `cursor` = fg @ α=0.85 (or explicit `colors.cursor`).
- `hotspot_filepath` = `ansi[6]` (cyan/teal).
- `hotspot_url` = `ansi[4]` (blue).
- `hotspot_error` = `ansi[1]` (red).
- `hotspot_gitref` = `ansi[3]` (yellow).
- `hotspot_issueref` = `ansi[5]` (magenta).
- `hyperlink` propagates from `hotspot_url` (existing rule).

Explicit `chrome_*` and `hotspot_*` keys in `[colors]` still override the derived values — just as they do today — so users who want decoupled chrome can still set them.

**Luminance helper**: add a small helper to `therminal-core::palette` (or reuse an existing one) for HSL-or-OKLCH shift. OKLCH is preferred (perceptually uniform) but HSL is acceptable if adding a crate dep is undesirable; `palette` crate is already in the workspace — check.

**Tests**:
- Given a dark palette, `header_bg` has lower luminance than fg and higher than bg.
- Given a light palette, `header_bg` is darker than bg (inverts sign of shift).
- Explicit `chrome_header_bg = "#ff00ff"` wins over derivation.
- Round-trip: setting only `background` + `foreground` produces a visually coherent chrome on both "Paper" (light) and "Tomorrow Night Bright" (dark).

**Interaction with tn-2xwr**: Once this lands, the per-preset chrome overrides from tn-2xwr become optional — the presets could drop them all and still produce correct-looking chrome via derivation. But keeping them as explicit overrides lets per-preset fine-tuning survive, so I'd leave tn-2xwr intact when this ships.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `ColorsConfig::chrome_palette` derives chrome role defaults from the resolved cell palette (bg/fg/ansi) instead of compile-time Codex constants
- [ ] Explicit `chrome_*` / `hotspot_*` keys in `[colors]` still override derived values
- [ ] Setting only `background = "#..."` (or using a minimal theme preset) produces readable chrome with appropriate contrast on both light and dark themes
- [ ] Unit tests covering: dark-theme derivation, light-theme derivation, explicit override precedence, luminance-shift sign inversion
- [ ] Existing palette tests continue to pass
- [ ] `./scripts/ci.sh` passes
close_reason: Implemented. `ColorsConfig::chrome_palette` now starts from `ChromePalette::derive_from_cells(bg, fg, ansi)` when the user has overridden any cell field, and from `ChromePalette::default()` otherwise — preserving the no-override path bit-for-bit (`chrome_palette_default_matches_default_chrome_palette` still passes unchanged as the anchor invariant). Derivation mixes bg toward fg in sRGB space so the luminance shift sign auto-inverts for light vs dark, pulls accents from ANSI indices when provided (idx 4 blue → focus, idx 3 yellow → warn, idx 1 red → alert, idx 6 cyan → filepath, idx 5 magenta → issueref) with Codex constants as fallback, and preserves all alpha bake-in for translucent roles. Explicit chrome_*/hotspot_* keys still win. 15 new tests: 9 in palette.rs covering derivation shape + sign flip + alpha preservation + WCAG AA contrast across 4 bg/fg pairs, 6 in config/mod.rs covering the wiring + explicit-override precedence + malformed-ansi fallback. ./scripts/ci.sh passes (fmt, clippy, build, 244 core tests + 7 integration tests). Committed as 022e2e9 on top of tn-2xwr (c58ea06). Not yet pushed — user is mid-test of tn-2xwr's rebuild.

title: Chrome (status bar / tab bar / CSD) stale on theme hot-reload — color change not detected
description:
User reports: editing [colors] in therminal.toml does not update the big header / footer (status bar, tab bar, CSD title strip, pane headers). They stay the original theme until app restart.

Root cause: apply_config in crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs:681-691 had a narrow colors_changed diff that only compared {background, foreground, cursor, selection, ansi}. Every chrome role override added by tn-g7oo (chrome_focus_border, chrome_header_bg, chrome_header_bg_dim, chrome_status_bar_bg, chrome_tab_bar_bg, chrome_tab_active_bg, chrome_csd_close, chrome_fg, chrome_fg_muted, chrome_fg_focus, chrome_fg_warn, chrome_fg_alert, hotspot_filepath, hotspot_url, chrome_hyperlink, hotspot_error, hotspot_gitref, hotspot_issueref, plus foreground_bright / foreground_muted / surface) was invisible to the diff. Editing only a chrome_* field left colors_changed == false, so apply_color_overrides was never called and GridRenderer.chrome_palette stayed stale. Every chrome module reads chrome_palette directly so the entire UI stayed on the pre-edit theme.

Fix: drop the colors_changed gate and always call apply_color_overrides on every config event. apply_color_overrides is cheap (field assigns + hex parse + clear_render_caches), and apply_config is already 500ms-debounced by the config watcher.
close_reason: colors re-applied unconditionally on every config event

title: Settings overlay: Shell + Accessibility sections empty on first open
description:
User reports: on first open of the settings overlay, the Shell and Accessibility sections render empty. Closing and re-opening shows them populated normally.

Root cause: SettingsOverlayState::seed_defaults (crates/therminal-app/src/window/settings_overlay/sections.rs:220-255) registers these sections with empty control vectors. The controls are populated lazily by rebuild_shell_section / rebuild_accessibility_section / rebuild_hotspots_section, which only run from sync_toggle_values. The only caller of sync_toggle_values was the keyboard handler in event_handler.rs:1384 — which fires AFTER the overlay has already rendered at least one frame. On first open the overlay draws before any key press, so the sections appear empty. On second open the stale populated state from the prior session's key event is still in memory.

Fix: call sync_toggle_values from open_settings_overlay (event_handler.rs:48) before reset_navigation so the first frame sees populated sections.
close_reason: sync_toggle_values now runs from open_settings_overlay

title: Hotspot underlines extend past content-area right edge during/after resize
description:
## Symptom

User (Windows native build, Claude Code in WSL pane) reports hotspot underlines (MCP tool call hotspots, file paths, URLs) extending off the right edge of the pane during or after a window/font-size resize. Observed in a screenshot where the pane dimensions had just changed from 205×35 to 151×36.

## Hypothesis

Stale hotspot column indices. Likely sequence:

1. Hotspot detection runs during a frame at grid_cols = 205.
2. Detected `TextHotspot` records cell coordinates in the 205-col coordinate space.
3. Font size changes (user logs: `font size adjusted font_size=17.0 → 16.0 → 15.0 → 14.0`), which triggers a grid re-measure to 151 cols.
4. The cached hotspot records are rendered against the new 151-col layout, so column indices ≥151 place the underline past the content-area right edge.
5. Either (a) the hotspot cache is not invalidated on resize, or (b) the underline rendering path uses the cached pixel positions from the 205-col metrics without rechecking.

## Investigation plan

1. Reproduce: open a pane with visible hotspots (e.g. `ls` a directory with colored file-path output and long URLs), change font size a few times with `Ctrl+-` / `Ctrl+=`. Watch for underlines past the right edge.
2. Check `crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs` hotspot detection caching: is the cache keyed on `(pane_id, revision)` or also on `grid_cols`? Look for `hotspot_map`, `detect_hotspots_from_text_with_wrap`, `extend_hotspots_from_patterns`.
3. Check `crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs` for where hotspot pixel positions are computed — does the recompute run when `cell_width` changes?
4. Decide: invalidate on resize (cheap, slight stutter), or recompute pixel positions every frame from cell-space coords (slightly more work but always correct).

## Severity

P2. Cosmetic, not a functional break. Transient — it appears during resize and may self-correct on the next full damage cycle. Only shows under Windows + font-size ping-pong, which is more common than you'd think because the user dropped the font from 17 → 14 in one burst during startup.

## References

- Screenshot `Screenshot 2026-04-11 015242.png` from user, showing MCP tool call hotspot underlines extending past the right edge
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs` — hotspot detection and rendering
- `crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs` — cell-to-pixel conversion
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Hotspot underlines (MCP tool calls, file paths, URLs) extend past the right edge of the content area during/after window/font-size resize. Reproduced on Windows native + WSL pane after user dropped font size from 17 → 14 in one burst. Cosmetic but ugly.

## Root cause (already diagnosed)
`GridRenderer::clear_render_caches()` clears `pane_row_cache` and `pane_last_column_count`, **but does NOT clear `hotspot_map`**. The hotspot_map is keyed `(PaneId, row, col)` and stores per-cell underline info computed against the pre-resize grid (e.g. 205 cols). After resize to 151 cols, those stale entries persist and draw at pre-resize pixel positions, extending past the content-area right edge.

## Task
Invalidate `hotspot_map` (and any other hotspot state keyed on stale column counts) on grid resize, so post-resize frames compute hotspot positions from current grid metrics.

## Key Files
- `crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs:454` — `hotspot_map: HashMap<(PaneId, usize, usize), HotspotInfo>` storage
- `crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs:791-798` — `clear_render_caches()` (missing `hotspot_map` clear)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs:847-866` — `resize()` path that calls `clear_render_caches`
- `crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs:802-817` — `pane_cache_matches_viewport()` (column count check)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs:1410-1478` — `process_hotspots` underline drawing (pixel x = `cell.col * cell_width` at line 1461)
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/hotspot_detection.rs:44-60` — `TextHotspot` stores cell-space (col, row)

## Implementation
1. In `clear_render_caches()`, also clear `self.hotspot_map` (or drain it — preserve the allocation). Simplest correct fix.
2. Alternatively (preferred if the row cache is already invalidated per-pane on column change): add a per-pane hotspot_map invalidation path tied to the `pane_cache_matches_viewport` column-count-change branch, so resizing one pane doesn't wipe hotspots from unrelated panes.
3. Verify the fix with the reproduction: open a pane with visible hotspots (e.g. `ls` a directory with long file paths), `Ctrl+-`/`Ctrl+=` several times, confirm no underlines extend past the content right edge.
4. No new test required — this is a rendering-layer visual bug that's hard to unit-test cleanly. A manual verification note in the commit is sufficient.

## When Done
Run `./scripts/ci.sh`, then: \`bd close tn-yxiz --reason "hotspot_map now invalidated on resize — underlines stay within content area\"\`
## Retro
- What worked: Root cause was precisely diagnosed in the task brief — fix was a one-line addition (`self.hotspot_map.clear()` in `clear_render_caches`). Expanded the doc comment on `clear_render_caches` to explain *why* hotspot_map must be cleared (damage-aware update + column-count change = stale entries past new right edge), and noted that `hyperlink_map` is already handled in `clear_frame_maps` so no parallel fix needed there.
- Considered option 2 (per-pane invalidation on column-count-change branch in `pane_cache_matches_viewport`) but option 1 is strictly simpler and correct: window resize typically affects every pane anyway, and font-size resize definitely does. The extra cost of dropping 2-3 panes worth of hotspot entries on resize is negligible compared to the row-cache rebuild already happening.
- CI green (fmt / clippy / build / test all passing including 7 integration scenarios).
close_reason: hotspot_map now invalidated on resize — underlines stay within content area

title: Stale docs: therminal-app CLAUDE.md module structure, daemon tool count, cli.md commands
description:
Multiple doc staleness issues found in code review:
1. therminal-app/CLAUDE.md lists monolithic chrome.rs but it's been split into chrome/ subdirectory
2. therminal-app/CLAUDE.md lists pane/layout.rs but it's now pane/layout/ subdirectory
3. therminal-app/CLAUDE.md missing 10+ new files (git_state.rs, settings_overlay.rs, trust_escalation_overlay.rs, render_driver.rs, init.rs, event_handler.rs, etc.)
4. therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md claims 27 tools, actual is 30 (missing capture_result, events.stats, patterns.stats, agents.get_session_detail)
5. therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md domains table lists 5 domains, code has 7 (missing terminal.events, terminal.patterns)

7. docs/cli.md decision table missing terminal.panes.capture_result
