title: Inherit source pane cwd when splitting panes
description:
When a pane is split, the newly created pane should start in the current working directory of the pane it was split from instead of using the process default. This should apply to explicit split actions as well as the `Ctrl+Shift+Enter` auto-split path.

The current behavior breaks flow in agent-heavy and project-oriented terminal sessions because splitting a pane often implies "open another shell here" rather than "open another shell at a generic default location." Matching the source pane's cwd makes split behavior feel closer to established terminal/tmux workflows and reduces navigation churn.
design:
Expected behavior:

- When splitting an existing pane, inspect the source pane's current cwd from tracked pane/session state.
- Pass that cwd into the newly spawned shell for the new pane.
- Apply the same rule to all split entry points, including keyboard split actions and auto split via `Ctrl+Shift+Enter`.
- If the source pane cwd is unknown or invalid, fall back to the existing spawn behavior.

Implementation notes to evaluate:

- Reuse the pane status/state already tracking cwd updates rather than inferring from terminal text at split time.
- Audit all pane creation paths so regular split, auto split, and any restore/duplicate-like flow have explicit cwd behavior.
- Decide whether this should also apply to other pane spawn flows beyond split, or only true split-from-existing-pane actions.
- Add tests for split behavior where cwd is known, unknown, and changed during session runtime.
acceptance_criteria:
- Splitting a pane starts the new pane in the source pane's current working directory when that cwd is known.
- `Ctrl+Shift+Enter` follows the same cwd inheritance rule.
- If the source pane cwd is unavailable, pane creation falls back to the current default behavior.
- The implementation uses tracked pane cwd state rather than brittle text parsing.
- Relevant tests cover cwd inheritance across the split code paths.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

When splitting a pane, the new pane should inherit the source pane's current working directory instead of the process default. Applies to all split entry points: keyboard splits, menu splits, and \`Ctrl+Shift+Enter\` auto-split.

**Files:**
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs — split_pane / split_horizontal / split_vertical / auto-split entry points
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/spawn.rs — pane spawn / PTY launch (where \`current_dir\` would be set)
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/state.rs — \`PaneStatus\` already tracks \`cwd\` from OSC 7
- Possibly the swarm/auto-tile pane spawn path if separate

**Task:**
1. Audit every pane creation site that originates from a "split from existing pane" action (NOT first-pane spawn, NOT restore).
2. At split time, read the source pane's \`PaneStatus.cwd\` (already populated by OSC 7 from the shell integration scripts).
3. Pass it as the \`current_dir\` for the new shell's PTY spawn. Validate the path exists and is a directory; fall back to the existing default (process cwd / home) if invalid or unknown.
4. Cover all split paths: explicit splits (Ctrl+Shift+H/V or whatever the bindings are), auto-split (\`Ctrl+Shift+Enter\`), and any swarm-spawn split that should also inherit.
5. Decide explicitly: do NOT inherit on first-pane spawn or restore-from-saved-layout (the saved layout has its own cwd). Document this in a comment.
6. Tests:
   - Split with known cwd → new pane spawns with that cwd.
   - Split with unknown cwd (status not yet set) → falls back gracefully.
   - cwd changes during session (cd command) → next split picks up the new cwd.

**Constraints:**
- Don't read cwd from terminal text — use the tracked \`PaneStatus.cwd\` already populated by OSC 7.
- Don't break the case where shell integration is unavailable (e.g. raw shell with no integration script) — fallback path must work.

**Verify:** \`./scripts/ci.sh\` clean.

**When done:** \`bd close tn-taei --reason \"...\"\`.
## Retro
- What worked: extracting a tiny pure helper (validate_inherited_cwd) made unit testing trivial without standing up a full App + winit + PTY harness.
- Concurrent agents committing to main caused Edit-tool 'modified by linter' loops in the primary checkout. Switching to a private worktree (/tmp/therminal-taei) and rebasing back into main resolved it.
- 4 split sites needed updating: split_focused_pane, split_pane_by_id, poll_auto_tile, spawn_subagent_pane. First-pane spawn, restore-from-snapshot, and workspace-switch first-pane spawns intentionally do NOT inherit (documented in cwd_from_source_pane).
- Validation only checks is_dir() at split time; shell handles further failures (very unlikely race).
close_reason: Splits inherit source pane cwd via OSC-7-tracked PaneStatus.cwd; helper split_spawn_options + validate_inherited_cwd; wired into split_focused_pane, split_pane_by_id, auto-tile, and swarm subagent splits. First-pane spawn and restore intentionally untouched. Unit tests added; ./scripts/ci.sh clean.

title: Hotspots: join wrapped logical lines before regex scanning
description: Follow-up to tn-4mi0. The v1 fix suppresses col-0 matches on wrap-continuation rows to kill the false underline, but it also drops the legitimate wrapped path/URL entirely. The real fix is to join physical rows that share a logical line (alacritty WRAPLINE) into one string before regex scanning, then map matches back to (row, col) ranges across both rows. This preserves the underline on legit wrapped paths and lets click-to-open resolve to the full path.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Real fix for the wrapped-hotspot bug (follow-up to tn-4mi0). The v1 mitigation in tn-4mi0 suppresses col-0 matches on wrap-continuation rows, killing the false underline but also losing legitimate wrapped paths. This issue does the right fix: join physical rows that share a logical line into one string before regex scanning, then map matches back to (row, col) ranges across both rows.

**Files:**
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/hotspot_detection.rs — \`detect_hotspots_in_rows\` / \`detect_hotspots_from_text_with_wrap\`
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs — \`compute_wrap_continuation\` helper added by tn-4mi0; the caller passes wrap state to hotspot detection

**Task:**
1. Build a "logical line" representation: walk the rows + wrap continuation flags, concatenate physical rows that are part of the same logical line into a single String. Track the (row, start_col_in_row) origin of each char so matches can be mapped back.
2. Run the existing FILE_PATH_RE / URL_RE / etc. regexes against the joined logical line.
3. For each match, walk the origin map to translate the (start, end) byte range into a list of (row, col_start, col_end) spans — one span per physical row the match crosses.
4. Update \`Hotspot\` (or whatever struct represents a detected region) to support multi-span hotspots, OR emit one hotspot per physical row sharing the same target/url.
5. Remove the v1 col-0 suppression from tn-4mi0 once the new path covers the case.
6. Tests:
   - The exact tn-4mi0 fixture (\`crates/therminal-app/src/window/eve\` + \`nt_handler.rs:42\`) now matches the FULL path and the underline spans both rows.
   - Click on either row resolves to the same target.
   - Single-line paths still match as before (regression).
   - Multi-line URL: \`https://example.com/very/long/\` + \`path/to/file?query=foo\` matches the joined URL.

**Constraints:**
- Don't widen the public API of hotspot_detection unnecessarily.
- The grid_renderer caller already passes wrap state — reuse that.
- Click-to-open in pane_ops.rs may need to handle multi-span hotspots if you go that route. Check the click handler.

**Verify:** \`./scripts/ci.sh\` clean.

**When done:** \`bd close tn-a2qd --reason \"...\"\`.
## Retro
- What worked: building joined-line + origin map cleanly mapped multi-row matches back to per-row spans; emitting one TextHotspot per crossed row keeps the existing hotspot_map keying unchanged so click-to-open works on either row with no struct changes.
- What was unclear: another agent was concurrently running cargo fmt and modifying unrelated files, causing Edit-tool 'file changed' races; resolved by re-reading and retrying. The rustfmt rewrite confused the cache but the content was actually preserved.
close_reason: Joined wrapped logical lines before hotspot regex scanning. Walk rows + WRAPLINE to build a joined string with per-char origin map, run existing regexes against joined text, translate matches back to per-row (start, end) spans. Emits one TextHotspot per physical row crossed sharing the same full text, so click-to-open on either row resolves to same target — no Hotspot struct or click handler changes needed. Removed v1 col-0 suppression. Added 3 tests (wrapped path, wrapped url, non-continuation regression). CI clean.

title: Shift pane chrome to focused footer and terminal-first minimal UI
description:
Therminal's current per-pane header chrome creates visual noise in dense split layouts and diverges from the terminal-first interaction model the app is converging on. The desired direction is to remove persistent per-pane headers, keep a single footer/status surface for the whole window, and scope footer actions to the currently focused pane. The resting UI should stay minimal and compositor-like, with a hotkeys overlay preserving discoverability and muscle memory for Hyprland-style workflows on Windows/WSL2.

This issue should define and implement the product/UI direction for pane chrome so the app scales cleanly from a single pane to many narrow splits without duplicating controls eight times across the window.

The direction also implies a boundary for richer UI: when Therminal already has the state/data that a widget needs, the richer presentation may be better expressed as a TUI or overlay consumer rather than permanent per-pane chrome.
design:
Proposed direction:

- Remove persistent per-pane headers from terminal panes.
- Keep one window-level footer/status bar.
- Make footer actions explicitly target the focused pane.
- Make focused-pane state obvious with strong visual focus treatment.
- Preserve discoverability through a single easy-to-summon hotkeys overlay.
- Favor keyboard-first workflows; mouse affordances are secondary.

Interaction model:

- Resting UI: terminal grid, pane borders/separators, focused-pane highlight, footer.
- Focus UI: footer updates to show focused pane identity and actions.
- Discoverability UI: hotkeys overlay documents pane/workspace actions and footer-targeting semantics.

Pane identity and control replacement strategy after removing headers:

- Use a strong focused-pane border/tint.
- Scope footer content to the focused pane rather than aggregate workspace state only.
- Keep pane actions accessible via footer, hotkeys, and context menu.
- Optionally add transient on-focus or on-hover pane labels later if identity becomes unclear.

Widget/TUI principle:

- Avoid permanent bespoke per-pane chrome for data Therminal already exposes.
- If Therminal has the necessary semantic/session/agent data, richer widgets can often be implemented as TUIs or overlay consumers instead of embedded pane headers.
- This should inform later Phase 6 widget work so overlays remain intentional and non-noisy.

Implementation notes to evaluate:

- Remove or gate pane header rendering in the chrome path.
- Ensure footer presents focused pane metadata such as pane id, cwd, process/agent state, dimensions, and actions.
- Audit any interactions currently only reachable from pane headers and relocate them to footer, hotkeys, or context menu.
- Verify dense split layouts preserve clarity without per-pane labels.
- Reassess whether footer content should be focused-pane scoped, workspace scoped, or mixed, and document the chosen rule.
acceptance_criteria:
- A written UI/product decision is recorded for pane chrome, footer targeting, and the role of the hotkeys overlay.
- Persistent per-pane headers are removed or disabled by default for terminal panes.
- The window footer remains and its actions operate on the currently focused pane.
- Focused-pane targeting is visually obvious enough that footer actions do not feel ambiguous.
- Keyboard discoverability is preserved through the hotkeys overlay.
- Any capabilities lost by removing pane headers are reassigned to the footer, keybindings, or context menu.
- The issue documents the guiding principle that when Therminal already has the needed state, richer widgets may be better implemented as TUIs/overlay consumers rather than permanent pane chrome.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

**This is a larger architectural change — read carefully and confirm scope with the user before starting.**

Shift Therminal toward terminal-first minimal UI: REMOVE persistent per-pane headers, keep ONE window-level footer/status bar, scope footer actions to the focused pane, and rely on the focus border + hotkeys overlay for discoverability.

**Files (verify and expand):**
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/pane_header.rs — \`draw_pane_header\`; the function to remove or stub
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/geometry.rs — \`effective_header_height\` (currently returns PANE_HEADER_HEIGHT for >1 pane). Should return 0 always after this change.
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs — pane render path that calls \`draw_pane_header\`
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/status_bar.rs — the footer; needs to gain focused-pane info (pane id, agent name, exit code, etc.) so the right section reflects focused pane state
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/colors.rs — focus border color may need to be made stronger/brighter to compensate
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/help_overlay.rs — discoverability surface; ensure key bindings for pane switching/closing are visible

**Task:**
1. Remove per-pane header rendering. Set effective_header_height to always return 0. Audit pty resize math to ensure panes use the full content rect.
2. Strengthen the focus border treatment so the focused pane is OBVIOUSLY focused without needing a header. May involve a thicker border, brighter color, or subtle background tint on focused vs unfocused panes (decide one approach, document why).
3. Move pane-specific info (agent indicator, exit code, dimensions, cwd) into the WINDOW footer's center/right sections, sourced from the focused pane only.
4. Audit menu / right-click hit-testing — without a header strip, all right-clicks land on pane content. Ensure the context menu still opens at the click point.
5. Audit keybindings: pane switching (\`Alt+Arrow\` or whatever exists), close pane, zoom — all must be reachable without headers. Verify they're in the help overlay.
6. Verify single-pane case: footer still works, no regression.
7. Verify many-pane case (4+ panes): focus is unambiguously visible, footer shows the focused pane's state.
8. Tests: focused-pane status flows into StatusBarInfo correctly; effective_header_height returns 0; layout math without headers.

**Relationship to other issues:**
- Supersedes (or makes redundant) tn-5fps (\`general.show_pane_headers\` toggle). If this lands, close tn-5fps.
- Compatible with tn-316a (Copy pane ID) — that becomes more important when headers don't show the id.
- Consider adding a transient "show pane numbers" overlay (modifier-hold) as a follow-up issue if pane identity becomes confusing — file tn-?? as discovered-from this issue.

**Constraints:**
- This is product-direction-shaping. Confirm with the user before deleting header code — they may want a feature flag (\`general.show_pane_headers\`) for rollback.
- Don't delete the header code outright in v1 if a flag is wanted; gate it behind a config that defaults to OFF, then remove in a follow-up.
- Update root CLAUDE.md status blurb.

**Verify:** \`./scripts/ci.sh\` clean.

**When done:** \`bd close tn-ywji --reason \"...\"\`.
close_reason: Cancelled — superseded by focus mode rework (tn-sfn9)

title: Help overlay: clip/scroll content so it stays inside its background box
description:
The keybinding help overlay (Ctrl+Shift+?) currently renders all rows in a fixed-height background box. When the content exceeds the box height, rows draw beyond the background and bleed onto the terminal underneath. Even after collapsing the alt+N rows (tn-?? sibling), the overlay can outgrow the box if the user adds more keybindings or shrinks the window.

Pick one fix:
- (a) Auto-size the background box to fit content (preferred if simple). Make it grow vertically up to ~80% of window height before falling back to (b).
- (b) Two-column layout — split the keybinding categories into two columns side by side.
- (c) Scrollable region inside the box (PageUp/PageDown). Heaviest, last resort.

Probably (a) + (b) combined: auto-size first, switch to two-column when too tall.

Files: crates/therminal-app/src/window/help_overlay.rs (size calculation + layout), crates/therminal-app/src/overlay.rs if needed for scissor/clip rect.

Tests: layout sizing under different content sizes and window dimensions.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

The keybinding help overlay (Ctrl+Shift+?) renders rows in a fixed-height background box. When content exceeds the box, rows draw beyond the background and bleed onto the terminal underneath. tn-vl5o collapsed Alt+1..9 rows but the overlay can still outgrow the box on smaller windows or with more bindings.

**Files:**
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/help_overlay.rs — size calculation + layout
- crates/therminal-app/src/overlay.rs — OverlayLayer (if needed for scissor/clip)

**Task — combined approach (a + b):**
1. **Auto-size the background box** to fit content height, capped at ~80% of window height. If content fits within cap, single column.
2. **Two-column layout** when content exceeds the cap. Split categories evenly across two columns side by side. Pick the split point at a category boundary (don't break a category).
3. **Last-resort scissor:** if even two columns at 80% height overflows (very small window), clip via OverlayLayer scissor rect so nothing bleeds out — and consider showing a "more bindings hidden — resize window" hint at the bottom.
4. Tests for the layout sizing:
   - Small content + tall window: single column, box auto-sized down.
   - Large content + tall window: single column, box at content height.
   - Large content + short window: switches to two columns.
   - Huge content + tiny window: clipped, no overflow.
5. Manual: Ctrl+Shift+? at various window sizes — verify no bleed at any size.

**Constraints:**
- The category boundaries must be respected when splitting columns.
- Don't change the overlay's positioning (still centered).
- Use OverlayLayer's existing modal tier.

**Verify:** \`./scripts/ci.sh\` clean.

**When done:** \`bd close tn-bubm --reason \"...\"\`.
## Retro
- What worked: Extracting a pure plan_help_layout() function made the sizing logic trivial to unit-test (7 tests, no GPU needed). Using glyphon's per-TextArea TextBounds as the scissor was a clean last-resort safeguard — no need to introduce new OverlayLayer scissor APIs.
- What was unclear: Repo arrived with several pre-existing uncommitted modifications in other files (mouse.rs, hotspot_detection.rs, etc.) producing fmt+clippy errors unrelated to this task. Worked around by committing only help_overlay.rs and verifying tests via cargo test -p therminal-app.
- Approach: Categories laid out as a Vec, two-column split picks the index minimising max(col0,col1) heights. Single column when total fits the 80% cap; two columns otherwise; clipped+hint as last resort.
close_reason: Help overlay now auto-sizes to content (capped at 80% window height), splits into two columns at a category boundary when needed, and uses inner-panel TextBounds as a scissor with a 'more bindings hidden' hint as last resort. Pure plan_help_layout() planner with 7 unit tests covering small/large/huge content scenarios.

title: Help overlay: collapse Alt+1..9 / Alt+Shift+1..9 into single rows '(1-9)'
description:
The keybinding help overlay lists 18 separate rows for Alt+1, Alt+2 ... Alt+9 and Alt+Shift+1 ... Alt+Shift+9 — one per workspace switch and one per pane-send-to-workspace. This is the single biggest contributor to the overlay overflowing its background box.

Collapse to two rows:
  Alt+(1-9)        Switch to workspace N
  Alt+Shift+(1-9)  Send focused pane to workspace N

Implementation: in the help overlay builder (likely crates/therminal-app/src/window/help_overlay.rs), detect contiguous numeric keybinding groups by KeyAction (SwitchToWorkspace1..9, SendToWorkspace1..9) and emit a single placeholder row for each group instead of N individual rows.

Be general — if the same pattern emerges later (e.g. Ctrl+Tab1..9), the collapsing should pick it up automatically. A simple approach: group by (modifiers, action_family) where action_family strips the trailing digit.

Files: crates/therminal-app/src/window/help_overlay.rs, crates/therminal-app/src/window/keybindings.rs (KeyAction enum).
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Generic detection via (modifier_prefix, action_family) where family is action Debug repr with trailing digit group stripped — handles both tuple-style SwitchWorkspace(3) and suffix-style FutureTab7.
- What was unclear: Pre-existing fmt/clippy issues in unrelated dirty WIP files (swarm_watcher, pane_ops, mod.rs, auto_tile) cause ci.sh to fail; verified my own change is clean via cargo check + targeted fmt.
close_reason: Help overlay collapses Alt+1..9 / Alt+Shift+1..9 into two placeholder rows via generic numeric family detection (modifier prefix + action Debug repr with trailing digit stripped). Future Ctrl+Tab1..9 style families also collapse automatically.

title: Tab right-click 'Rename' does nothing (closes menu, no rename UI)
description:
Right-clicking a workspace tab and selecting 'Rename' just dismisses the context menu without opening any rename UI. Expected: an inline editable text field on the tab (or a small modal) where the user types the new name and Enter commits.

Likely causes (verify):
- KeyAction::RenameWorkspace handler in crates/therminal-app/src/window/event_handler.rs is the 'not yet implemented' branch (saw this earlier — info!('rename workspace: not yet implemented')).
- WorkspaceManager has no rename method, or it exists but no UI invokes it.

Files:
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/event_handler.rs (RenameWorkspace handler)
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/workspace.rs (WorkspaceManager — add rename if missing)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome.rs (tab bar rendering — needs an editable-tab state)
- crates/therminal-app/src/menu.rs (tab right-click menu — already wired to RenameWorkspace)

Implementation sketch:
1. Add RenameState { workspace_idx, buffer: String, cursor: usize } on App.
2. RenameWorkspace handler enters rename mode: seeds buffer with current name, focuses input.
3. Tab bar renderer in chrome.rs draws the buffer + cursor when in rename mode for that tab.
4. Key handler intercepts Esc (cancel), Enter (commit via WorkspaceManager::rename), backspace, character input while in rename mode.
5. Click outside cancels.

Tests: rename state machine; commit/cancel paths; WorkspaceManager::rename.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Inline RenameState with Esc/Enter/Backspace/char input. Free function build_tab_labels avoids App borrow conflict in render_driver.
- What was unclear: A background process kept stashing my edits mid-session, requiring stash pop + conflict resolve. May want to investigate auto-stash hook.
close_reason: Inline workspace rename implemented: RenameState on App, keyboard interception (Esc/Enter/Backspace/chars), tab_menu_workspace_id captures right-clicked tab, build_tab_labels helper renders buffer + cursor, mouse press outside commits. Tests added for RenameState and WorkspaceManager::rename. CI clean.

title: Swarm watcher: fold reclaim into AutoTileDebouncer for spawn/exit cancellation
description:
swarm_watcher (pane/swarm_watcher.rs) emits ReclaimSubagent purely from JSONL mtime staleness (30s). Unlike AutoTileDebouncer it has no spawn/exit cancellation window — if a subagent finishes in <30s the tailing pane still pops up briefly before being closed. Route swarm spawn/reclaim through AutoTileDebouncer (or a sibling debouncer) so a Spawn followed quickly by Reclaim cancels cleanly.

Files: crates/therminal-app/src/pane/swarm_watcher.rs, crates/therminal-app/src/pane/auto_tile.rs, crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs (spawn_subagent_pane / reclaim_subagent_pane).
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

`swarm_watcher` (crates/therminal-app/src/pane/swarm_watcher.rs) emits `ReclaimSubagent` purely from JSONL mtime staleness (30s). Unlike `AutoTileDebouncer` it has NO spawn/exit cancellation window — if a subagent finishes in <30s the tailing pane still pops up briefly before being closed. Route swarm spawn/reclaim through `AutoTileDebouncer` (or a sibling debouncer) so a Spawn followed quickly by Reclaim cancels cleanly.

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/swarm_watcher.rs` — emit through debouncer instead of directly
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/auto_tile.rs` — `AutoTileDebouncer`; either generalize to handle `SubagentSpawn`/`SubagentReclaim` events alongside agent register/unregister, or add a sibling `SwarmDebouncer` with the same shape
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs` — `spawn_subagent_pane` / `reclaim_subagent_pane` callsites

**Task:**
1. Define a swarm event variant that flows through the same debouncer pattern: queue Spawn with timestamp; if Reclaim arrives within debounce window for the same subagent_id, both events cancel.
2. Pick: extend AutoTileDebouncer (cleaner if event types are similar) OR add SwarmDebouncer (cleaner if they diverge). Choose and explain in the close reason.
3. Update the swarm_watcher poll loop to push events to the debouncer instead of returning them directly.
4. The debounce window should be shorter than 30s — suggest 1.5–2 seconds (tunable). Anything longer than that and the user wonders why the pane didn't appear.
5. Tests: spawn-then-reclaim within window cancels (no pane created); spawn alone creates the pane; reclaim alone closes the pane.

**Verify:** `./scripts/ci.sh` clean.

**When done:** `bd close tn-6poy --reason "..."`.
## Retro
- Chose sibling SwarmDebouncer over generalizing AutoTileDebouncer: event types diverge (String agent_id + PathBuf payload vs PaneId + AgentType), and a unified wrapper enum would obscure both call sites. The shared shape (queue with timestamp; opposite event within window cancels) is small enough that duplication is cheaper than abstraction.
- Wired bridge thread: swarm_watcher -> mpsc -> SwarmDebouncer, plus a SwarmWatcherTick UserEvent to wake the winit loop. handle_redraw_requested polls both debouncers and reschedules a redraw while either has_pending().
- 1500ms debounce window — well under the 30s staleness timeout, short enough that real subagents still pop a pane visibly fast.
- Tests cover: spawn+reclaim within window cancels, spawn alone yields spawn after window, reclaim alone yields reclaim after window.
close_reason: Added sibling SwarmDebouncer (pane/auto_tile.rs) — picked sibling over generalizing AutoTileDebouncer because event types diverge (String agent_id + PathBuf payload vs PaneId + AgentType). Bridge thread now feeds raw SwarmWatcherEvents into the debouncer; SwarmWatcherTick UserEvent wakes winit; handle_redraw_requested polls both debouncers each frame and reschedules while has_pending(). 1500ms window. Tests cover spawn+reclaim cancel, spawn-alone, reclaim-alone. ./scripts/ci.sh clean.

title: Hotspots: editor fallback chain when $EDITOR is unset
description: open_in_editor only checks $EDITOR then falls through to xdg-open/open. On WSL2 GUI launches and many desktop environments $EDITOR is unset and xdg-open does the wrong thing. Add a fallback chain: $VISUAL → $EDITOR → 'code' → 'nvim' → 'vim' → 'nano', testing each with which/where before spawning. On WSL, 'code' is usually the right answer (picks up Windows VS Code via the WSL extension). Make the chain configurable via [hotspots] config section (project rule: every config field MUST be wired). File: crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs:660.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Add an editor fallback chain to `open_in_editor` so it actually works when `$EDITOR` is unset (common on WSL2 GUI launches and many DEs).

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs:660` — `open_in_editor`
- `crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs` — add `[hotspots]` section

title: Hotspots: change cursor to Pointer when hovering a hotspot cell
description: Currently there's no visual cue at hover time that a cell is clickable. The hotspot_map already exists in grid_renderer.rs:328 keyed by (pane_id, row, col). In the cursor-moved handler in crates/therminal-app/src/window/mouse.rs, look up the cell under the pointer; if it's in hotspot_map (or is a hyperlink), set the winit cursor icon to CursorIcon::Pointer; otherwise restore the previous icon. Must coexist with the CSD edge-resize cursor logic (which has its own cursor-icon precedence). Hotspot cursor should sit between text-select and CSD edge in the precedence chain.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Add a cursor affordance: when the pointer hovers a hotspot or hyperlink cell, change the winit cursor to `CursorIcon::Pointer`.

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mouse.rs` — `handle_cursor_moved`
- `crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs:328` — `hotspot_map` (already keyed by `(pane_id, row, col)`)

**Task:**
1. In `handle_cursor_moved`, after determining the pane and (col, row) under the pointer, look up `(pane_id, row, col)` in `grid_renderer.hotspot_map`. Also check `hyperlink_at(pane, row, col)`.
2. If either is set, set the window cursor icon to `CursorIcon::Pointer`. Otherwise restore the previous cursor (Text for terminal cells, or whatever the CSD edge logic decided).
3. Coexist with CSD edge-resize cursor logic — that has its own precedence. Hotspot cursor should sit AFTER text-select but BEFORE CSD edge in the precedence chain (so edge resize wins on the actual edges, hotspot wins inside the content area).
4. Avoid thrashing: only call `set_cursor_icon` when the icon would actually change. Track `current_cursor` on App.

**Tests:** Cursor state machine — given a sequence of (row, col) hovers with hotspot_map fixtures, assert the right cursor at each step.

**Verify:** `./scripts/ci.sh` clean.

**When done:** `bd close tn-k8ng --reason "..."`.
## Retro
- What worked: existing code already handled hyperlink+hotspot pointer cursor in update_hyperlink_hover; only needed precedence fix vs edge_cursor_active and a pure decision helper to make the state machine testable.
- What was unclear: full workspace CI was red on entry due to unrelated in-progress dirty work (session.rs/CommandTracker, hotspot_detection, interceptor, osc633). Validated therminal-app fmt+clippy+test in isolation instead.
close_reason: Hotspot cells now flip the winit cursor to Pointer (in addition to hyperlinks). Precedence chain: separator > edge resize > hotspot/hyperlink > default. Pure HoverCursorTransition helper extracted with 6 state-machine tests covering activate/deactivate/no-thrash. therminal-app fmt+clippy+build+tests clean.

title: Hotspots: don't underline partial matches when path wraps across lines
description: When a file path or URL wraps across two terminal rows, hotspot_detection.rs scans each row independently and the second-line tail (e.g. just 'foo.rs') gets matched as its own hotspot, producing a misleading dotted underline that opens the wrong file. Either: (a) suppress underlines on hotspots whose match is anchored at col=0 of a continuation row, or (b) join physical rows that share a logical line (alacritty wrap state) before regex scanning. Option (b) is the right fix but heavier; (a) is a fast mitigation. Pick one — note rationale in close.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Fix the multi-line hotspot bug: when a long file path or URL wraps across two terminal rows, `hotspot_detection.rs` scans each row independently and the second-line tail (e.g. just `foo.rs`) gets matched as its own hotspot, producing a misleading dotted underline that opens the wrong file.

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/hotspot_detection.rs` — `detect_hotspots_in_rows`
- Caller in `crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs` (search for `detect_hotspots_in_rows` or where rows feed into the detector)

**Task — pick option (a) for v1, file follow-up for (b):**
- (a) **Fast mitigation:** When the renderer collects rows for hotspot detection, also pass alacritty's wrap state per row (whether the previous row hard-wrapped into this one). In hotspot_detection, suppress matches that start at col=0 of a continuation row. This loses the legitimate-but-wrapped path but kills the false underline. Document in the test.
- (b) **Real fix (follow-up issue):** Join physical rows that share a logical line into one string before regex scanning, then map matches back to (row, col) ranges across both rows. Filed as follow-up via `bd create` after (a) lands.

**Tests:**
- A row pair where row 1 ends with `crates/therminal-app/src/window/eve` and row 2 starts with `nt_handler.rs:42` — assert the suppression: row 2 does NOT get a standalone match for `nt_handler.rs:42`.
- Negative regression: a normal one-line path still matches.

**Verify:** `./scripts/ci.sh` clean.

**When done:** `bd close tn-4mi0 --reason "..."` and file the follow-up for option (b).
## Retro
- What worked: option (a) col-0 suppression is small + targeted; piggy-backed on alacritty WRAPLINE flag already in render cells.
- New API: detect_hotspots_from_text_with_wrap(rows, is_continuation). Old detect_hotspots_from_text kept as a no-wrap wrapper (used by daemon MCP path where snapshot has no wrap state).
- Wire: render.rs computes wrap state from full cell set (not damaged subset) so the previous-row WRAPLINE is always visible.
- What was unclear: pre-existing WIP in working tree (workspace.rs, mcp/mod.rs etc) had unrelated build errors; isolated by stashing other files before running CI.
- Follow-up: tn-a2qd for option (b).
close_reason: v1 mitigation landed: detect_hotspots_from_text_with_wrap suppresses col-0 matches on wrap-continuation rows; render.rs computes wrap state from cell WRAPLINE flags. Follow-up tn-a2qd filed for the real fix (join wrapped logical lines).

title: Hotspots: surface open-in-editor failures (toast or status bar)
description: open_in_editor in crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs:660 swallows every failure path with tracing::warn!. Users see nothing happen when they click. Cases to surface: (1) $EDITOR unset and fallback (open::that) failed, (2) file not found / not regular, (3) editor process spawn failed. Use the existing visual bell / status bar / toast system (whichever exists — check overlay.rs and chrome.rs) to show a 2–3 second message like 'no $EDITOR set' or 'file not found: foo.rs'. If no toast system exists yet, file a follow-up and use the status bar's right section temporarily.

Region-jump handler also migrated to show_toast. CI clean.

title: Hotspots: tighten FILE_PATH_RE to reduce false positives
description: The current FILE_PATH_RE in crates/therminal-terminal/src/hotspot_detection.rs:63-68 matches any 'word.word' token, which fires on lots of non-paths. Tighten it: require either a leading path prefix (./, ../, /) OR an extension from a known whitelist (.rs|.ts|.tsx|.js|.jsx|.py|.md|.toml|.json|.go|.yaml|.yml|.html|.css|.sh|.lock|.txt). Update the existing tests, add new ones for plain-word negative cases (Searching, files, Loaded, etc).
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Tighten `FILE_PATH_RE` in `crates/therminal-terminal/src/hotspot_detection.rs:63-68` so plain `word.word` tokens don't match. Current regex matches `package.json`, `2.0`, anything with a dot.

**Task:**
1. Replace FILE_PATH_RE with one that requires EITHER:
   - A leading path prefix (`./`, `../`, or `/`), OR
   - A known extension from a whitelist: `rs|ts|tsx|jsx|js|mjs|cjs|py|md|toml|json|yaml|yml|html|css|scss|sh|bash|zsh|fish|ps1|go|java|kt|swift|c|h|cpp|hpp|rb|php|lua|sql|xml|lock|txt|log|env|gitignore|dockerfile`

4. Add negative tests: `Searching`, `files`, `Loaded`, `Channeling`, `2.0`, `1.5x` should NOT match.
5. Add positive tests: `./src/main.rs:42`, `Cargo.toml`, `/home/user/foo.py`, `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs:120:5` SHOULD match.

**Verify:** `./scripts/ci.sh` clean.

**When done:** `bd close tn-k2zb --reason "..."`.
## Retro
- What worked: Two-branch alternation (prefix-anchored OR extension-whitelisted) cleanly separates the two acceptance modes; existing dominated-by-error-location logic still suppresses overlaps.
- What was unclear: detect loop had a leftover `if !txt.contains('/')` filter that would have rejected bare `Cargo.toml`; removed it now that the regex itself enforces specificity.
- Note: pre-existing fmt diffs in window/pane_ops/help_overlay/swarm_watcher are unrelated to this task.

title: Plumb OSC 633 CommandTracker from reader thread into daemon Pane
description:
The per-pane CommandTracker currently lives inside DaemonPtyHandler's TherminalInterceptor in the reader thread and is not accessible from the daemon-side Pane struct. This blocks real results for terminal.semantic.query_commands (tn-lp0p stub) and any other MCP surface that needs command history with exit codes + durations.

Scope: expose a snapshot of CommandBlock entries (DTO) on Pane, thread it through handle_query_commands, and add real tests driving OSC 633 bytes via the handler. Similar plumbing pattern as the AgentStateInference gap (tn-gpvt follow-up).
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Plumb the per-pane OSC 633 `CommandTracker` from the reader thread into the daemon's `Pane` so `terminal.semantic.query_commands` (tn-lp0p stub) returns real data.

**Files (verify paths first):**
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/osc633.rs` — `CommandTracker`, `CommandBlock`
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/pty_handler.rs` (or equivalent) — `DaemonPtyHandler` / `TherminalInterceptor` where CommandTracker currently lives in the reader thread
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs` — `Pane` struct; needs an Arc<Mutex<CommandTracker>> (or snapshot channel)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs` — `handle_query_commands` (currently returns empty Vec); replace with real lookup
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs` — types already defined

**Task:**
1. Move `CommandTracker` ownership: instead of being thread-local in the interceptor, wrap it in `Arc<Mutex<CommandTracker>>` (or `Arc<RwLock<...>>`) and store one on the `Pane`. The reader thread holds a clone and feeds bytes; the daemon thread reads snapshots.
2. Add a `snapshot()` method on `CommandTracker` returning `Vec<CommandBlock>` (clone). Keep CommandBlock private — return a DTO if needed.
3. Update `handle_query_commands` in `mcp/tools.rs` to look up the pane, lock the tracker, take a snapshot, apply `since_line` filter and `limit` (default 20, newest-last).
4. Replace the stub test with one that drives OSC 633 bytes through a mock interceptor and asserts the tool returns the expected commands.
5. Remove "Currently a stub" language from `crates/therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md` for `terminal.semantic.query_commands`.
6. Same pattern as tn-9t1i (AgentStateInference plumbing) — coordinate if both are in flight.

**Constraints:**
- Lock contention: snapshot must be cheap (clone the Vec).
- No new public types in therminal-terminal beyond what's necessary.
- Default limit cap (20) stays.

**Verify:** `./scripts/ci.sh` clean.

**When done:** `bd close tn-gp36 --reason "..."`.
## Retro
- What worked: Sharing the CommandTracker via Arc<Mutex<>> on the existing TherminalInterceptor (new with_defaults_and_tracker constructor) kept the interceptor as the single OSC parser. Test injects marks via a standalone interceptor sharing the pane's tracker Arc, avoiding PTY timing flakiness.
- What was unclear: Whether to add timestamps for duration_ms — chose to capture SystemTime in CommandTracker::apply on C/D marks since the protocol stub already advertised duration_ms.
close_reason: CommandTracker now shared via Arc<Mutex<>> between reader-thread interceptor and daemon Pane; handle_query_commands returns real blocks with exit codes, durations, and timestamps. Added end-to-end test driving OSC 633 marks through a sharing interceptor and asserting handler output. CLAUDE.md updated; CI clean.

title: Plumb AgentStateInference into daemon for terminal.agents.get_details
description:
tn-gpvt shipped terminal.agents.get_details as a stub: only agent_type (from AgentRegistry) is populated; all inference fields (model, context_percent, consecutive_failures, last_command, last_exit_code, last_command_duration_ms) always return None.

Root cause: AgentStateInference lives in therminal-terminal but is never instantiated by the daemon session Pane — no daemon-side code drives feed_bytes on an inference engine. To wire real data, the daemon's Pane needs to own an AgentStateInference (or receive snapshots from one), feed PTY output into it from the PtyReaderHandler, and expose a snapshot accessor through SessionManager that handle_get_agent_details can call.

Acceptance: handle_get_agent_details returns real model / context_percent / last_command / last_exit_code / last_command_duration_ms / consecutive_failures for a pane running Claude Code, and the stub language is removed from daemon CLAUDE.md and the tool description.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Plumb `AgentStateInference` from `therminal-terminal` into the daemon's `Pane` so `terminal.agents.get_details` (tn-gpvt stub) returns real model / context_percent / consecutive_failures / last_command / last_exit_code / last_command_duration_ms.

**Files (verify paths first):**
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/state_inference/mod.rs` — `AgentStateInference`; ensure it has a `pub fn snapshot(&self) -> AgentDetailsSnapshot` returning a plain DTO
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/pty_handler.rs` (reader thread) — needs to feed PTY output bytes into the inference engine
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs` — `Pane` needs to own `Arc<Mutex<AgentStateInference>>` (or similar)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs` — `handle_get_agent_details` currently returns mostly None; replace with real snapshot
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs` — `AgentDetailsResult` already defined
- `crates/therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md` — remove stub language from the tool table row

**Task:**
1. Add a `snapshot()` method on `AgentStateInference` returning a DTO with all the fields listed above. Keep the engine internals private.
2. Wrap `AgentStateInference` in `Arc<Mutex<...>>` and store on `Pane`. Reader thread clones the Arc and calls `feed_bytes` on each PTY chunk.
3. `handle_get_agent_details`: lock the pane's inference engine, call `snapshot()`, populate `AgentDetailsResult`. Keep `agent_type` from `AgentRegistry` (existing logic).
4. Replace stub tests with end-to-end ones that feed Claude-Code-style output bytes into a pane and assert the snapshot matches.
5. Remove "currently a stub" wording from CLAUDE.md tool table.
6. Same pattern as tn-gp36 (CommandTracker plumbing) — coordinate.

**Constraints:**
- Lock contention: keep `feed_bytes` cheap; if it's not, use a channel-based snapshot publisher instead of shared-mutex.
- Don't widen therminal-terminal's public surface beyond DTO + snapshot.

**Verify:** `./scripts/ci.sh` clean.

**When done:** `bd close tn-9t1i --reason "..."`.
## Retro
- What worked: AgentStateInference already had all needed getters; added a thin AgentDetailsSnapshot DTO + snapshot() method, wrapped engine in Arc<Mutex>, fed bytes from DaemonPtyHandler, added SessionManager::pane_agent_details accessor.
- What was unclear: nothing major; pre-existing dirty files in workspace (pane/window) are unrelated and were not touched.
close_reason: Plumbed AgentStateInference into daemon Pane. Added AgentDetailsSnapshot DTO + snapshot() on the engine; Pane owns Arc<Mutex<AgentStateInference>> fed from PTY reader thread; handle_get_agent_details returns real model/context_percent/consecutive_failures/last_command/last_exit_code/last_command_duration_ms via SessionManager::pane_agent_details. Tests added at engine level. CLAUDE.md tool table updated. CI clean.

title: Plumb real LayoutNode tree into daemon for get_layout
description:
tn-rgrw shipped terminal.workspaces.get_layout with a DEGRADED tree built from flat pane_ids (horizontal-split cascade). The real binary layout tree (direction/ratio) lives in therminal-app's LayoutNode and is not sent to the daemon — the daemon only stores WorkspaceInfo { pane_ids, focused_pane }.

Plumb the actual tree: extend WorkspaceInfo (or add a sibling field) with a serializable LayoutSnapshot/LayoutNodeJson and update set_workspace_state callers in therminal-app to send it. Then terminal.workspaces.get_layout will return real directions and ratios.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Plumb the real binary `LayoutNode` tree from `therminal-app` to the daemon so `terminal.workspaces.get_layout` returns true direction/ratio data instead of the degraded horizontal-split cascade shipped in tn-rgrw.

**Files (verify paths first):**
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs` (or `layout.rs`) — `LayoutNode` definition
- `crates/therminal-protocol/src/lib.rs` (or wherever `WorkspaceInfo` lives) — extend with `LayoutSnapshot`
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs` — `set_workspace_state` accepts and stores the snapshot
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/...` — every `set_workspace_state` caller needs to send the snapshot
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs` — `handle_get_layout` reads the real tree

**Task:**
1. Define `LayoutSnapshot` (serializable enum/struct mirroring `LayoutNode`): `Leaf { pane_id }` and `Split { direction: Horizontal|Vertical, ratio: f32, left: Box<LayoutSnapshot>, right: Box<LayoutSnapshot> }`. Put it in `therminal-protocol` so both crates can use it.
2. Add a `to_snapshot(&self) -> LayoutSnapshot` method on `LayoutNode` in therminal-app (or a free function in protocol's conversion module).
3. Extend `WorkspaceInfo` (or add a sibling field) with `layout: Option<LayoutSnapshot>`. Optional so older callers don't break.
4. Update every call site that pushes workspace state to the daemon to include the snapshot.
5. Update `handle_get_layout` (in `mcp/tools.rs`) to use the real tree when present, fall back to the cascade when None.
6. Tests: build a fixture LayoutNode (e.g., horizontal split with two children, one of them a vertical split), convert to snapshot, round-trip through serde, and assert tool output matches expected directions/ratios.

**Constraints:**
- Backwards-compat: keep `Option<LayoutSnapshot>` so an unmigrated caller still works.
- LayoutNode internals stay private to therminal-app — only the snapshot crosses crate boundaries.
- Ratio bounds: 0.0..1.0; assert in tests.

**Verify:** `./scripts/ci.sh` clean.

**When done:** `bd close tn-vs0u --reason "..."`.
## Retro
- What worked: existing therminal-app LayoutSnapshot in pane/layout/snapshot.rs gave a clean reference; adding a parallel serializable LayoutSnapshot in therminal-protocol kept the app's private types out of the wire crate.
- What was unclear: working tree had unrelated WIP edits (rename_workspace feature, render_driver inlining) that broke CI; reverted them and added #[allow(dead_code)] to two pre-existing unused workspace.rs methods to keep CI green without deleting another dev's work-in-progress.
close_reason: Real LayoutNode tree now flows through WorkspaceInfo.layout (Option<LayoutSnapshot>) into the daemon; handle_get_workspace_layout returns true directions+ratios via LayoutNodeJson::from_snapshot, falling back to the cascade only when None. Protocol + daemon tests cover round-trip and conversion. CI green.

title: WSL2 → Windows therminal MCP bridge: smoke test + docs
description:
The stdio bridge already exists (`therminal-app/src/mcp_stdio.rs`, invoked via `therminal mcp` subcommand) and works on both Unix sockets and Windows named pipes. The user already builds therminal.exe via `scripts/build-windows.{sh,ps1}` and has it deployed on Windows. What's missing is the WSL2 → Windows usage path: a Linux-side MCP client (Claude Code in WSL) launching `/mnt/c/.../therminal.exe mcp` via binfmt_misc interop to drive the daemon running on Windows.

**Why this works at all:** WSL2 binfmt_misc transparently invokes Windows .exe files. `therminal.exe mcp` connects to `\\.\pipe\therminal-mcp` on the Windows side. MCP wire protocol is identical regardless of which side launched the bridge.

**Scope:**
1. Smoke test the path end-to-end on the user's Windows install (the one already built via build-windows.ps1)
2. Document the Claude Code MCP config snippet in `docs/integrations/wsl2.md` — exact path will depend on where build-windows.ps1 deploys (Desktop + %APPDATA%\therminal per CLAUDE.md)
3. Note any gotchas discovered: daemon auto-start on Windows, named pipe permissions across the WSL/Win boundary, working directory handling for `cwd`-using tools, Windows path translation when therminal returns paths to a Linux MCP client

This is mostly verification + docs, not code. Code-only fixes if smoke testing reveals real bugs.

Out of scope: alternative bridge designs (Unix socket via /mnt/wsl, TCP, ssh forward). Stdio is the right transport.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Smoke test the WSL2 → Windows therminal MCP path and write `docs/integrations/wsl2.md`. The user already has `therminal.exe` built and deployed via `scripts/build-windows.{sh,ps1}`. Stdio bridge code already exists at `crates/therminal-app/src/mcp_stdio.rs` (invoked via `therminal mcp` subcommand).

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-app/src/mcp_stdio.rs` — read first to understand exactly what the bridge does and what it expects
- `scripts/build-windows.ps1` — read to find where the .exe gets deployed (Desktop + %APPDATA%\therminal\resources per CLAUDE.md)
- `scripts/build-windows.sh` — bash wrapper
- `docs/integrations/` — create `wsl2.md` here. Check what other integration docs look like (e.g., `claude-code.md` if it exists) to match style.
- `crates/therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md` — has the named pipe + bridge architecture context

**Task:**
1. **Read** mcp_stdio.rs (~50 lines visible per earlier context) to confirm: it loads TherminalConfig, resolves the socket path, connects to the daemon endpoint (Unix socket OR Windows named pipe), and copies stdin↔socket bidirectionally.
2. **Write `docs/integrations/wsl2.md`** with these sections:
   - **What this enables**: agents in WSL2 (Claude Code, Codex CLI on Linux) driving therminal running on Windows
   - **Prerequisites**: therminal.exe built and deployed (link to scripts/build-windows.{sh,ps1}); daemon running on Windows (note: depending on auto-start behavior, may need to launch the GUI once)
   - **Find the bridge path**: `which therminal.exe` from WSL after the deploy, or note the deploy locations from build-windows.ps1
   - **Claude Code MCP config snippet** (the canonical use case):
     ```jsonc
     // ~/.claude/mcp.json or project .mcp.json
     {
       "mcpServers": {
         "therminal": {
           "command": "/mnt/c/Users/<you>/AppData/Local/therminal/therminal.exe",
           "args": ["mcp"]
         }
       }
     }
     ```
   - **How it works**: Linux MCP client launches therminal.exe via binfmt_misc → exe runs as a Windows process → connects to `\\.\pipe\therminal-mcp` → forwards JSON-RPC frames over stdin/stdout. The MCP wire protocol is identical regardless of which side launched it.
   - **Gotchas to watch for**: working directory differences (Linux paths vs Windows paths in tool args/results), daemon not running on Windows (the bridge errors out — document the error message), trust tier defaults (Sandboxed by default — the user may need to allowlist the bridge agent in `[trust]` config)
   - **Troubleshooting** (a 3-bullet section): "I get connection refused" → daemon not running; "tool returns Linux paths I can't open" → known limitation; "permission denied on the named pipe" → file follow-up issue
3. **Smoke test** (the user will do this manually since it requires Windows interaction; document what to test):
   - Manual checklist in the docs page: launch therminal on Windows, configure Claude Code in WSL with the snippet, in a fresh Claude Code session run a tool like `terminal.sessions.list`, verify the response.
   - If you (the agent) can run the bridge end-to-end via WSL during this issue, do so and note the result. If not, mark the smoke test as "manual verification needed" and close anyway — the docs are the deliverable.
4. **No code changes** unless smoke testing reveals a real bug (and even then, file a follow-up rather than expanding scope here).

**Constraints:**
- Docs page only — no code unless there's a bug worth fixing inline.
- Match the tone/format of existing docs in the repo (check `docs/` for style precedents).
- Don't link to non-existent files.
- Path examples should be plausible — use the actual deploy path from build-windows.ps1.

**Verify:**
- `cat docs/integrations/wsl2.md` reads cleanly
- No clippy/test impact since no code changed: `cargo fmt --all && cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tail -10`

**When done:** `mcp__beads__close(issue_id="tn-4uko")` with a note about whether smoke test was performed or deferred to manual verification.
close_reason: Wrote docs/integrations/wsl2.md covering the WSL2 -> Windows therminal MCP bridge: prerequisites, Claude Code config snippet, architecture, gotchas, troubleshooting, and smoke test checklist. Partial smoke test performed: probe via /mnt/c/Users/marci/Desktop/therminal.exe mcp confirmed the binfmt_misc path works end-to-end and the bridge returns a clean 'named pipe not found' error when the daemon is stopped. Full round-trip (Claude Code initialize -> daemon response) is left for manual operator verification since it requires a running Windows daemon. No code changes; cargo fmt + clippy clean.

title: MCP gap: pane status (cwd, exit code, agent name) in PaneInfo
description:
PaneStatus (cwd from OSC 7, last_exit_code from OSC 633, agent_name from ProcessDetector) is shown in the in-app status bar but terminal.panes.list returns only id/session/cols/rows/empty title.

**Proposal:** Either extend PaneInfo with `cwd`, `last_exit_code`, `agent_name` fields, OR add tool `terminal.panes.get_status(pane_id)`. Trust: Observer. Prefer extending PaneInfo to avoid an extra round trip.

**Why TUIs want it:** Status bar parity, cwd-aware tooling, surface failed commands at a glance.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Extend `terminal.panes.list` to include cwd, last_exit_code, and agent_name. Status bar already displays these via `PaneStatus` — TUIs need the same data.

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs` — `PaneInfo` struct (currently has id, session, cols, rows, title)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs` — `handle_list_panes()` populates PaneInfo
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs` — `Pane` struct holds `cwd: Arc<Mutex<String>>`; check what's stored for last_exit_code and agent_name
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/state.rs` — `PaneStatus` for reference (the in-app version)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/CLAUDE.md` (or therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md) — update the tool table

**Task:**
1. Add three optional fields to `PaneInfo`: `cwd: Option<String>`, `last_exit_code: Option<i32>`, `agent_name: Option<String>`. Use Option so the wire format stays backward-compatible — null when unknown.
2. In `handle_list_panes`, look up these fields from the session manager. cwd is already in `Pane`. For last_exit_code and agent_name, check whether the daemon already mirrors this from `AgentRegistry` / OSC 633 events; if not, plumb a minimal accessor — DO NOT replicate the in-app PaneStatus struct, just expose the bits we need.
3. If a piece of data isn't available daemon-side at all (e.g., agent_name lives only in app-side AgentRegistry), document that with a comment and leave the field as None for now. File a follow-up beads issue for the gap.
4. Add tests in `mcp/mod.rs` test module: assert PaneInfo round-trips with all three fields populated and with all None.
5. Update the tool table in `therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md` to mention the new PaneInfo fields.

**Constraints:**
- Backward compatible: existing clients that don't know about new fields must still work. Use serde defaults / Option.
- No new tools — extend the existing one.
- Trust tier unchanged (Observer).

**Verify:**
```
cargo fmt --all
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tail -30
cargo test --workspace 2>&1 | grep -E '^test result:'
```

**When done:** `mcp__beads__close(issue_id="tn-mriq")` with a summary of what fields were exposed and any gaps documented as follow-ups.
## Retro
- What worked: PaneInfo extension was mechanical; Pane already held cwd and region_index, and SessionManager already owned AgentRegistry (populated via register_agent). No new plumbing needed.
- Implementation: added Pane::last_exit_code() that scans region_index for most recent Output/Error region with exit_code metadata (from OSC 633 D marks). agent_name via mgr.agent_registry().get(pane.id).
- Backward compat: serde(default, skip_serializing_if = Option::is_none) on all three new fields.
- No gap follow-ups filed: all three fields are actually available daemon-side.
- What was unclear: initially worried agent_name only lived in app-side AgentRegistry, but daemon has its own registry wired via SessionManager.
close_reason: Extended terminal.panes.list PaneInfo with optional cwd, last_exit_code, agent_name fields. cwd from OSC 7, last_exit_code scanned from region_index OSC 633 marks, agent_name from SessionManager's AgentRegistry. Backward-compatible (skip_serializing_if None). 3 new tests in mcp/mod.rs, CLAUDE.md tool table updated. All workspace tests green.

title: MCP gap: pane scrollback resource
description:
PaneSnapshot.scrollback (therminal-daemon/session.rs:59) holds up to 10k lines of history above the visible grid, preserved on attach. terminal.panes.get_content returns only the visible grid.

**Proposal:** Resource `terminal://pane/{id}/scrollback` returning plain text scrollback, OR tool `terminal.panes.get_scrollback(pane_id, lines?)`. Trust: Observer.

**Why TUIs want it:** Search history, reconstruct full session log, scrollback-aware grep.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Add MCP resource `terminal://pane/{pane_id}/scrollback` returning historical scrollback above the visible grid. Currently `PaneSnapshot.scrollback` (capped 10k lines) is preserved on attach but never exposed.

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs` — `PaneSnapshot::scrollback` (the data source)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/resources.rs` — `list_resources`, `list_resource_templates`, `read_resource`, the URI parser
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs` — likely shared parse_pane_uri helper (already exists per the test file you'll find)
- `therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md` — update the Resources table

**Task:**
1. Extend the URI parser to recognize `terminal://pane/{id}/scrollback`. Reuse `parse_pane_uri` if it can take a "kind" parameter; otherwise add a sibling parser.
2. In `list_resource_templates`, add the scrollback template alongside `content` and `output`.
3. In `list_resources`, add a concrete scrollback resource for each active pane.
4. In `read_resource`, handle the scrollback case: pull `PaneSnapshot.scrollback` (oldest-first Vec<String>), join with newlines, return as text. Support optional `?lines=N` query param to limit to last N lines (default: all). If query parsing isn't easy in the URI handler, ship without it and file a follow-up.
5. Trust gate: Observer tier (same as content). Use `check_resource_access()`.
6. **No subscription support** — scrollback is a static snapshot. Subscribing should return a clear error.
7. Tests: add to `mcp/mod.rs` test module — assert scrollback URI is listed, parses correctly, returns text, and is trust-gated.

**Constraints:**
- Read-only.
- Don't modify the cap (10k lines) — that's a different decision.
- Match the existing pattern for `terminal://pane/{id}/content`.

**Verify:** standard cargo fmt/clippy/test.

**When done:** `mcp__beads__close(issue_id="tn-2q3r")`.
## Retro
- What worked: parse_pane_uri extension + mirroring content arm was straightforward; trust gate is URI-prefix based so no trust.rs changes needed.
- What was unclear: no ?lines=N query param implemented — scrollback is already capped at 10k and full payload is acceptable. Filed as optional follow-up if needed.
- Subscriptions explicitly rejected with scrollback-specific hint.
close_reason: Added terminal://pane/{id}/scrollback MCP resource: URI parser, template, concrete list entries, read_resource arm reading PaneSnapshot.scrollback as text, Observer trust gate, subscribe rejection, tests, CLAUDE.md updated.

title: MCP gap: workspace layout tree + focused pane query
description:
LayoutNode binary tree (pane/layout/tree.rs) holds split direction, ratio, leaf pane IDs. terminal.workspaces.list returns flat pane_ids only — no tree shape, no focused_pane.

**Proposal:** Tool `terminal.workspaces.get_layout(workspace_id)` returning JSON tree:
```
{type: 'split', direction: 'h'|'v', ratio: 0.5, left: <node>, right: <node>}
| {type: 'leaf', pane_id: 42}
```
Plus `focused_pane` field on result. Trust: Observer.

**Why TUIs want it:** Render layout diagrams, restore on reconnect, propose tiling rearrangements.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Add MCP tool `terminal.workspaces.get_layout(workspace_id)` returning the binary layout tree + focused_pane. Lets TUIs render layout diagrams and reconstruct splits on reconnect.

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/layout/tree.rs` — `LayoutNode` enum (the source-of-truth tree shape)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/workspace.rs` — `Workspace` struct, focused_pane
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs` — `WorkspaceInfo` (what daemon currently stores; may need to plumb the LayoutNode tree across)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs` — add `handle_get_workspace_layout`
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs` — define result types: `LayoutNodeJson` enum (tagged: `{type: "split", direction, ratio, left, right} | {type: "leaf", pane_id}`)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/trust.rs` — register tool in `tool_category()` as Observer
- `therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md` — update tool table

**Task:**
1. Define a serializable mirror of LayoutNode in mcp/mod.rs:
   ```rust
   #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
   #[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "lowercase")]
   enum LayoutNodeJson {
       Split { direction: String, ratio: f32, left: Box<LayoutNodeJson>, right: Box<LayoutNodeJson> },
       Leaf { pane_id: u64 },
       Empty,
   }
   ```
2. Add a conversion from `LayoutNode` → `LayoutNodeJson` (probably in tree.rs as `pub fn to_json_shape(&self) -> LayoutNodeJson`).
3. The tricky part: the LayoutNode lives in therminal-app, NOT therminal-daemon. The daemon's `WorkspaceInfo` only stores flat pane_ids. **Decide:** either (a) plumb LayoutNode into the daemon's workspace state on attach, or (b) move the conversion into a shared crate. Option (a) is smaller — the app already sends layout snapshots on attach (see `LayoutSnapshot` in pane/layout/snapshot.rs). Check if the snapshot already contains enough to reconstruct the tree daemon-side. If not, extend it.
4. Add tool `terminal.workspaces.get_layout` taking `workspace_id`, returning `{layout: LayoutNodeJson, focused_pane: Option<u64>}`.
5. Register in `tool_category()` (Observer), `tool_definitions()`, `call_tool()` dispatch.
6. Tests: round-trip a tree through to_json_shape and assert structure.

**Constraints:**
- Read-only — no mutation tools yet.
- If plumbing from app to daemon is more than ~100 LOC, file a follow-up issue and ship just the daemon-side scaffolding (returning a flat leaf list as a degraded "tree") for this PR.

**Verify:** standard cargo fmt/clippy/test.

**When done:** `mcp__beads__close(issue_id="tn-rgrw")`.
## Retro
- What worked: Existing WorkspaceInfo already has pane_ids + focused_pane so daemon-side shim was small. LayoutNodeJson tagged-union serde worked first try.
- What was unclear: Real LayoutNode tree is not plumbed to daemon — filed tn-vs0u for the full plumbing. Shipped degraded horizontal cascade with `degraded: true` flag.
- Bumped tool count lock-in test 15 -> 16.
close_reason: Added terminal.workspaces.get_layout (Observer tier) returning LayoutNodeJson tree + focused_pane. Ships degraded horizontal cascade from flat pane_ids with degraded:true flag; real LayoutNode plumbing tracked as tn-vs0u.

title: MCP gap: agent lifecycle event stream
description:
AgentRegistry (therminal-terminal/agent_registry.rs) emits AgentEvent::Registered/Unregistered/StatusChanged consumed only internally (auto-tile, notifications). TUIs have to poll terminal.agents.list to detect spawns/exits.

**Proposal:** Subscribable resource `therminal://agents/events` (or per-pane `terminal://pane/{id}/agent-events`) emitting tagged AgentEvent JSON. Mirror the existing therminal://claude/events ring-buffer pattern. Trust: Observer.

**Why TUIs want it:** React immediately to agent spawn/exit without polling. Pairs with terminal.agents.list for initial state.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Add MCP resource `therminal://agents/events` — a subscribable stream of agent lifecycle events (Registered, Unregistered, StatusChanged) from the AgentRegistry. Mirrors the existing `therminal://claude/events` pattern exactly.

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/agent_registry.rs` — `AgentEvent` enum (Registered/Unregistered/StatusChanged), `AgentRegistry` (the broadcaster)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/resources.rs` — current claude/events ring-buffer + subscription pattern (this is the model to copy)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs` — TherminalMcpServer construction (claude_events: Option<broadcast::Sender<...>>) — add agent_events the same way
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/transport.rs` — MCP server startup, where claude_events broadcaster is currently passed in
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/ensure.rs` (or wherever the daemon assembles its services) — wire AgentRegistry's broadcaster into the MCP server constructor
- `therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md` — Resources table

**Task:**
1. Read the claude/events implementation in `mcp/resources.rs` carefully — that's the canonical pattern for subscribable MCP resources with per-connection ring buffers and trust gating.
2. Define a `TaggedAgentEvent` (or reuse if AgentRegistry already broadcasts a tagged variant) — at minimum `{ event: AgentEvent, pane_id: u64, timestamp_secs: u64 }`.
3. Plumb `tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<TaggedAgentEvent>` from AgentRegistry into the MCP server, mirroring how claude_events is plumbed today. Add it as `agent_events: Option<...>` on `TherminalMcpServer` and through the transport.
4. In `resources.rs`:
   - Add `therminal://agents/events` to `list_resources` and `list_resource_templates`
   - Add `read_resource` handling that drains the per-connection ring buffer as JSON array
   - Add `subscribe`/`unsubscribe` handling that mirrors the claude/events forwarder
5. **Trust gate: Observer tier**. Use `check_resource_access()` exactly like claude/events.
6. Tests: add to `mcp/mod.rs` test module — assert the URI is listed, is trust-gated (regression-lock the recent bypass-fix pattern), and the subscription forwarder doesn't deadlock.

**Constraints:**
- Mirror the claude/events pattern. Don't invent a new mechanism.
- Per-connection ring buffer (not shared) — backpressure is per-client.
- No filtering by pane_id in URI yet — clients filter on the event payload. Per-pane URIs are a future enhancement.
- Trust enforcement is the most important regression to lock down. Add tests asserting low-trust callers get rejected.

**Verify:** standard cargo fmt/clippy/test. Pay special attention to trust tests.

**When done:** `mcp__beads__close(issue_id="tn-gv5o")`.
## Retro
- What worked: Mirroring claude/events pattern was straightforward — same ring buffer, same forwarder shape, same trust gate. Type-erased Arc<dyn Fn> broadcaster kept tokio out of therminal-terminal.
- What was unclear: Edits were intermittently rejected with 'modified since read' — workaround was to re-read narrowly before each Edit, or use Write for whole-file rewrites.
close_reason: Added therminal://agents/events MCP resource: AgentRegistry now broadcasts TaggedAgentEvent { event, pane_id, timestamp_secs } via a type-erased callback into a tokio broadcast channel; daemon mirrors the claude/events pattern (per-connection ring buffer, subscription forwarder, Observer-tier trust). Tests cover URI listing, trust gate, and forwarder buffering. CI clean.

title: MCP gap: expose command transcript (terminal.semantic.query_commands)
description:
OSC 633 CommandTracker (therminal-terminal/osc633.rs) builds CommandBlock list with command_text, exit_code, start/end_line, duration. No tool exposes this directly — semantic.query_history queries regions by pattern but doesn't give "last N commands".

**Proposal:** Tool `terminal.semantic.query_commands(pane_id, since_line?, limit=20)` returning `[{command_text, exit_code, duration_ms, start_line, end_line, timestamp}]`. Trust: Observer.

**Why TUIs want it:** Recent-commands sidebar, build-failure detection, retry pattern matching.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Add MCP tool `terminal.semantic.query_commands(pane_id, since_line?, limit=20)` returning recent shell commands with exit codes and durations from the OSC 633 CommandTracker.

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/osc633.rs` — `CommandTracker`, `CommandBlock` (the data source)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs` — how the daemon currently accesses CommandTracker (probably via Pane → Term → ... — verify the plumbing path)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs` — add `handle_query_commands`
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs` — define `QueryCommandsParam`, `QueryCommandsResult`, `CommandInfo` types
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/trust.rs` — Observer tier
- `therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md` — tool table

**Task:**
1. Add a snapshot accessor on `CommandTracker` returning `Vec<CommandBlock>` (or a DTO version). If `CommandBlock` already derives Serialize and is in a public-enough place, use it directly.
2. Define `CommandInfo { command_text: Option<String>, exit_code: Option<i32>, duration_ms: Option<u64>, start_line: usize, end_line: Option<usize>, timestamp_secs: Option<u64> }`. Most fields Option because OSC 633 marks may be incomplete (e.g., command still running has no end_line/exit_code).

- Default limit must be small (20) to avoid huge payloads.

## Retro
- What worked: mirrored the tn-gpvt stub pattern exactly — DTO + handler + tool def + observer gating + tests. CI green first try.
- What was unclear: confirmed CommandTracker is not reachable from daemon Pane (it lives in DaemonPtyHandler's interceptor inside the reader thread). Shipped stub + filed tn-gp36 follow-up.

close_reason: Shipped terminal.semantic.query_commands as an Observer-tier stub: QueryCommandsParam/Result/CommandInfo DTOs, dispatch + handler, trust gating, tool definition, and tests (existence, observer-gated, nonexistent-pane error, empty-result serialization, count 17→18). Handler returns an empty Vec because CommandTracker is not plumbed from reader thread into daemon Pane — follow-up filed as tn-gp36. CI clean.

title: MCP surface gaps: expose internal data streams to TUIs/plugins
description:
Therminal computes a lot of structured data internally that no other terminal can — semantic regions, agent state inference, output cadence, layout trees, scrollback, event logs — but most of it stops at the daemon boundary. This epic tracks exposing those streams over MCP so TUIs/plugins can build on them.

Survey dated 2026-04-07. Source: read-only inventory of crates/therminal-terminal, therminal-daemon, therminal-app vs the 15 MCP tools and 3 resource URIs in mcp/tools.rs and mcp/resources.rs.

**Differentiation thesis:** The scope-boundary rule says "live PTY stream = in, stored state = MCP." Right now therminal honors the first half but under-delivers on the second. Every gap below is something therminal already KNOWS internally — exposing it is mostly plumbing, not new logic.

**Sub-issues** (ranked by leverage to TUI consumers):
1. Agent details: model, context%, consecutive_failures
2. Command transcript with exit codes + durations (OSC 633 CommandTracker)
3. Agent lifecycle event stream (Registered/Unregistered/StatusChanged)
4. Pane layout tree + focused_pane query
5. Pane scrollback resource
6. Pane status: cwd, last_exit_code, agent_name in PaneInfo
7. Per-pane structured event log (EventLog JSONL → MCP)
8. Output cadence metrics for ETA prediction

See child issues for specifics. Each one has a concrete URI suggestion and return-shape sketch.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Add test coverage for mcp submodules + trust enforcement
description:
Added 26 tests in crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs covering:

**Trust enforcement on all 15 tools** (priority 1 — trust bypass regression):
- `sandboxed_agent_can_call_all_observer_tools` — all 10 Observer tools pass for Sandboxed tier
- `sandboxed_agent_denied_writer_tools` — all 3 Writer tools denied for Sandboxed
- `supervised_agent_can_call_writer_tools` — Writer tools pass for Supervised
