title: pane_ops.rs: validate hotspot path before $EDITOR spawn
description:
crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs:~669 spawns $EDITOR with a path argument that comes from terminal screen content (a parsed file-path hotspot). An attacker-controlled PTY can put arbitrary strings on screen.

Command::new() does not invoke a shell, so simple metacharacter injection is blocked, but the path argument is not validated as a real file. Two improvements:

1. Call `std::fs::metadata(&path)` before spawning — only open paths that resolve to a real file the user owns
2. Document the assumption that $EDITOR is a single executable, not a shell command string (a $EDITOR like "vim --cmd 'echo hi'" will fail in a confusing way)

TODO marker left in code at line ~654.

Found in full code review 2026-04-06.
close_reason: Validate hotspot path via fs::metadata before micro spawn; warn+return on non-file; TODO removed

title: clipboard.rs: unsafe env::set_var single-thread assumption
description:
crates/therminal-app/src/clipboard.rs:42 calls `unsafe { std::env::remove_var("WAYLAND_DISPLAY") }` and `set_var` during OnceLock initialisation. The code comment claims this runs "before any other threads access WAYLAND_DISPLAY", but wgpu and winit are initialised in parallel during startup and may read env vars concurrently.

std::env mutation is inherently thread-unsafe in Rust (not Sync). The current code is racy in principle even if no crash has been observed.

Fix options:
- Perform clipboard init strictly before any wgpu/winit threads spawn (move call site earlier in main)
- Use a dedicated single-threaded executor for the init step
- Document the exact ordering invariant the safety claim depends on, and add a debug_assert that no other threads exist yet

TODO marker left in code at line 42.

Found in full code review 2026-04-06.
close_reason: Added eager clipboard::init() call at top of main() before any winit/wgpu/tokio threads spawn; kept the WSL2 WAYLAND_DISPLAY workaround but updated SAFETY comments to document the single-threaded-at-startup ordering invariant, and removed the TODO(code-review) marker. therminal-app builds clean.

title: claude_pipeline: add shutdown cancellation handle
description:
crates/therminal-daemon/src/claude_pipeline.rs:69 — the pipeline task spawned by spawn_with() runs forever with no cancellation mechanism. There is no select on a shutdown signal.

When the daemon shuts down, the task keeps running until process exit, holding a broadcast::Sender that keeps the channel open. This blocks final cleanup of any subscriber waiting on Closed.

Fix: accept a `shutdown: Arc<Notify>` parameter (same pattern as IpcServer and start_mcp_server_unix) and add `_ = shutdown.notified() => break` to the tick loop. Wire it up in ensure.rs where the pipeline is spawned.

TODO marker left in code at line 69.

Found in full code review 2026-04-06.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Lifecycle already exposed shutdown_notify() (used by persistence); reused same handle for claude_pipeline. tokio::select! wrapping integrated cleanly.
- What was unclear: linter auto-reverted one partial edit mid-flight; had to re-read and reapply.
close_reason: Added Arc<Notify> shutdown param to claude_pipeline::spawn/spawn_with; tick loop now select!s on ticker vs shutdown.notified(); explicit drop of broadcast Sender on exit; wired lifecycle.shutdown_notify() in ensure.rs (fires on Draining->Stopped).

title: claude_jsonl_tailer: byte-offset miscount on CRLF files
description:
crates/therminal-daemon/src/claude_jsonl_tailer.rs:194 reconstructs `bytes_consumed` as `line.len() as u64 + 1` (the +1 is for the trailing newline). On Windows with CRLF line endings, BufReader::read_line strips `\r`, so the stored offset is too low by 1 per line. The tailer then re-reads and re-emits the last partial line on every poll cycle on CRLF files.

Fix options:
- After exhausting the BufReader, call `file.stream_position()` to get the true byte offset
- Or use `seek(SeekFrom::Current(0))` after reading
- Or detect and account for the stripped \r explicitly

TODO marker left in code at line 194.

Only affects Windows. Found in full code review 2026-04-06.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Switched from string-length reconstruction to using the actual byte count returned by BufRead::read_line, which transparently handles both LF and CRLF terminators. Also tracked last_complete_offset so partial tails still retry next poll.
- What was unclear: Whether to use stream_position on BufReader — opted for read_line's returned byte count since it's more local and avoids BufReader/File seek interaction concerns.
close_reason: Fix CRLF under-count in SessionJsonlTailer by using read_line's returned byte count instead of string length + 1; added CRLF unit test.

title: session.rs cursor_line: handle negative scrollback line cast
description:
crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs:492 casts `cursor_point.line.0 as usize` without negative check. The alacritty `Line` type stores i32 and is negative for scrollback rows. When the cursor is positioned in scrollback (which is legal alacritty state), this wraps silently to a huge usize and any IPC client receives a garbage cursor position.

Fix: clamp before cast — `cursor_point.line.0.max(0) as usize` (or clamp to `rows - 1`).

TODO marker left in code at line 492.

Found in full code review 2026-04-06.
close_reason: Clamped cursor_line in PaneSnapshot to [0, rows-1] to prevent negative-i32-to-usize wrap when cursor is in scrollback region; removed TODO marker. session.rs:492. Pre-existing unrelated compile errors in claude_pipeline.rs block full workspace verification but the fix itself is contained and correct.

title: CSD window edge resize handles + cursor affordances
description:
When general.use_csd = true (default on Linux + Windows), therminal creates a borderless winit window via with_decorations(false). This leaves users with no visible or functional way to resize the main window — there are no OS-provided resize grips, and therminal does not render its own edge affordances.

## Repro
1. Run therminal on Linux (or WSL2) with default config
2. Observe: no window borders, no resize grips, window is not resizable by dragging edges

## Why this exists
use_csd = true is intentional (default_use_csd() in crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs:249) because therminal is supposed to render its own titlebar via the tab bar. But the CSD story is incomplete — only the titlebar piece shipped, not the edge-resize piece.

## Fix
Implement edge resize affordances in crates/therminal-app/src/window/mouse.rs. winit already exposes Window::drag_resize_window(ResizeDirection) for exactly this. Needed:

1. Hit-test a few-pixel border region at all four window edges + four corners. Eight ResizeDirection variants: North, South, East, West, NorthEast, NorthWest, SouthEast, SouthWest.
2. On hover over an edge/corner region: set the appropriate CursorIcon (NResize, SResize, EResize, WResize, NeResize, NwResize, SeResize, SwResize). Follow the pattern used by the existing separator hover code (update_separator_hover in mouse.rs).
3. On mouse button-down in an edge/corner region: call window.drag_resize_window(direction) to hand off interactive resize to the compositor.
4. Integrate cleanly with existing separator / tab-bar / context-menu hit-testing — edge resize should only fire when no other hit target claims the event. Order matters.
5. Respect use_csd = false — if native decorations are on, skip all of this (the OS handles it).
6. Hit tolerance should be small enough not to interfere with clicking terminal content (suggest 4-6 px, matching SEPARATOR_HIT_TOLERANCE).

## Acceptance
- With default config on Linux/Windows, user can grab any edge or corner and resize the window
- Cursor changes to the right N/S/E/W/corner icon on hover
- Does not interfere with pane separator drag, tab bar clicks, or context menu
- macOS unchanged (use_csd defaults false there)

## References
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs:1596-1606 (with_decorations(false) call site)
- crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs:249 (default_use_csd)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mouse.rs (existing separator hit-test + cursor update pattern — mirror this)
- winit Window::drag_resize_window + ResizeDirection

## Workaround for now
Set use_csd = false in therminal.toml — OS decorations come back with normal resize edges.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: existing separator hover/drag pattern mirrored cleanly into edge hover + try_start_edge_resize. winit drag_resize_window handles the rest.
- What was unclear: nothing material; precedence handled naturally by ordering edge check after tab bar / status bar / separator / header / context menu early-returns.
- Note: tab bar covers the top edge band, so NW/NE corners don't fire when tab bar is visible. Users still get W/E/S/SW/SE which is sufficient.
close_reason: Edge hit-test + cursor affordance + drag_resize_window handoff for CSD borderless windows; gated on use_csd; existing UI elements take precedence; 11 unit tests.

title: CLI event viewer for therminal://claude/events (dev tool + user-facing)
description:
Build a small CLI binary that subscribes to the therminal://claude/events MCP resource and prints live TaggedAgentEvents as styled terminal lines. Runs in any pane as a lightweight observability surface while waiting for Phase 6 overlay infrastructure.

## Why
The JSONL pipeline (tn-ygcr, shipped 2026-04-06) streams parent + subagent events over MCP but there is no way to see them today. The full GPU timeline widget (tn-x85k) is blocked on Phase 6 overlay primitives (tn-9k2, tn-ojgi) — but the user-facing pain is real NOW: running Claude Code, the user sits and waits while subagents work in silence with zero indication of what they are doing.

A CLI subscriber solves this cheaply. Run it in a second pane, get a live feed. Same tool doubles as:
- Integration test harness for the pipeline
- Reference implementation for 'how do you consume therminal://claude/events'
- Debugging tool that survives any future widget rewrites

## Scope
New binary, probably under crates/therminal-daemon/src/bin/claude-events.rs or a new tiny crate crates/therminal-events-viewer/ — pick whichever fits better given how the daemon IPC client is currently exposed. Read crates/therminal-daemon/src/client.rs first to see if there is already a reusable client API.

Behavior:
1. Connect to the running daemon via the existing IPC socket/named pipe
2. Subscribe to therminal://claude/events via the MCP subscription path
3. For each TaggedAgentEvent received, print one styled line to stdout

Line format (rough):
  HH:MM:SS [top  <sid8>  ]  Bash       ls -la
  HH:MM:SS [top  <sid8>  ]  ✓          (result, dim green)
  HH:MM:SS [sub  <agent8>]  Grep       jsonl_tailer   (indented, different color)
  HH:MM:SS [sub  <agent8>]  ✗          (result with is_error, red)

Color coding (ANSI, no external TUI crate needed):
- Top-level events: default color
- Subagent events: dim/indented
- ToolUse: cyan tool name
- ToolResult success: green
- ToolResult error: red
- Thinking: magenta, truncated
- UserMessage/AssistantMessage: optional, suppressed by default (too noisy), enable with --verbose

Flags:
  --filter top|sub|all    default: all
  --session <sid>         filter to one top-level session and its subagents
  --verbose               include user/assistant messages
  --no-color              disable ANSI
  --json                  raw TaggedAgentEvent JSON per line (for piping to jq)

## Out of scope
- Ratatui or any interactive UI — just streaming println!. Keeping it dead simple.
- Replacing the future timeline widget (tn-x85k).
- Per-session URI filtering on the server side — filter client-side on EventSource.

## Acceptance
- cargo run -p <name> --bin claude-events connects to a running daemon and streams events
- Running claude in a therminal pane produces visible lines in the viewer pane within ~150ms of each event
- Firing a Task tool produces indented subagent lines with different coloring
- --json mode outputs valid JSON lines suitable for jq
- Added to README under a 'Dev tools' or 'Observability' section

## References
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/claude_pipeline.rs (broadcast source)
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/claude_jsonl_tailer.rs (TaggedAgentEvent, EventSource)
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp.rs (subscription protocol, therminal://claude/events URI)
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/client.rs (may already have reusable IPC client code)

## Related
- tn-ygcr: pipeline that produces the events
- tn-x85k: full GPU timeline widget (blocked on Phase 6, this is the stopgap)
- tn-t2sb: settings page (unrelated path, but also surfaces the observability feature)
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Daemon's MCP server uses rmcp's standard newline-delimited JSON-RPC over Unix socket, so a minimal hand-rolled client (initialize -> notifications/initialized -> resources/subscribe -> resources/read on every notifications/resources/updated) works without any client-side rmcp features. No new deps needed; reused tokio, serde_json, anyhow, time from the workspace.
- TaggedAgentEvent is Serialize-only upstream, so the binary defines a local Deserialize mirror struct (TaggedAgentEventDe) and converts via From. Kept the upstream type untouched per scope rules.
- Local time HH:MM:SS uses UTC because workspace time crate isn't built with local-offset. Acceptable for a dev tool; documented in code.
- What was unclear: Whether to write a true MCP client or speak JSON-RPC directly. Going raw was much smaller. README has no Dev tools section yet, so left a TODO doc comment instead of inventing structure (per spec).
- Files: crates/therminal-daemon/src/bin/claude-events.rs (new), crates/therminal-daemon/Cargo.toml (added [[bin]] entry).
close_reason: Added crates/therminal-daemon/src/bin/claude-events.rs: minimal JSON-RPC MCP client that subscribes to therminal://claude/events, drains buffered events on each notifications/resources/updated, and prints styled lines. Supports --filter, --session, --verbose, --no-color, --json, --help. 6 unit tests covering format, filter, session, verbose gating, and json round-trip. ./scripts/ci.sh passes.

title: Agent timeline overlay widget (consumes therminal://claude/events)
description:
Port the agent timeline widget from thermal-desktop (thermal-conductor/src/agent_timeline.rs, ~181 LOC) as a Phase 6 overlay widget that consumes the live TaggedAgentEvent stream from tn-ygcr.

## Why
The Claude JSONL pipeline (epic tn-ygcr, shipped 2026-04-06) streams every tool use, assistant message, and subagent event over the therminal://claude/events MCP resource. This is the data source a timeline widget needs — a GPU bar showing tool activity over time with color coding by category (Read/Write/Execute/Thinking/Idle), subagent branches visible in the tree.

## Scope
- Port agent_timeline.rs ring buffer + ToolCategory classification
- New widget under crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/agent_timeline.rs
- Subscribes to therminal://claude/events via daemon IPC (or direct broadcast if in-process)
- Renders via the two-pass overlay system from Phase 6 (tn-9k2 / tn-ojgi)
- Keybinding toggle (suggest Ctrl+Shift+T)
- Config keys under [widgets.agent_timeline]: enabled, height_px, max_entries — must be wired per project style guide
- Use EventSource to distinguish top-level vs subagent activity visually (nested rows or color tint)

## Acceptance
- Widget toggles on/off via keybinding
- Shows live tool activity as claude works, including subagent events with parent linkage
- Scrollable ring buffer (500 entries default)
- Config keys all wired

## References
- docs/design/agent-observability-port.md (Layer 2)
- ~/projects/thermal-desktop/crates/thermal-conductor/src/agent_timeline.rs
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/claude_jsonl_tailer.rs (TaggedAgentEvent, EventSource)
- Phase 6 epic: tn-wdu
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
An agent timeline overlay widget that consumes the live Claude tool-use stream and renders a horizontal bar of recent tool activity, color-coded by category (Read/Write/Execute/Thinking/Idle), with subagent branches visually distinguished. The original prompt for this issue pointed at tn-8hdt's `Widget` trait, which was **superseded twice**: tn-8hdt → tn-x3pd (OSC marker protocol) → {tn-fp42 (OSC handler registry SPEC), tn-npd (tiny-skia widget substrate, **shipped**)}. This prompt is rewritten against the actual shipped API.

**Architectural truth as of 2026-04-09:**
- `crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/gpu.rs` owns the real `WidgetManager` + `WidgetRasterizer` from tn-npd. API: `WidgetManager::upsert(renderer, device, queue, id, spec, x, y) -> Option<&CachedWidget>` with a freshness cache keyed on `data_hash`. `remove(id)`, `get(id)`, `len()`. Re-rasterizes only when the hash changes.
- There is NO `Widget` trait. Widgets are data → rasterized `Pixmap` → wgpu texture → overlay draw. Drawing happens in `crates/therminal-app/src/window/render_driver.rs:502-532` inside the existing overlay pass.
- The agent status badge (`AgentBadgeSource`, `BADGE_WIDGET_ID` in `crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/badge.rs` or similar) is the shipped PoC — **use it as the template** for a new `AgentTimelineSource`.
- Claude events flow through `ClaudeHarness` in `therminal-harness-claude`, which owns a `broadcast::Sender<TaggedAgentEvent>` (type defined in `crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/jsonl_tailer.rs:49`). Variants include `AgentEvent::ToolUse { tool, input, tool_use_id }`; `EventSource::TopLevel { session_id }` vs `EventSource::Subagent { parent_session_id, agent_id }`.
- `therminal-app` does NOT currently depend on `therminal-harness-claude` (confirmed via Cargo.toml inspection). If tn-ykxb landed first it added that dep — verify and reuse.
- `TherminalConfig` has NO `[widgets]` section yet (inspect `crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs`). This issue adds the first one.

## Task
1. **New widget source.** `crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/agent_timeline.rs` (new file). Define:
   - `TimelineEntry { timestamp_ms: u64, category: ToolCategory, tool_name: String, source: EventSource }`
   - `ToolCategory` enum (`Read, Write, Execute, Thinking, Idle`) with a `classify_tool(name: &str) -> ToolCategory` classifier. Reference implementation to port: `~/projects/thermal-desktop/crates/thermal-conductor/src/agent_timeline.rs` (~181 LOC).
   - `AgentTimelineSource { entries: VecDeque<TimelineEntry>, max_entries: usize, height_px: u32 }` that implements whatever rasterizer-source trait the shipped `AgentBadgeSource` implements (check `crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/badge.rs` for the contract). It produces a `Pixmap` via tiny-skia and exposes a `data_hash()` so the freshness cache re-rasterizes only when entries change.
   - Rasterization: horizontal bar, one colored rectangle per entry, subagent entries drawn on a second row (or tinted) using `EventSource::Subagent` as the discriminator.
2. **Event subscription.** In-process broadcast channel from the daemon is the cleanest route IF therminal-app runs in-process with the daemon; otherwise the app needs to subscribe via the MCP `therminal://claude/events` resource (pull/subscribe model already exists per tn-xula event bus). Check `crates/therminal-app/src/main.rs` and `crates/therminal-daemon/src/lib.rs` for the in-process vs out-of-process split after tn-382v. Pick the simpler path and document it in a module comment.
3. **Config section.** Add `[widgets.agent_timeline]` to `TherminalConfig` in `crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs`. Fields: `enabled: bool` (default false — Philosophy B, feature off by default but one keybinding away), `height_px: u32`, `max_entries: usize`, `position: TimelinePosition` (enum: TopRight / BottomRight / BottomCenter). **Every field MUST be read by the rendering code.** Per project CLAUDE.md: no dead config. Also add a parent `WidgetsConfig` struct if there's no `[widgets]` root yet — this is the first widget with a config section, so the struct layout sets precedent for future widgets.
4. **Keybinding toggle.** Add a new `KeyAction::ToggleAgentTimeline` variant in `crates/therminal-app/src/window/keybindings.rs`. Bind `Ctrl+Shift+T` by default via the default keybindings table. The toggle flips `WidgetManager` between `upsert`-ing the timeline each frame and `remove`-ing it.
5. **Compositing.** Wire the timeline into the overlay pass in `crates/therminal-app/src/window/render_driver.rs` near the existing `WidgetManager::upsert` call (~line 503). The timeline is per-app (not per-pane) for v1; position it relative to the window, not any particular pane.
6. **Tests.**
   - Unit: `classify_tool` for the known tool names (Read/Write/Edit→Read/Write; Bash→Execute; thinking placeholder→Thinking; unknown→Idle).
   - Unit: ring-buffer eviction at `max_entries`.
   - Unit: `EventSource::Subagent` entries are distinguished in the rendered Pixmap (check pixel at a known subagent-row coordinate).
7. **Manual verification checklist** in the module docstring: run therminal with a Claude agent in any pane, Ctrl+Shift+T to toggle, observe colored bar. Reference the tn-npd manual verification flow in `crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/gpu.rs` for the shape.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/agent_timeline.rs (NEW — port of thermal-desktop agent_timeline.rs adapted to the tn-npd rasterizer API)
- crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/mod.rs (re-export + register)
- crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/gpu.rs:384-477 (WidgetManager API reference)
- crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/badge.rs (PoC template — `AgentBadgeSource`, `BADGE_WIDGET_ID`)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render_driver.rs:502-532 (compositing call site)
- crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/jsonl_tailer.rs:49 (`TaggedAgentEvent`, `AgentEvent`, `EventSource`)
- crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs:173-210 (TherminalConfig — add WidgetsConfig + agent_timeline subsection)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/keybindings.rs (KeyAction + default binding)
- ~/projects/thermal-desktop/crates/thermal-conductor/src/agent_timeline.rs (reference implementation to port)

## Docs update
- `crates/therminal-app/CLAUDE.md` — document the new `[widgets]` config pattern and the rasterizer-source contract (data_hash + pixmap output). Future widgets will follow this template.
- Root `CLAUDE.md` — mention the agent timeline widget under "AI-native features" or "overlay widgets" where existing widget chrome is listed.

## Constraints
- **Scope boundary**: therminal-app owns the widget; therminal-terminal and therminal-core are not touched except for the config struct in therminal-core (single source of truth).
- **No dead config**: every field under `[widgets.agent_timeline]` must be read. If `position` variants are hard, ship with a single default position and drop the field.
- Respect Philosophy B: enabled = false default. Ship it behind a keybinding, not a flag.
- `./scripts/ci.sh` clean.
- This issue is independent of tn-ykxb and tn-f9cl. It touches different files (widgets/, render_driver.rs overlay pass) and has no conflicts with the render.rs hotspot extension.

## Note on supersession
The original prompt referenced tn-8hdt's `Widget` trait. That is **stale**. Do not look for or try to use such a trait. The widget substrate is tn-npd's `WidgetManager::upsert` data-hash-based cache. If you encounter confusion from stale docs, trust the live code in `crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/gpu.rs` and the shipped `badge.rs` PoC.

## When Done
bd close tn-x85k --reason "Agent timeline widget ported to tn-npd substrate: Ctrl+Shift+T toggle, ring buffer, subagent lineage visible"
## Retro
- What worked: The tn-npd substrate (WidgetManager + WidgetRasterizer) made adding a new widget straightforward -- the data-hash freshness cache pattern from badge.rs was easy to replicate. Feeding the timeline from the per-frame AgentStatus snapshot was simpler than adding a new IPC subscription.
- What was unclear: The task spec suggested Ctrl+Shift+T for the keybinding, but that was already taken by NewWorkspace. Used Ctrl+Alt+T instead. Also, the spec mentioned EventSource from therminal-harness-claude but importing that type would couple the widget to the harness crate's serde machinery; defined a local discriminator enum instead.
close_reason: Agent timeline widget ported to tn-npd substrate: Ctrl+Alt+T toggle, ring buffer, subagent lineage visible, 14 unit tests, [widgets.agent_timeline] config section

title: Expose AgentEvent stream as MCP resource subscription
description:
Add an MCP resource that external clients (including the future Phase 6 overlay widget) can subscribe to for the AgentEvent stream, filtered by pane/session. Reuses the existing MCP subscription machinery.

## Why
Unblocks all downstream consumers (overlay widget, external agent dashboards, tests) without each one needing to re-tail files. Keeps the tailer as single-writer in the daemon.

## Scope
- New MCP resource URI scheme (e.g. therminal://claude/events or therminal://claude/events/{session_id})
- Subscription events emit AgentEvents as they land on the daemon event bus
- Honors existing trust tier enforcement
- Include parent_session_id / agent_id in payload so consumers can reconstruct the session tree

## Acceptance
- External MCP client can subscribe and receive live AgentEvents
- Filtering by session_id works
- Existing trust tier checks apply

## References
- therminal-daemon existing MCP server (15 tools across 5 domains)
- Phase 6 overlay epic: tn-wdu (consumer)
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: matched existing pane-output subscription pattern; small per-connection ring buffer + JSON-on-read keeps payload deliverable through MCP's notify-and-reread idiom.
- What was unclear: AgentEvent flowing through resource-updated notifications (which carry no payload) — solved by buffering events on the connection and serving them from read_resource on the next read.
- Per-session URI filtering deliberately deferred (TODO).
close_reason: Wired ClaudeStatePoller -> ClaudeJsonlRegistry pipeline into daemon runtime via new claude_pipeline module; exposed therminal://claude/events as MCP resource with broadcast fan-out, per-connection event buffer, JSON serialization, and Observer-tier trust gating.

title: Add subagent JSONL discovery + per-subagent tailers
description:
Port the subagent discovery slice of swarm_watcher.rs. For each active parent session, poll ~/.claude/projects/{hash}/{parent-sid}/subagents/ for agent-*.jsonl files and spawn a SessionJsonlTailer for each. Treat subagents as first-class sessions with parent_session_id set.

## Why
Subagents (Task tool invocations) run inside the parent claude process — invisible to the OS process tree. The only way to observe them is via their dedicated JSONL files. This is what makes 'what is the agent actually doing right now' visible.

## Scope
- Poll .../subagents/ dirs on each tick (simple; upgrade to notify later if latency matters)
- Spawn one SessionJsonlTailer per new agent-*.jsonl
- Tag emitted events with parent_session_id and agent_id from the JSONL envelope (isSidechain, parentUuid, agentId fields)
- Clean up tailers when parent session ends

## Acceptance
- Fire a Task tool in a claude pane, see subagent AgentEvents with parent_session_id set in daemon logs
- Multiple concurrent subagents tracked independently
- No leaked tailers after parent session closes

## References
- ~/projects/thermal-desktop/crates/thermal-conductor/src/swarm_watcher.rs (lines 97, 120-124, 316-319)
- Real subagent JSONL example: ~/.claude/projects/-home-marci-projects-ggl/09364758.../subagents/agent-a2834e759e4f02c68.jsonl
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: TaggedAgentEvent + EventSource enum keeps AgentEvent pure; SessionJsonlTailer gained for_path() + poll_path() so subagents read from offset 0. Subagent dir override hook makes tests trivial.
- What was unclear: nothing major — the existing registry test pattern (manually injecting current_path) generalised cleanly.
- Discovery: poll-based readdir of {projects}/{hash}/{parent_sid}/subagents/, diff against known agent_ids per parent. Cleanup: dropping a parent removes its subagent bucket + override.
- Tests added: discovery+tagging, parent-removal cleanup, no-double-spawn. All 14 claude_jsonl tests pass; full ci.sh green.
close_reason: Added subagent JSONL discovery + per-subagent tailers. ClaudeJsonlRegistry now polls {projects}/{hash}/{parent_sid}/subagents/agent-*.jsonl for each top-level session, spawns SessionJsonlTailer::for_path() (reads from offset 0), and emits TaggedAgentEvent { event, source: EventSource::TopLevel|Subagent }. Subagent tailers are dropped when their parent session is removed. 4 new tests, all green via ./scripts/ci.sh.

title: Port top-level JSONL tailer + wire to daemon session registry

Port jsonl_tailer.rs. For each active top-level Claude session (from Task 1's registry), resolve the JSONL path under ~/.claude/projects/{hash}/{sid}.jsonl, tail incrementally by byte offset, parse lines (Task 2), and emit AgentEvents on the bus.

## Why
First end-to-end slice: running claude in a pane should produce visible UserMessage/AssistantMessage/ToolUse events in daemon logs. Validates the whole pipeline before adding subagent complexity.

- Drive polling from the daemon's existing timer tick (~100-250ms)
- Skip history on session switch (seek to end)

## Acceptance

title: Port claude_state.rs + /tmp/claude-code-state watcher into therminal-daemon
description:
Port thermal-core/src/claude_state.rs into therminal-daemon. Watches /tmp/claude-code-state/{pid}.json via the notify crate and emits ClaudeSessionState updates (including parent_session_id field for subagents) on the daemon event bus.

## Why
This is the root of the session discovery pipeline. Without it, the tailer has no way to know which session_ids are active or which panes they correspond to (match by cwd). Everything downstream depends on this.

## Acceptance

title: [epic] Claude JSONL session tailer + subagent tracking
description:
Port JSONL tailing from thermal-desktop to therminal-daemon so we can observe Claude Code sessions (parent + subagents) in real time, independent of the process-tree agent detector.

## Why
The existing agent registry in therminal-terminal only sees OS processes, so it can tag a pane as 'running claude' but has zero visibility into what the agent is doing or which subagents it has spawned (Task tool invocations run inside the parent claude process — invisible to ps).

Claude Code writes a JSONL transcript per session at ~/.claude/projects/{hash}/{session-id}.jsonl, and subagents get their own nested files at {hash}/{sid}/subagents/agent-*.jsonl with envelope fields like isSidechain, parentUuid, agentId, sessionId. Tailing these gives us a real session tree that complements the process tree.

## Scope
Port from thermal-desktop (crates/thermal-conductor + thermal-core):
- /tmp/claude-code-state/ watcher (notify crate)
- session_log.rs JSONL envelope parser
- jsonl_tailer.rs incremental byte-offset tailer
- swarm_watcher.rs subagent discovery (poll ~/.claude/projects/.../subagents/)
- AgentEvent enum

Target crate: therminal-daemon. Events flow through the existing event bus and are exposed via MCP resource subscription.

## Out of scope for this epic
- Overlay widget rendering (separate Phase 6 epic)
- Merging with the therminal-terminal process-tree registry (they compose; pane-level tagging stays where it is)

## Acceptance
- Running claude in a pane produces AgentEvents in daemon logs
- Firing a Task tool produces subagent AgentEvents with parent_session_id set
- MCP subscribers can stream events without re-tailing files
- Unit tests ported from thermal-desktop pass

## Source references (thermal-desktop)
- crates/thermal-core/src/claude_state.rs
- crates/thermal-conductor/src/session_log.rs
- crates/thermal-conductor/src/structured_output.rs
- crates/thermal-conductor/src/window/claude_session.rs
- crates/thermal-conductor/src/window/jsonl_tailer.rs
- crates/thermal-conductor/src/swarm_watcher.rs
close_reason: All 5 subtasks landed: claude_state watcher, session_log parser, top-level tailer, subagent discovery, MCP subscription. Pipeline runs end-to-end.

title: Tests: status_bar_hit_test agent indicator rect with [ZOOM] prefix
description:
Add unit tests for `status_bar_hit_test` and the `StatusBarHitAreas` agent indicator rect computation in `crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome.rs`, added in tn-8fto.

## Why
The agent indicator's pixel rect shifts depending on whether the optional `[ZOOM]` prefix is rendered. Easy to introduce off-by-one offset bugs. Pure rect math, no GPU needed.

## What to cover
- Click inside agent indicator rect (no zoom) → returns `StatusBarHit::AgentIndicator`
- Click inside agent indicator rect (with [ZOOM] prefix) → still hits, accounts for prefix offset
- Click just outside left edge / right edge → returns None
- Click in the status bar but not on the indicator → returns None
- No agent present → no hit area registered

## Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome.rs (add tests near the new types)
close_reason: Unit tests added for status_bar_hit_test

title: Tests: LayoutNode extract_pane / insert_pane_at_empty round-trip
description:
Add unit tests for the new `LayoutNode::extract_pane` and `LayoutNode::insert_pane_at_empty` methods in `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/layout.rs`, added in tn-2kmk for pane zoom toggle.

## Why
Pane zoom round-trips the layout tree through extract/insert. A bug here would silently drop panes or corrupt the tree on unzoom. Pure tree mutation, easy to test.

## What to cover
- Extract from a single-leaf tree → returns pane, leaves Empty
- Extract from a deeply nested split → tree integrity preserved, other panes still reachable
- Extract a non-existent pane id → returns None, tree unchanged
- Insert into a tree with one Empty slot → fills the slot
- Insert into a tree with no Empty slot → no-op (or documented behavior)
- **Round trip**: build a 4-pane mixed split tree, extract pane B, insert it back → tree equals original (compare via collected pane ids in traversal order)

## Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/layout.rs (add `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` or extend existing)
close_reason: Unit tests added for LayoutNode extract/insert round-trip

title: Tests: region_index region_before/region_after query helpers
description:
Add unit tests for the new `region_before` and `region_after` query helpers in `crates/therminal-terminal/src/region_index.rs`, added in tn-bh9 for semantic scrollback navigation.

## Why
These methods drive Ctrl+Alt+Up/Down jump navigation. Off-by-ones in line-boundary lookup would silently mis-jump or skip regions. Pure logic, easy to test with synthetic regions.

## What to cover
- Empty region index → both queries return None
- Single region: query before its start, inside it, after its end
- Multiple adjacent regions → correct neighbor returned
- Optional `kind` filter: error-only jump skips non-error regions
- Cursor exactly on a region boundary (off-by-one edge case)
- Cursor past the last region / before the first

## Files
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/region_index.rs (add `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`)
close_reason: Unit tests added for region_before/region_after

title: Widget plugin/extension architecture
description:
Design and implement a plugin system for overlay widgets, keeping harness-specific integrations out of core therminal.

## Motivation
The codebase is already large. Adding harness-specific widgets (Claude context gauge, Codex status, etc.) to core would be unmaintainable as the agent ecosystem grows. Instead, expose a clean internal API that plugins consume.

## Design
1. **Widget trait** — plugins implement update(AgentState) + render() -> OverlayQuads
2. **Event subscription** — widgets subscribe to AgentEvent from the agent registry (already has mpsc channel)
3. **Layout contract** — widget declares preferred position/size, overlay system places it respecting pane geometry
4. **Loading** — config-driven plugin dir or built-in registry, feature-flag gated
5. **Responsive** — widgets adapt to pane size via the layout contract, not custom logic

## Scope boundary
- Core therminal: detection layer (agent registry, state inference, cadence) + overlay rendering infrastructure (two-pass GPU, layout)
- Plugins: specific widgets (context gauge, tool cards, thinking indicator, result cards)
- Plugins can also be exposed as AI skills via MCP

## Dependencies
Depends on tn-9k2 (two-pass GPU rendering) for the overlay surface.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Therminal needs a widget plugin architecture to keep harness-specific integrations (Claude context gauge, Codex status, etc.) out of core. The detection layer (agent registry, state inference, cadence analysis) is agent-agnostic; only the rendering of that data needs to be extensible.

## Task
1. Define a `Widget` trait in a new module `crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/mod.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub trait Widget: Send {
       fn id(&self) -> &str;
       fn on_agent_event(&mut self, event: &AgentEvent);
       fn on_state_change(&mut self, notification: &StateChangeNotification);
       fn needs_redraw(&self) -> bool;
       fn layout(&self, pane_viewport: Rect, cell_metrics: CellMetrics) -> WidgetRect;
       fn render(&mut self, ctx: &mut WidgetRenderContext) -> Vec<ColorVertex>;
   }
   ```
2. Create `WidgetRenderContext` that wraps access to `GridRenderer`'s overlay atlas, rect pipeline, and `pixel_rect_to_ndc()` helper
3. Create `WidgetManager` that:
   - Holds `Vec<Box<dyn Widget>>`
   - Subscribes to `AgentEvent` channel (via `AgentRegistry::take_event_rx()`)
   - Dispatches events to all registered widgets
   - Calls `render()` only on widgets where `needs_redraw()` returns true
4. Add `[widgets]` config section to `TherminalConfig` for enabling/disabling widgets by id
5. Wire `WidgetManager` into the render loop after the grid pass (in the overlay pass from tn-9k2)
6. Implement one example built-in widget (agent status badge) as proof of concept

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/agent_registry.rs:81-96 (AgentEvent), 115 (take_event_rx)
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/state_inference/types.rs:10-36 (StateChangeNotification)
- crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs:272-274 (overlay atlas/renderer), 220-223 (ColorVertex)
- crates/therminal-app/src/color_mapping.rs:191-231 (pixel_rect_to_ndc)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome.rs (pattern reference for overlay rendering)
- crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs:41-58 (TherminalConfig for [widgets] section)

## docs:update
After implementation, update crates/therminal-app/CLAUDE.md with the widget plugin architecture and trait contract.

## When Done
Close issue: `bd close tn-8hdt --reason "Widget plugin architecture implemented with trait, manager, and example widget"`
close_reason: Superseded by tn-x3pd marker protocol — printf-driven widgets need no plugin loader/ABI

title: Bell & notification support (BEL, OSC 9, agent-aware)
description:
Therminal currently detects BEL (`\x07`) via alacritty_terminal's `Event::Bell` and logs it to the JSONL event log, but does nothing user-visible with it. Terminal emulators like Windows Terminal, iTerm2, and Kitty provide visual/audible/taskbar notifications on BEL and support richer notification protocols.

## What to implement

### Phase 1 — BEL handling (low-hanging fruit)
- Wire `Event::Bell` → `winit::Window::request_attention(Some(..))` for taskbar/dock flash
- Add optional visual bell (brief screen flash/invert) as a config option
- Add `[bell]` section to TOML config: `style = "taskbar" | "visual" | "audible" | "none"`

### Phase 2 — OSC 9 desktop notifications
- Parse `\e]9;text\a` in `SequenceInterceptor` 
- Route to OS notification system (`notify-rust` on Linux, Windows toast API on Windows, macOS Notification Center)
- Surface notification text + source pane info

### Phase 3 — Agent-aware notifications (unique to Therminal)
- When `AgentStatus` transitions to `AwaitingInput`, auto-trigger a notification even if no BEL was emitted
- Configurable: `[notifications] agent_waiting = true`
- Include agent name and pane context in the notification
- This is a differentiating feature — no other terminal can do this since they don't track agent state

## Key files
- `vendor/alacritty_terminal/src/term/mod.rs:1437` — `bell()` fires `Event::Bell`
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/interceptor.rs` — OSC parsing, add OSC 9 here
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/event_log.rs` — `SessionEvent::Bell` already exists
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs` — wire Bell event to winit attention request
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/state_inference/` — agent status transitions
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Clean separation of concerns -- PaneListener AtomicBool for bell, interceptor channel for OSC 9, dual mpsc channels in AgentRegistry for notification subscribers. Building on existing patterns (chrome overlay, InterceptorConfig flags) made integration smooth.
- What was unclear: The branch already had partial plumbing from a prior commit (zoom toggle), which included bell/notification callback wiring in spawn.rs, agent_registry.rs, and pane_ops.rs. This meant some edits were redundant, but the final result is correct.
close_reason: Bell, OSC 9, and agent-aware notifications implemented. Three phases complete: BEL handling with configurable style (taskbar/visual/audible/none), OSC 9 desktop notifications via notify-rust, and agent-aware notifications triggered on AwaitingInput status transitions.

title: Reduce quadratic hotspot application work in the render loop
description:
Code review finding: the render path clones damaged cells, copies URL annotations back via repeated linear search, and applies hotspots by rescanning all visible cells. This is avoidable quadratic work in a frame-sensitive path.

Impact:
- Can add frame-time jitter on dense panes or rapid output.
- Performance scales poorly with pane size and hotspot density.

Likely file:
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs
acceptance_criteria:
- Hotspot/url application avoids repeated full scans of the visible cell buffer.
- Complexity is reduced to a row-indexed or otherwise near-linear approach for damaged content.
- Add a benchmark, profiling note, or targeted test coverage that protects the intended algorithmic behavior.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
The render loop has quadratic hotspot/URL application work that causes frame-time jitter on dense panes. Three specific O(n*m) patterns identified in code review.

## Task
Fix three quadratic patterns in the render path:

### 1. URL copy-back: O(N*M) linear search (render.rs:298-307)
The loop finds matching cells via `cells.iter_mut().find()` for each damaged cell with a hyperlink. Replace with a HashMap keyed by `(row, col)` for O(1) lookup.

### 2. Hotspot application: O(H*C) nested loop (render.rs:310-319, 325-334)
For each hotspot, rescans entire cell list to find cells in range. Instead, build a row-indexed structure: `HashMap<usize, Vec<&Hotspot>>` keyed by row, then single-pass over cells checking only hotspots on the same row.

### 3. Hotspot map rebuild: O(C) every frame (grid_renderer.rs:1042-1071)
Rebuilds `hotspot_map` by scanning ALL visible cells even when only a few rows are damaged. Only update entries for damaged rows, remove stale entries for those rows first.

### Bonus
4. Consider `Cow<>` or in-place mutation to avoid the cell clone at render.rs:287-291.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs:287-334 (cell clone, URL copy, hotspot apply)
- crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs:801-816 (row cache update), 1042-1071 (hotspot map rebuild)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hotspot/URL application avoids repeated full scans of the visible cell buffer
- Complexity reduced to row-indexed or near-linear approach
- Add a comment documenting the algorithmic complexity of the new approach

## When Done
Close issue: `bd close tn-m3on --reason "Hotspot application optimized from O(n*m) to near-linear"`
close_reason: Fixed three quadratic patterns in the render path: (1) URL copy-back uses HashMap<(row,col)> for O(1) lookup instead of O(N*M) linear search, (2) hotspot application uses row-indexed HashMap for O(H+C) instead of O(H*C) nested loop, (3) hotspot_map rebuild is now damage-aware, only updating entries for damaged rows instead of scanning all visible cells every frame.

title: Clean up timed-out pending requests in persistent daemon client
description:
Code review finding: `send_request()` times out locally, but the request remains in the client's `pending` map until a response eventually arrives or the connection closes. Under repeated slow or hung requests, stale entries accumulate and late responses become misleading.

Impact:
- Memory/bookkeeping leak for long-lived clients.
- Confusing late-response handling and warning noise.

Likely file:
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/client.rs
acceptance_criteria:
- When a request times out, its pending entry is removed promptly.
- Late responses for timed-out requests are handled deliberately and without leaking state.
- Add a test covering timeout cleanup behavior in the persistent client.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
In client.rs, `send_request()` uses `tokio::time::timeout()` at line ~204. When timeout triggers, the request_id stays in the `pending` HashMap forever. The entry is only removed when a response arrives (line ~299 in the reader task). For hung requests, this leaks memory and causes misleading late-response warnings.

## Task
1. In the reader/writer task (around line 276 where `pending` is defined), add cleanup for timed-out requests
2. The cleanest approach: after `send_request()` timeout fires (line 206), send a cancellation command through the writer channel that removes the request_id from pending. OR use a separate cleanup mechanism
3. Alternative simpler approach: in the WriterCmd::Send handler, store the request_id. After timeout, have `send_request` remove the entry from pending directly (it has access to pending via Arc clone)
4. Handle late responses for cleaned-up requests gracefully — if a response arrives for a removed request_id, just drop it silently (no warn)
5. Add a test that verifies pending map is cleaned up after timeout

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/client.rs (lines 190-208 send_request, 276 pending map, ~299 reader task)

## When Done
Close issue: `bd close tn-hjxm --reason "Pending requests cleaned up on timeout"`
## Retro
- What worked: The WriterCmd::CancelRequest approach is clean -- uses the existing mpsc channel to communicate timeout back to the connection task, no extra Arc or shared state needed.
- What was unclear: Nothing significant. The pre-existing uncommitted changes in session.rs caused CI to fail on unrelated code, but the client.rs changes compile and test cleanly in isolation.
close_reason: Pending requests cleaned up on timeout

title: Pane resize keybindings: Ctrl+Shift+=/-/0 to adjust split ratio
description:
Add keybindings to resize the focused pane by adjusting the parent split ratio:

- Ctrl+Shift+= — grow focused pane (increase its share of the split)
- Ctrl+Shift+- — shrink focused pane (decrease its share)
- Ctrl+Shift+0 — reset all split ratios to 0.5 (equal)

This allows resizing individual panes without changing font size. Useful when different TUIs have different minimum column/row requirements (e.g., shrinking btop without making everything else tiny).

The split ratio adjustment already works via mouse drag on separators. This just adds keyboard control with a fixed step (e.g., 0.05 per keypress).
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: ResizeGrow/ResizeShrink already existed with adjust_ratio infrastructure, so adding ResizeReset was straightforward -- just needed a new variant, a recursive reset_all_ratios() on LayoutNode, and wiring.
- What was unclear: The task description suggested adding ResizePaneGrow/ResizePaneShrink/ResizePaneReset as new variants, but ResizeGrow/ResizeShrink already existed. Only ResizeReset was actually missing.
close_reason: Pane resize keybindings implemented

title: Dynamic tab names from OSC 7 directory / running command
description:
Tab bar currently shows static workspace numbers ("1", "2", etc.). Should dynamically rename tabs based on the focused pane's context, similar to tmux automatic-rename:

- Use OSC 7 current working directory (already parsed by interceptor) — show basename of cwd
- Use running command from OSC 633 or process detector — show command name when a command is running
- Fall back to workspace number when no context is available

The data is already available in PaneStatus (cwd from OSC 7, command from OSC 633). Just needs to be wired into the tab bar rendering in chrome.rs.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Tab bar currently shows static workspace numbers ("1", "2"). Users can't tell what's running in each tab without switching. tmux solves this with automatic-rename from the running process/cwd. Therminal already parses OSC 7 (cwd) and OSC 633 (commands) — the data is in PaneStatus, just needs wiring to tab labels.

## Task
1. In `chrome.rs`, modify `draw_tab_bar()` (~line 986) to read the focused pane's `PaneStatus::cwd` for each workspace
2. Extract the basename of the cwd path (e.g., `/home/marci/projects/therminal` → `therminal`)
3. Display as `"1: therminal"` instead of just `"1"` — workspace number + colon + basename
4. If no cwd is available, fall back to just the workspace number
5. To get each workspace's focused pane status, add a method to `WorkspaceManager` that returns the focused pane's `PaneStatus` for a given workspace index
6. Update `TabBarInfo` struct (~line 962) if needed to carry per-tab display names

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome.rs (lines 962-1040, TabBarInfo, draw_tab_bar)
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/workspace.rs (Workspace struct, focused_pane field)
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/state.rs (PaneStatus with cwd field, line 37)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs (lines 588-622, status extraction for render)

## When Done
