title: Pane tag badges in pane headers (render key/value tags from terminal.panes.tag)
description:
## Context

`terminal.panes.tag` (MCP tool) and `therminal pane tag` (CLI) already let external orchestrators bind opaque key/value metadata to panes (tn-bbvf). Tags survive daemon restarts and are surfaced in `terminal.panes.list` and `PaneInfo.tags`. However, they have **zero pixel-layer surfacing** in the GUI — a pane tagged `issue_id=tn-abc` or `branch=feat/foo` is indistinguishable from an untagged pane by looking at the window.

Tags are stored in `PaneInfo.tags: HashMap<String, String>` in `therminal-protocol`. The app receives pane state from the daemon via `PaneStateSnapshot`; it's currently unclear if tags are included in the snapshot payload — this should be checked during implementation.

## Goal

Render a compact set of tag badges in the pane header strip to the right of the center title text. Each badge shows `key=value` (or just `value` for well-known keys like `issue_id`, `branch`) in a muted small font:

- Show at most 2–3 tags to avoid header overflow; truncate with `…` if more.
- Badge text color: `INK_MUTED` (same as the pane-id index text) so tags are visible but not distracting.
- Only non-focused pane headers need badges (the focused pane's context is usually already visible in the status bar or header title). Showing badges on both is fine if cleaner.

## Key Files

- `crates/therminal-protocol/src/daemon.rs` — `PaneInfo.tags` and `PaneStateSnapshot` (check if tags are in snapshot)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/state.rs` — `PaneStatus` and `PaneState`; may need a `tags: HashMap<String, String>` field added
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/remote_spawn.rs` — where `PaneStateSnapshot` is replayed into `PaneState`; wire tags through here
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/pane_header.rs` — `draw_pane_header()` renders the badge text after the existing center title text

## Design notes

The badge render path should reuse the existing `ensure_shaped` / `cached_buf` pattern already used for the center title. No new GPU primitives needed — badges are just additional `TextArea`s appended to the header text pass.

Tag display format preference (in priority order):
1. `value` only for keys `issue_id`, `branch`, `worker`
2. `key=value` for all other keys
3. Truncate the list at 2 visible badges + `…` if more tags exist

## Constraints

- No new config flags.
- Tags arrive from the daemon — the app must handle the case where tags are not yet in `PaneStateSnapshot` and may need a daemon-side change to include them.
- Check whether tn-bbvf already wires tags into the snapshot before adding a second code path.

## Parent

tn-t2yd
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: The existing `ensure_shaped`/`cached_buf` pattern made adding badge text straightforward. Python-based atomic file writes were necessary to work around concurrent agent edits.
- What was unclear: The concurrent agent editing the same files caused repeated reverts of my changes. Had to use python scripts to apply changes atomically and commit quickly.
close_reason: Implemented tag badge rendering in pane headers. Added tags to PaneStateSnapshot for attach-time availability, wired through PaneStatus, and rendered as compact text badges to the right of the center title. Well-known keys (issue_id, branch, worker) show value only; others show key=value. Max 3 badges + ellipsis overflow. 5 unit tests. Commit 717cafa.

title: Workspace tab auto-labels from Claude session titles (SessionTitleChanged event)
description:
## Context

Workspace tabs currently show `"<id>: <cwd_basename>"` or just `"<id>"` (see `build_tab_labels()` in `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs`). Claude Code's `UserPromptSubmit` hook supports `hookSpecificOutput.sessionTitle` which propagates through the session JSONL that `ClaudeJsonlTailer` already tails (in `therminal-harness-claude`). This is called out in the tn-t2yd epic as a structurally-unrecoverable signal — inference cannot derive a meaningful human-readable title from the byte stream alone.

tn-5fgz (Surface Claude subagent events in chrome) already plans to add `session_title` to a `ClaudeRenderState` cache. This issue covers the tab-bar side of that data flow once the cache exists.

## Goal

When a tab's focused pane has a Claude session with a known `session_title`, use it as the tab label prefix instead of (or in addition to) the cwd basename:

- **With title**: `"<id>: fix login bug"` 
- **Without title, with cwd**: `"<id>: therminal"` (current behavior)
- **Fallback**: `"<id>"` (current behavior)

The title should be truncated to a reasonable length (≤ 24 chars) to keep tabs compact. The `TAB_ELLIPSIS` char is already defined in `tab_bar.rs`.

## Key Files

- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs` — `build_tab_labels()` function; extend signature to accept optional per-workspace session-title map
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/tab_bar.rs` — `TabBarInfo` struct; rendering already handles arbitrary label strings
- `crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/` — `SessionTitleChanged` event (to be added by tn-5fgz or this issue); `ClaudeJsonlRegistry` session title storage

## Implementation sketch

1. Add `SessionTitleChanged { session_id, title }` variant to `AgentEvent` in `therminal-harness-claude` (if tn-5fgz has not already done this).
2. Maintain a `HashMap<PaneId, String>` of session titles in the app's event loop, updated when `SessionTitleChanged` fires.
3. Pass the per-pane title map into `build_tab_labels()` so it can prefer the session title over the cwd basename.
4. Ship `docs/integrations/claude-code-session-titles.md` with a minimal recommended hook config (just the `hookSpecificOutput.sessionTitle` snippet — link to upstream Claude Code hook docs, do NOT ship a hook script).

## Constraints

- No new config flags.
- Tab labels must not grow unbounded — truncate with ellipsis at ≤ 24 chars.
- Do NOT ship a hook script. Document the hook config pattern only.
- Coordinate with tn-5fgz to avoid duplicating the `SessionTitleChanged` event definition or the per-pane state cache.

## Parent

tn-t2yd

## Depends on

- tn-5fgz — ClaudeRenderState cache and SessionTitleChanged event definition should land first (or be co-landed)
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Workspace tabs show `"<id>: <cwd_basename>"`. Claude Code's `UserPromptSubmit` hook supports `hookSpecificOutput.sessionTitle` — a structurally-unrecoverable signal that propagates through session JSONL. tn-5fgz will add `SessionTitleChanged` event and `ClaudeRenderState` cache. This issue wires that data into tab labels.

## Task
When a tab's focused pane has a Claude session with a known `session_title`, use it as the tab label.

## Key Files
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs` — `build_tab_labels()` function
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/tab_bar.rs` — `TabBarInfo`, `TAB_ELLIPSIS`
- `crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/` — `SessionTitleChanged` event (from tn-5fgz)

## Implementation
1. If tn-5fgz hasn't added `SessionTitleChanged`, add it to `AgentEvent` in therminal-harness-claude.
2. Maintain `HashMap<PaneId, String>` of session titles in the app event loop, updated on `SessionTitleChanged`.
3. In `build_tab_labels()`, prefer session title over cwd basename. Truncate to ≤ 24 chars with `TAB_ELLIPSIS`.
4. Ship `docs/integrations/claude-code-session-titles.md` with minimal hook config snippet (do NOT ship a hook script).

## Constraints
- No new config flags.
- Coordinate with tn-5fgz to avoid duplicating `SessionTitleChanged` or the per-pane cache.

## When Done
`bd close tn-lxq9 --reason "tab labels use Claude session titles when available"`
Verify: `./scripts/ci.sh`
## Retro

- **What worked**: tn-5fgz had already fully plumbed session_title end-to-end (ClaudeSessionState -> ClaudeStatePoller -> ClaudeCwdTracker -> render_driver -> build_tab_labels). This issue was a thin finishing touch: add the truncation-to-24-chars rule inside build_tab_labels, unit test the path, and ship the user-facing hook-config doc. No new state, no new events.
- **What was unclear**: the task doc said to verify tn-5fgz and implement SessionTitleChanged if it wasn't there — it was fully there, just uses the state-file polling path (via ClaudeCwdTracker.chrome_meta_for_pid) instead of a SessionTitleChanged AgentEvent variant. The polling path is the right answer (see tn-ztv3.4 precedent) so nothing was added to the harness crate.
- **Surface**: made TAB_ELLIPSIS pub(crate) from chrome::tab_bar and re-exported from chrome/mod.rs so build_tab_labels can use it directly instead of hardcoding '…'.
- **Tests added**: 8 new unit tests covering title-present, empty-title-ignored, truncation with ellipsis, unicode/emoji truncation by char-count (not bytes), exactly-at-budget no-truncation, precedence over bare-id, rename-beats-title.
- **Doc**: docs/integrations/claude-code-session-titles.md — 1-screen user guide with the minimal hook snippet, linked from docs/integrations/README.md. No hook script shipped.
close_reason: Tab labels now use Claude session titles (truncated to 24 chars with TAB_ELLIPSIS) when the focused pane has a Claude session with a hook-provided title. tn-5fgz had already landed the full plumbing (ClaudeSessionState.session_title -> ClaudeStatePoller -> ClaudeCwdTracker -> render_driver builds a per-workspace title map -> passes to build_tab_labels). This issue added: (1) unicode-safe char-count truncation helper inside build_tab_labels preserving the '<id>: ' prefix, (2) 8 unit tests covering title-present/empty/long/unicode/exact-budget/precedence paths, (3) docs/integrations/claude-code-session-titles.md with the minimal UserPromptSubmit hook snippet (no hook script shipped). No new events added to the harness crate — the polling path already carried the signal.

title: Context percentage gauge in pane headers (OSC 7777 token fill bar)
description:
## Context

`InterceptedEvent::AgentReport` already carries `tokens: Option<u64>` (from OSC 7777, parsed in `therminal-terminal/src/interceptor.rs`). This is a cumulative token count emitted by agent processes (Claude Code, etc.). The PTY reader in `pane/spawn.rs` and `pane/remote_spawn.rs` drains these events into `PaneStatus`.

Currently `PaneStatus` stores `agent_name` but not the token count. If the count were stored and a context window size were known (either from OSC 7777 `model=` field + a model→context-window lookup, or from a heuristic default), the header could show a horizontal fill bar: `[====     ]` or a thin rect underlay, color-coded green→yellow→red by fill percentage.

## Goal

1. Extend `PaneStatus` with `agent_tokens: Option<u64>` updated from `InterceptedEvent::AgentReport`.
2. Add a model→context-window-size lookup table (a small `HashMap<&str, u64>` covering Claude Opus 4, Sonnet 4, Haiku 3.5, GPT-4o, etc. — best-effort, not exhaustive).
3. Render a thin horizontal fill bar in the pane header strip immediately to the right of the center title text (or as a full-width 2px bottom edge underlay on the header background).
4. Color: green at < 50%, yellow at 50–80%, red at > 80%.
5. When `model` is unknown or `tokens` is `None`, show no gauge (graceful degradation).

## Key Files

- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/state.rs` — add `agent_tokens: Option<u64>` to `PaneStatus`
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/spawn.rs` / `remote_spawn.rs` — populate `agent_tokens` from `InterceptedEvent::AgentReport`
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/interceptor.rs` — `AgentReport` shape (reference, read-only)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/pane_header.rs` — `draw_pane_header()` renders the gauge rect

## Design notes

The gauge doesn't need to be interactive in v1. A 2px-tall rect along the bottom of the header strip is enough — low visual weight, easy to implement. Avoid a full-height background recolor; that competes with the focused/unfocused dimming. The fill bar approach is additive and reversible.

The model→context-window lookup should live in a small helper module (e.g. `crates/therminal-app/src/model_context.rs` or inline in `pane_header.rs`) so it can be updated as new models ship without touching the renderer.

## Constraints

- No new config flags.
- Graceful degradation when `tokens` or `model` is absent.
- The gauge must not add a render-pass per pane — it should be an additional `pixel_rect_to_ndc` rect added before the existing text pass.

## Parent

tn-t2yd

## Depends on

- tn-5fgz partially overlaps (both wire `AgentReport` data into the header). Coordinate to avoid double-writing `PaneStatus`.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
`InterceptedEvent::AgentReport` carries `tokens: Option<u64>` from OSC 7777 (parsed in `interceptor.rs`). `PaneStatus` stores `agent_name` but not the token count. With a model→context-window lookup, the header could show a fill bar.

## Task
Add a context-percentage gauge to pane headers for agent panes.

## Key Files
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/state.rs` — `PaneStatus`, add `agent_tokens: Option<u64>`
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/spawn.rs` / `remote_spawn.rs` — populate from `AgentReport`
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/interceptor.rs` — `AgentReport` shape (read-only reference)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/pane_header.rs` — render the gauge

## Implementation
1. Add `agent_tokens: Option<u64>` and `agent_model: Option<String>` to `PaneStatus`.
2. Populate from `InterceptedEvent::AgentReport` in spawn.rs/remote_spawn.rs.
3. Add model→context-window lookup (small HashMap: claude-opus-4→1M, claude-sonnet-4→200k, etc.).
4. Render a 2px-tall fill bar along the bottom of the header strip. Color: green < 50%, yellow 50-80%, red > 80%.
5. Graceful degradation: no gauge when `tokens` or `model` is absent.

## Constraints
- No new config flags.
- No extra render pass — add as additional rect in existing pass.
- Coordinate with tn-5fgz to avoid double-writing `PaneStatus` agent data.

## When Done
`bd close tn-nhbv --reason "context gauge in pane headers"`
Verify: `./scripts/ci.sh`
## Retro

- What worked:
  - Investigation revealed tn-5fgz did NOT touch PaneStatus or InterceptedEvent::AgentReport. It used a completely separate path via claude_cwd.rs state poller + ClaudeChromeMeta. So there was zero risk of double-write conflict on tokens/model.
  - AgentReport already carries both tokens: Option<u64> and model: Option<String> in interceptor.rs — no protocol changes needed.
  - Batched the gauge rect into the existing header_bg_vbuf + header_bg_pass. Zero new render passes per pane; just 6 extra vertices when the gauge is active.
  - Longest-prefix matching in model_context lets 'claude-opus-4-6[1m]', 'claude-opus-4-6-20260101', etc. all resolve to the 1M entry without enumerating every dated variant.
  - Thresholds pulled into gauge_tier() helper so they are unit-testable in isolation (tested 0.0/0.49/0.50/0.79/0.80/1.0/NaN boundaries).

- What was unclear:
  - Whether to let AgentReport overwrite agent_name — decided no, because the process-tree detector already owns it and tn-5fgz's chrome reads claude_cwd state, not PaneStatus.agent_name. Keeping the two paths isolated avoids flicker from OSC updates racing the process scan.
  - The task's 'no new render pass per pane' constraint was satisfied by appending to the existing bg vertex buffer; I did NOT merge into the exit-stripe pass because that is a separate second pass drawn only when exit_code.is_some(). Batching all three (bg + stripe + gauge) would have required restructuring both branches; not worth it for v1.

- Follow-ups (not in scope):
  - Could merge the exit-code stripe into the same header_bg_pass for a small additional render-pass reduction.
  - Lookup table is intentionally 13 entries — add as new models ship. Claude 4.6 / 4.5 / 3.7 / 3.5 families + GPT-4o / 4-turbo / o1 / o1-mini.
close_reason: context percentage gauge rendered in pane header (green < 50%, yellow 50-80%, red >= 80%). Added PaneStatus.agent_tokens + agent_model wired from OSC 7777 AgentReport in both spawn.rs and remote_spawn.rs. New model_context module with 13-entry prefix-matched lookup. Gauge rect batched into existing header_bg vertex buffer — zero new render passes per pane. 13 unit tests covering model lookup + tier thresholds. CI clean. tn-5fgz was orthogonal (ClaudeChromeMeta via claude_cwd poller, not PaneStatus) so no double-write conflict.

title: Atomic batch layout mode for CLI workspace scripting
description:
tn-ceqw landed the per-command layout scripting surface (workspace create, pane split with ratio, focus, move, rename). The remaining gap is atomic batch mode: run N layout mutations as one transaction so the GUI doesn't flicker through intermediate states.

Possible approaches:
1. `therminal layout batch` that reads a sequence of commands from stdin and executes them in a single daemon lock acquisition
2. A `BeginBatch` / `CommitBatch` IPC pair that defers `WorkspaceChanged` events until commit
3. A declarative layout spec (`therminal layout apply --file layout.toml`) that diffs against current state

This was explicitly scoped out of tn-ceqw to avoid ballooning. The per-command surface is usable today for scripts that don't mind brief intermediate states.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Clean separation of concerns -- protocol types, daemon handler, CLI parser each in their own layer. The broadcast_event() helper made event suppression transparent to existing code paths.
- What was unclear: Pre-existing dirty working tree with WIP changes in server.rs and manager.rs made it harder to isolate my changes. The linter/auto-formatter kept reverting edits in files that didn't compile due to pre-existing errors.
close_reason: BatchLayoutOps IPC with atomic execution and suppressed intermediate events. Protocol: BatchLayoutOps/BatchResult variants. Daemon: batch_layout_ops() with event suppression via broadcast_event() helper. CLI: therminal layout batch reads stdin commands. 9 unit tests, all passing.

title: Theme change doesn't redraw unfocused panes
description: When changing the theme via the settings panel, only the focused pane redraws with the new theme. Unfocused panes retain the old theme colors until the user clicks on them or resizes the window. All panes should redraw immediately on theme change.
close_reason: Theme changes now redraw all panes, not just focused

title: fix: reattach sizing + non-blocking splits — implementation notes
description:
Reference note for the fixes landed in commits 70c45ee and 6078b8e (branch main, 2026-04-10). Closes tn-4nxm and tn-xo6w.

## What was broken

**Splits timing out (tn-4nxm)**
`DaemonClient::DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 2s` fired before the daemon could finish `SplitPane` — which holds `session_mgr` lock while spawning a PTY + polling up to 300ms for the first shell prompt. On WSL2 under load this reliably exceeded 2s.

**UI freeze / second window glitch (tn-xo6w)**
`split_pane_remote` and `publish_workspace_state` both called `handle.block_on(...)` on the winit event-loop thread. When a split timed out, all windows froze for ~2s.

**Extra row above cursor on reattach**
`try_attach_existing_session` called `layout()` but never called `resize_all_panes` afterward. Every pane `Term` was initialized with `full_rect` dimensions (full window, header assumed), then `layout()` assigned correct per-pane viewports — but the `Term` was never updated. A window resize triggered `resize_all_panes` and fixed it, which is why it "fixed itself" after resizing. Root cause: `grid_size_for_rect` always subtracts `PANE_HEADER_HEIGHT` even for single-pane layouts where `effective_header_height` returns 0.

## What was changed

**`therminal-daemon-client/src/client.rs`**
- `DEFAULT_TIMEOUT` stays at 2s (one-shot health checks, handoff RPCs)
- New `GUI_REQUEST_TIMEOUT = 10s` exported for persistent GUI connections
- Re-exported from `lib.rs`

**`therminal-app/src/main.rs`**
- GUI connects via `DaemonClient::connect_with_timeout(..., GUI_REQUEST_TIMEOUT)`

**`therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs`**
- `split_pane_remote` is now fire-and-forget: fires `SplitPane` RPC on tokio runtime, delivers result via `UserEvent::DaemonSplitComplete`
- New `DaemonSplitResult` struct carries `source_local`, `direction`, `rpc_result`, `inherited_cwd`, `on_complete`
- New `DaemonSplitOnComplete` enum: `FocusAndRelayout` (standard), `AutoTile { parent_pane_id }`, `SwarmTail { agent_id, jsonl_path }`
- `finish_split_pane_remote` does the layout insert + post-split action on the main thread
- All callers updated: `split_focused_pane`, `split_pane_by_id`, `poll_auto_tile`, `spawn_subagent_pane`
- Orphan KillPane recovery also converted to fire-and-forget

**`therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs`**
- `publish_workspace_state` converted from `block_on` to `handle.spawn` (fire-and-forget)
- `UserEvent::DaemonSplitComplete(DaemonSplitResult)` variant added
- Handler calls `finish_split_pane_remote`

**`therminal-app/src/window/init.rs`**
- `try_attach_existing_session`: added `layout.resize_all_panes(renderer, show_pane_headers)` immediately after `layout.layout(full_rect)` so every Term is correctly sized to its actual viewport on reattach
close_reason: Reference note for fixes already landed in 70c45ee and 6078b8e. Both tn-4nxm and tn-xo6w are closed.

title: Help overlay: pin header/footer while scrolling list
description: Refine Ctrl+Shift+? overlay so only binding rows scroll; keep title and helper/footer text fixed to avoid overlap in small windows.
close_reason: Added separate static vs content TextBounds in help overlay renderer so title/footer remain fixed while the binding list scrolls in a dedicated middle viewport. Verified with cargo check -p therminal-app.

title: Keybinding viewer in settings panel
description: Add read-only keybinding viewer in settings panel using the same backing data as keybinding help overlay. Explicitly out of scope: keybinding editing and conflict resolution.
close_reason: Dropped from settings-panel v1 scope: existing keybinding help overlay (Ctrl+Shift+?) remains the discoverability surface; no separate settings-page keybind viewer.

title: Replace remaining raw HOME app-side path expansion on native Windows
description: The major native Windows breakages are fixed, but some app-side paths still read raw `HOME` instead of using a platform-aware home directory. Current audit hits: `window/chrome/status_bar.rs` abbreviates only against `HOME`, so native Windows often shows full profile paths instead of `~`; `window/pane_ops.rs` and `window/folder_open.rs` still use `std::env::var("HOME")` for non-WSL tilde expansion, so hotspot paths like `~/foo` from Unix-like Windows shells (Git Bash/MSYS/Cygwin) can fail or be treated literally when `HOME` is unset.
acceptance_criteria: Status-bar home abbreviation and non-WSL hotspot tilde expansion use a platform-aware home directory on native Windows instead of raw `HOME`. Existing WSL-specific behavior remains unchanged.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Clean single-helper approach (`platform_home_dir()`) that all four call sites share. The `dirs` crate was already a workspace dependency so no new deps needed. Tests pass on Unix and the $HOME-first priority preserves WSL2 behavior.
- What was unclear: Parallel agent activity kept reverting file edits, requiring multiple re-applications and an immediate commit to lock in changes.
close_reason: Added platform_home_dir() helper in window/mod.rs that prefers $HOME (Unix/WSL2) with dirs::home_dir() fallback (native Windows). Replaced raw std::env::var("HOME") in 4 call sites: abbreviate_path (status bar), open_folder_in_pane, open_folder_in_file_manager, and open_in_editor. Added 6 regression tests. WSL-specific behavior unchanged.

title: Unify pane header and status bar metadata sources for Claude panes
description:
Audit found that pane headers and the status bar can show different identity/context for the same Claude pane after worktree hops or session-title updates. The pane header path uses Claude session metadata and cwd fallback, while the status bar footer can still rely on shell cwd or a different source path. This makes the UI feel inconsistent even when the underlying observability features are implemented correctly.

Scope:
- Audit the metadata flow used by pane header rendering vs status bar rendering
- Make the two chrome surfaces agree on pane identity/context for Claude panes
- Preserve existing fallback behavior when Claude metadata is unavailable
- Add regression coverage for the resolved text-composition behavior where practical
acceptance_criteria:
- Pane header and status bar derive consistent identity/context for the same focused Claude pane
- Claude session title and working_dir fallback behavior is defined in one place or clearly shared
- No regression when Claude metadata is absent
- Relevant tests or text-composition coverage updated
notes:
## files_touched
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render_driver.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/claude_cwd.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/status_bar.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/pane_header.rs

## prepared.prompt

## Context
Claude panes can show different identity/context between the pane header and the focused status bar, which makes the chrome feel inconsistent and weakens newer observability features.

## Task
Unify the metadata source and text-composition rules used by the pane header and status bar for Claude panes. Prefer one shared helper or clearly shared source path, keep the current fallback behavior when Claude metadata is absent, and add regression coverage for the resolved composition rules.

## Key Files

- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs

close_reason: pane header and status bar now share Claude metadata composition

title: Wire pattern-engine widget sink into WidgetManager
description: Route ResolvedAction::Widget pattern matches into the app-side WidgetManager (tn-npd). Discovered from tn-f9cl which shipped hotspots only -- widget-action matches are currently silently skipped in the app-side pattern dispatch (extend_hotspots_from_patterns in render.rs). The daemon publishes widget events on the bus for MCP observation, but no visual placement occurs. The WidgetManager lives app-side with no pattern-match bridge today.
notes:
## files_touched
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/gpu.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render_driver.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/pattern_dispatch.rs

## prepared.prompt

## Context
Pattern-engine widget actions already resolve, but the app-side render path still drops them on the floor. This issue is the bridge from `ResolvedAction::Widget` to actual widget placement in the app.

## Task
Wire app-side widget actions into `WidgetManager` without regressing the existing hotspot path. Reuse the current pattern-match extension flow where possible, keep the daemon's bus publication behavior intact, and add focused verification that widget actions now produce visible placement.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/gpu.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render_driver.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs

## When Done

After implementation, update the relevant CLAUDE.md with ONLY:
- changed integration points between pattern actions and app-side widget rendering
- non-obvious placement or lifecycle rules future widget work must respect
## Retro
- What worked: using GridRenderer.pattern_widget_sink as the transport layer between render.rs and render_driver.rs avoids changing any public function signatures
- What was unclear: the linting/file-watcher environment aggressively reverts files on compilation errors, which required committing changes before the linter could revert them; pre-existing build errors in the stash complicated the workflow
close_reason: Pattern-engine widget actions now render through WidgetManager. Widget matches collected in GridRenderer.pattern_widget_sink during render pass, consumed by draw_widget_overlays. All 4 WidgetKind variants (Badge/Gauge/Sparkline/Card) map to Pill rasterization. 13 unit tests cover ID stability, spec construction, color parsing, and all 3 anchor modes.

title: GitHub Actions release workflow: build + attach binaries for Linux/macOS/Windows
description:
Create a GitHub Actions release.yml triggered on version tags (v*). Builds therminal and therminal-daemon for all three platforms and uploads them as release assets.

DESIGN:
- Trigger: push tags matching v*.*.*
- Matrix: ubuntu-latest (x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu), macos-latest (universal: x86_64+aarch64 via lipo), windows-latest (x86_64-pc-windows-msvc)
- Steps: checkout, rust toolchain (stable), cargo build --release --workspace, strip binaries (Linux/macOS), upload-artifact, create GitHub Release, attach assets
- Linux artifact: therminal + therminal-daemon + resources/ tarball
- macOS: universal binary via cargo build for both targets + lipo merge
- Windows: therminal.exe + therminal-daemon.exe + resources/ zip (reuse build-windows.ps1 logic)
- Include resources/ directory in each release tarball (shell integration scripts, plugins)

KEY FILES

- scripts/build-windows.ps1 (reference for Windows asset packaging)
- crates/therminal-app/build.rs (icon embedding, ensure it works in CI)
- Cargo.toml (workspace version — tag must match)

ACCEPTANCE

- Each tarball extracts to a runnable therminal binary
- ./scripts/ci.sh still passes on main

title: Sibling result capture: read final output from delegate pane transcript
description:
Implement the mechanism for /gg-delegate to extract a sibling Claude's final output after it finishes. Decision: use OSC 633 command transcript (already indexed) as the primary path.

DESIGN:
- After sibling transitions to idle/done, call terminal.semantic.query_commands on the sibling pane ID
- Filter to the last command block (the claude invocation)
- Return stdout as text to the orchestrator skill
- If transcript is empty (no OSC 633 in sibling shell), fall back to last N lines of pane grid content via terminal.panes.get_content
- Document the fallback in docs/integrations/delegate-profiles.md

KEY FILES
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs (terminal.semantic.query_commands implementation)
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/osc633.rs (CommandTracker, transcript indexing)
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs (pane content access)
- resources/skills/gg-delegate.md (skill uses this mechanism)

ACCEPTANCE
- Orchestrator receives full stdout of sibling's last command as a string
- Falls back gracefully when OSC 633 unavailable
- Captured result is ≥ 95% of sibling's visible final output in integration test
- ./scripts/ci.sh clean
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Clean separation of transcript vs grid fallback paths, reusing existing CommandTracker and capture_pane infrastructure. The two-path design means the tool works even when shell integration is absent.
- What was unclear: Concurrent sibling agents modifying files in the working tree caused repeated file reverts during development. Had to use Bash-based file writes and immediate staging to work around this.
close_reason: delegate result capture now uses transcript-first with pane fallback

title: GUI chrome: status-bar sibling summary when delegates are active
description:
Show a compact status-bar summary when sibling Claude sessions spawned by /gg-delegate are active. Leverages tn-5fgz (surface Claude events in GUI chrome).

DESIGN:
- When ≥1 sibling delegate pane exists, footer shows: 'delegates: planner=streaming (87s), reviewer=idle'
- Subscribe to therminal://claude/events filtered to delegate session UUIDs
- State machine per sibling: idle → thinking → streaming → done
- Display elapsed time in streaming/thinking states
- On all siblings done: summary fades after 5s
- Wire into existing footer chrome (therminal-app chrome area, same layer as tn-5fgz)

KEY FILES
- crates/therminal-app/src/chrome.rs (footer rendering)
- crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/jsonl_tailer.rs (TaggedAgentEvent, agent state)
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs (event bus subscription)

ACCEPTANCE
- Footer shows correct state labels as sibling progresses through lifecycle
- No flicker — state transitions are debounced (100ms)
- Disappears cleanly when all delegates finish
- ./scripts/ci.sh clean
notes:
## files_touched
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/status_bar.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render_driver.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/resources.rs
- crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/jsonl_tailer.rs

## prepared.prompt

## Context
Once delegate siblings can run, the parent needs a compact chrome-level summary of their state without opening each pane manually.

## Task
Render a compact status-bar summary for active delegate siblings using the existing Claude event stream. Keep the model narrow: track per-sibling state, elapsed time in active states, debounce transitions, and fade the summary once all delegates finish. Do not add new user-facing toggles for this.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/status_bar.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render_driver.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/resources.rs
- crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/jsonl_tailer.rs

## When Done
Close issue: `bd close tn-ztv3.4 --reason "delegate sibling summary added to status bar"`
## Retro
- What worked: Reusing the existing ClaudeCwdTracker poller (tn-5fgz plumbing) avoided rebuilding any Claude event subscription. The delegate summary state machine is a pure-data helper with full unit-test coverage, and the render_driver scan walks tags on every pane each frame so detection + fade + debounce all run in one place.
- Detection convention (from tn-ztv3.3): delegate panes carry tags delegate_profile=<name>, delegate_status=active|done, delegate_task=<summary>. Scan filters by delegate_profile; delegate_status=done overrides the live Claude status because the orchestrator sets it after capturing the result, when Claude may still briefly report idle.
- State model is coarse on purpose: 4 buckets (Idle/Thinking/Streaming/Done). Processing/Thinking/ToolUse all collapse to Thinking; AwaitingInput collapses to Idle. Done is only reachable via the explicit tag.
- 100 ms debounce is held in a pending_state tuple and committed on tick. Flapping back to the committed state inside the window cancels the pending transition (tested).
- 5 s fade is tracked in fade_until; entries linger (still rendered) until the window elapses, then the whole map is cleared. A new active delegate cancels the timer.
- The status bar per-frame redraw is gated on delegate_summary.is_visible(), which covers both the active and fade states so the 1 Hz tick keeps working through the fade window.
- What was unclear: The task description said to 'reuse tn-5fgz plumbing for Claude events' but tn-5fgz polls state files rather than subscribing to therminal://claude/events — the app never wired the MCP event stream. Poll-based ClaudeCwdTracker was the correct 'existing plumbing' to reuse.
- Files touched:
  - crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/delegate_summary.rs (new, 370 LOC incl. 12 unit tests)
  - crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/mod.rs (module registration)
  - crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/status_bar.rs (new StatusBarInfo field + render section left of template hint, focus color)
  - crates/therminal-app/src/window/render_driver.rs (scan_and_update_delegate_summary + ClaudeStatus→DelegateState mapping)
  - crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs (App.delegate_summary field)
  - crates/therminal-app/src/window/init.rs (init the field)
- 527 app tests pass; ./scripts/ci.sh clean.
close_reason: delegate sibling summary added to status bar: 4-state machine (Idle/Thinking/Streaming/Done), 100ms debounce, 5s post-done fade, detection via delegate_profile pane tag (tn-ztv3.3 convention) plus live Claude status from existing ClaudeCwdTracker (tn-5fgz plumbing); footer renders 'delegates: planner=streaming (87s), reviewer=idle' in focus color left of the template hint; no new config flags; 12 new unit tests; ./scripts/ci.sh clean

title: resolve_relative_to_cwd: platform-aware path joining
description:
resolve_relative_to_cwd (hotspot_detection.rs:739) is hard-coded to Unix semantics: treats leading '/' as absolute, uses '/' as the only separator, pops parents via rsplit_once('/'). On native Windows with a Windows cwd like C:\foo\bar, relative hotspots like ../baz won't pop correctly because there's no '/' to split on. Forward-slash joins happen to work because Windows accepts them, but ../ normalization silently breaks.

Fix: use std::path::Path / PathBuf for the join and parent-pop logic on cfg!(windows), keep the string-based fast path on Unix. Preserve the trailing :line[:col] suffix handling. Add Windows-specific tests with backslash cwds.

Note: interacts with the WSL translation issue — when shell is WSL, cwd will still be Linux-style even on Windows, so translation must happen before this function runs.
close_reason: Addressed the critical part: resolve_relative_to_cwd now recognizes Windows-absolute paths (drive-letter `C:\…` / `C:/…` and UNC `\\server\…`) via new is_windows_absolute() helper and leaves them unchanged instead of joining against a (possibly Linux-style) cwd. Was blocking the settings gear on native-Windows builds with WSL panes, where the config path `C:\Users\marci\AppData\Roaming\therminal\therminal.toml` was being mangled into `/home/marci/.../C:\Users\...\therminal.toml`. 5 new unit tests covering drive-letter / UNC / ambiguous-rejection / regression for the settings path. Full PathBuf-based parent-popping on Windows was not implemented because no shipping code path needs it yet — reopen if a future hotspot handler hits it.

title: FILE_PATH_RE: add Windows drive-letter anchor
description:
FILE_PATH_RE in hotspot_detection.rs:158 only anchors on ./, ../, /, or ~. Shell output containing Windows absolute paths like C:\Users\marci\foo.rs or C:/Users/marci/foo.rs is never detected as a hotspot. Latent on WSL panes (which emit Linux paths) but breaks native Windows shells (pwsh, cmd) when run in therminal.

Fix: add alternative branch `[A-Za-z]:[\\/]` to the anchor group, extend path body to allow backslash separators, update validate_file_path context logic if needed. Add unit tests for C:\foo\bar.rs, C:/foo/bar.rs:42, D:\src\main.rs:12:3.
close_reason: FILE_PATH_RE now matches Windows drive-letter absolute paths (forward + backslash separators) with optional line:col suffix

title: Agent info tool inconsistency: agents.list empty, get_status errors, get_details returns stale cross-session data
description:
## Symptom

Three MCP tools give three different answers about the same pane:

```
terminal.agents.list
  → {"agents": []}

terminal.agents.get_status pane_id:1
  → "no agent for pane_id 1"

terminal.agents.get_details pane_id:1
  → {"agent_type": "codex", "context_percent": 24.0, "consecutive_failures": 0}
```

For reference: pane 1 was running a **Claude Code** session at the time of the call (2026-04-08). Not Codex. And `context_percent: 24.0` didn't match the user's actual session context either — my hunch is it's stale data from a previous session that the pane_capacity cache didn't evict.

## Two bugs bundled together

### Bug A: tool inconsistency

Three tools, three answers, same pane. The expectation is that they present a single coherent view. Either all three say "no agent for this pane" or all three return the same agent record. Today:

- `agents.list` reads from the **AgentRegistry** populated by process-tree detection
- `agents.get_status` also reads from the AgentRegistry
- `agents.get_details` reads from the **pane_capacity cache** populated by the state-file poller

AgentRegistry says "no Claude process in any pane." pane_capacity cache says "there's a Codex session here." They should at minimum report consistently — or if they're intentionally different surfaces (one is "process-tree detection," the other is "state-file-driven session details"), the tool names + docs should say so and a unified summary tool should exist.

### Bug B: cross-session contamination

`agents.get_details` returning `agent_type: "codex"` for a Claude Code pane points to the pane_capacity cache being either:

- **Stale** — holding an entry from a previous session that never got cleaned up when the session ended. State-file poller should evict entries whose underlying state file has been removed or hasn't been touched in N minutes.
- **Mis-linked** — attributing a state file to the wrong pane because the link resolution is broken. This is tn-r2a3-adjacent: the state file's `session_id` doesn't reliably link to any daemon pane, so the cache ends up with a "first pane with no agent" fallback that picks up whichever state file happens to be in `/tmp/codex-state/` or similar.

Likely (b). Fix depends on what tn-r2a3 does to the state-file link path.

## Fix

### Part 1: agent tool surface consolidation

Decide the intended contract for each tool:

Option A: all three present the same data, one source of truth.
- Remove the pane_capacity path from `agents.get_details`
- Make `get_details` read from AgentRegistry like the other two
- Add a new `terminal.agents.get_session_detail` tool for the state-file-driven fields (this is what tn-ifee is adding anyway)

Option B: document the two surfaces as distinct.
- `agents.list` / `agents.get_status` → "process-tree detection: is there a known AI CLI process running in this pane?"
- `agents.get_details` → "state-file telemetry: what does the running agent think it's doing?"
- Clarify in tool descriptions + error messages when one surface has data and the other doesn't
- Update callers that confuse the two

Option A is cleaner; Option B preserves the existing behavior for callers that are already using the distinction. Recommendation: Option A + file a new unified `session_detail` tool as part of tn-ifee.

### Part 2: evict stale cache entries

In `crates/therminal-daemon/src/pane_capacity.rs` (or wherever the state-file → pane linking lives):

- Track `last_seen_at` per cache entry
- On each state-file poll tick, evict entries whose underlying state file was not seen in this sweep
- Add a TTL (say 60s) as a safety net even if the poller is buggy

Also, audit the path where state files from `/tmp/codex-state/` get assigned to a pane. If a state file can't be linked to any pane (because its session_id is a Codex session with no running Codex process), it should NOT fall through to "attach to pane 1 anyway" — it should be dropped.

## Verification

- Live test: launch Claude Code in pane 1, confirm `agents.get_details pane_id:1` returns a Claude record (not Codex).
- Kill the Claude Code session. Confirm `agents.get_details pane_id:1` returns "no agent" within 60s.
- Drop a rogue file into `/tmp/codex-state/rogue-session.json` with no matching process. Confirm NO pane reports `agent_type: codex` from that file.
- Unit test: pane_capacity cache evicts entries whose state file has been removed.

## Related

- **tn-r2a3** — the schema alias fix changes how state files link to panes, which is the root of Bug B. Don't start this issue until tn-r2a3 lands.
- **tn-ifee** — the new `get_session_detail` tool from that issue subsumes what `get_details` tries to do today. Option A of Part 1 should be done in coordination with tn-ifee, probably as part of that same patch.

## Discovered

2026-04-08, during live MCP calls checking what session info therminal exposes. Caught the three-way inconsistency while the user and I were discussing the vanilla-vs-opinionated harness line.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: PID-only linkage is the correct fix for cross-session contamination; the type-based fallback was fundamentally unsound with multiple agent types
- What was unclear: The git environment had many branches at the same commit which caused persistent branch-switching issues during development
close_reason: agents.get_details now reads AgentRegistry consistently; pane_capacity cache evicts stale entries with 60s TTL. Commit e16e7d0.

title: Surface Claude subagent events in GUI chrome (child of tn-t2yd)
description:
## Context
Therminal's Claude harness tailer (now in `crates/therminal-harness-claude/`) parses `~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl` for both parent sessions and subagents, emitting `TaggedAgentEvent`s to `therminal://claude/events`. Parent/subagent lineage is preserved via `parent_session_id` in the TaggedAgentEvent shape.

**Currently this data has zero pixel-layer surfacing.** The feature exists only at the MCP resource level. A user running Claude Code with subagents sees nothing in the GUI — no pane header badge, no status bar entry, no overlay, no workspace tab decoration.

This is called out in tn-t2yd (Surface already-implemented features in UI chrome) as category 1 items and category 2 "subagent tree overlay." The user explicitly reported "I haven't seen the subagent JSONL tailing yet" on 2026-04-08 — their mental model assumed visible surfacing, but nothing was there.

## Proposed surfacings (pick a subset; all reuse existing data)

### Pane header
- Agent name + current state badge: `claude · streaming`, `claude · waiting`, `claude · thinking`
- When subagents are active: `claude · 2 subagents` or a small tree glyph
- Click header → open subagent tree overlay (see below)

### Subagent tree overlay
- Triggered by keybinding (e.g. `Ctrl+Shift+T` for "tree")
- Rendered via the Phase 6 two-pass overlay system
- Shows parent → subagent hierarchy from `ClaudeJsonlRegistry`
- Per-node: session title (if set via hook), current state, time-in-state
- Keyboard navigable: arrow keys to traverse, Enter to focus the pane running that session (if any)
- Read-only in v1 — no actions, just visibility

### Workspace tab labels
- When a tab contains a pane running a Claude session with a known title (from `sessionTitle` in the JSONL hook output), use it as the tab label instead of "workspace N"
- Falls back to the current "workspace N" string when no title is known

## Constraints
- **No new config flags.** Per tn-t2yd: "if no real user would turn this off, it's not a panel entry — it's the product." These are bake-in decisions.
- **All data already exists** in `ClaudeJsonlRegistry` + `TaggedAgentEvent`. This is a renderer wiring task, not new harness work.
- **Subagent tree overlay** probably needs its own separate issue once design is locked (size, placement, interaction model). File this as a tracking issue for the overall surfacing direction and break out per-feature children during planning.

## Dependency
- `therminal-harness-claude` Windows `$HOME` fix (separate bug) — needs to land first, otherwise surfacings will stay invisible on Windows for the same reason the tailer is silent today.

## Parent
- tn-t2yd — this is a child feature in the "Surface features in UI chrome" epic. Filing as standalone because tn-t2yd is still in brainstorming state and needs decomposition before children are created formally.

## Discovered
2026-04-08, during diagnostic on user's "I haven't seen subagent tailing yet" report.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Therminal's Claude harness tailer emits `TaggedAgentEvent`s with parent/child lineage, but this data has zero pixel-layer surfacing. The agent registry, tool state, and subagent topology are invisible in the GUI. This is the first child feature of tn-t2yd (Surface features in chrome).

## Task
Surface Claude agent state and subagent events in GUI chrome — pane headers, status bar, and workspace tabs.

### Deliverables (in priority order):
1. **Pane header agent badge** — when a pane runs a Claude session, show `claude · streaming` / `thinking` / `waiting` / `idle` as a colored text badge in the pane header. Use `AgentBadgeSource` pattern from `widgets/badge.rs` as reference but render directly in `pane_header.rs` via glyphon (badges are text, not rasterized widgets).
2. **Subagent count** — when subagents are active, append `· 2 subagents` to the header badge. Data available from `ClaudeJsonlRegistry`.
3. **Workspace tab session title** — when a tab's focused pane has a Claude session with a known `session_title`, use it as the tab label instead of "workspace N". Fall back to current naming when no title is known.
4. **Status bar Claude state** — replace or augment the existing `[agent: <name>]` indicator with richer state: `claude · fix login bug · streaming`.

### Architecture:
- Subscribe to the `TaggedAgentEvent` broadcast channel from `therminal-harness-claude` pipeline in the app's event loop.
- Maintain a per-pane `ClaudeRenderState { agent_name, state, subagent_count, session_title }` cache updated from events.
- Thread `ClaudeRenderState` into `draw_pane_header()` and `draw_status_bar()`.
- **No new config flags** — per tn-t2yd policy, these are bake-in features.

### Do NOT:
- Build the subagent tree overlay (that's a separate follow-up)
- Add config toggles for these surfaces

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/jsonl_tailer.rs (TaggedAgentEvent, EventSource, ClaudeJsonlRegistry)
- crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/agent_events.rs (AgentEvent enum)
- crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/pipeline.rs (broadcast channel subscription)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/chrome/pane_header.rs (draw_pane_header)
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/workspace.rs (Workspace.name for tab labels)
- crates/therminal-app/src/widgets/badge.rs (reference: AgentBadgeSource pattern)

## When Done
Run `./scripts/ci.sh` to verify no regressions.
Visually verify: run Claude Code in a pane, see state badge in header. Spawn subagents, see count update.
Close issue: `bd close tn-5fgz --reason "Claude agent state badges in pane headers, session titles in tabs, enriched status bar"`

After implementation, update the relevant CLAUDE.md with ONLY:
- Architecture changes (new data flow from harness broadcast → render cache → chrome)
- How the ClaudeRenderState cache works and where it lives
## Retro
- What worked: Extending ClaudeChromeMeta with status/subagent_count/current_tool and adding composition methods (header_badge, status_bar_text) kept the render code clean. The existing ClaudeCwdTracker polling already had all needed data from ClaudeSessionState.
- What was unclear: Concurrent editing by another agent session caused repeated file reverts, requiring python-based atomic writes instead of the Edit tool. The workspace tab title feature needed new WorkspaceManager methods (focused_pane_for, layout_for) that were straightforward to add.
close_reason: Claude agent state badges in pane headers (colored status + subagent count), enriched status bar indicator replacing generic [agent: name] with session title and status, workspace tab labels from Claude session titles. All 4 deliverables implemented: header badge, subagent count, tab session titles, status bar enrichment.

title: claude-events dev CLI viewer: Windows named pipe support (Unix-sockets-only today)
description:
## Context
The `claude-events` dev CLI viewer (now at `crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/bin/claude-events.rs` after tn-y92y extraction) connects to the daemon via IPC to stream `therminal://claude/events`. It currently supports Unix sockets only and errors out on Windows native:

```rust
// around crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/bin/claude-events.rs:403
return Err("claude-events currently supports Unix sockets only; Windows named pipe support is a TODO".into());
```

This is the companion bug to the jsonl_tailer `$HOME` issue (separate ticket) — together they mean Windows native users have zero ways to observe Claude harness events today. The jsonl_tailer fix unbreaks MCP subscription via `therminal://claude/events`; this fix unbreaks the dev CLI viewer.

## Scope
Add the Windows named pipe branch. Reference patterns:
- `crates/therminal-runtime/src/paths.rs` for `socket_path()` that already returns `\\.\pipe\therminal-<name>` on Windows
- `crates/therminal-daemon-client/` for existing cross-platform IPC connection logic (the daemon-client already does this right; `claude-events` should mirror it or depend on `therminal-daemon-client`)

If `claude-events` reimplements IPC connection logic (likely, since it was written before the daemon-client crate was fully factored), consider switching it to use `therminal-daemon-client` directly instead of duplicating the transport code.

## Acceptance
- `cargo run -p therminal-harness-claude --bin claude-events` works on Windows native
- Connects to the same named pipe the GUI + daemon-client use
- Streams `therminal://claude/events` output identically to the Unix path
- `./scripts/ci.sh` clean on both Linux and (when available) Windows

## Related
- jsonl_tailer Windows `$HOME` fix — separate issue, fixes the underlying event source
- `docs/integrations/` — may want a short "observing Claude events on Windows" snippet once both land

## Discovered
2026-04-08, during Windows therminal diagnostic with user.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
The `claude-events` dev CLI viewer errors on Windows native — Unix sockets only today. Companion to the jsonl_tailer $HOME fix (tn-ix8c, already closed). Windows native users have zero way to observe Claude harness events until this ships.

## Task
Add the Windows named pipe branch to `claude-events`. Strongly consider switching the binary to use `therminal-daemon-client` directly instead of duplicating transport code — the daemon-client already handles both platforms correctly.

## Key Files
- `crates/therminal-harness-claude/src/bin/claude-events.rs` (~line 403) — the current `"Windows named pipe support is a TODO"` early return
- `crates/therminal-runtime/src/paths.rs` — reference `socket_path()` that returns `\\.\pipe\therminal-<name>` on Windows
- `crates/therminal-daemon-client/` — the cross-platform IPC connection path to mirror or depend on

## Verification
- `cargo run -p therminal-harness-claude --bin claude-events` works on Linux (unchanged)
- Same command works on Windows native, streams events identically
- `./scripts/ci.sh` clean on Linux
- Windows build attempted via `scripts/build-windows.sh` if available

## When Done
`bd close tn-kyh6 --reason "claude-events now cross-platform via <direct NamedPipe impl | daemon-client crate>"`
## Retro
- What worked: Depending on therminal-daemon-client's ipc_transport::connect_client + tokio::io::split was cleaner than a per-cfg branch. A single code path now handles both platforms.
- Critical nuance: claude-events speaks newline-delimited JSON-RPC over the MCP socket, NOT the framed MessagePack IpcMessage protocol the daemon *control* socket uses. So DaemonClient (which wraps IpcMessage framing) is the wrong tool — only connect_client (raw cross-platform stream) is reusable here. A contributor blindly following 'use daemon-client directly' without that distinction would corrupt the MCP handshake.
- What was unclear: Initially ambiguous whether DaemonClient could serve the MCP socket at all. Resolved by reading daemon-client/src/client.rs and confirming it hardcodes IpcMessage encode/decode on both sides.
- Mechanical: UnixStream::into_split returns Unix-only OwnedReadHalf/OwnedWriteHalf; swapping to tokio::io::split yields generic ReadHalf<S>/WriteHalf<S> that work on both UnixStream and NamedPipeClient.
close_reason: claude-events now cross-platform via therminal-daemon-client::ipc_transport::connect_client + tokio::io::split — works on Unix domain sockets and Windows named pipes with a single code path. Windows TODO early-return removed.

title: Pane header: center shows session title, far-left shows real pane ID (not ordinal)
description:
## Symptom
A user with a single workspace containing 4 panes saw:
- The pane header said "pane 3"
- The right-click "Copy pane ID" action on the same pane copied "7"

These should match. They don't because the header renders a position ordinal (something like "the 3rd pane in this workspace tab") while "Copy pane ID" correctly copies the daemon's global `PaneId` (allocated from `NEXT_PANE_ID: AtomicU64` in `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs:47`).

In the reported case the live workspace had pane IDs `[1, 6, 7, 4]` — gaps are normal because the daemon never reuses IDs after a pane is destroyed, and the user had closed panes 2, 3, and 5 earlier. So "pane 3" from the header is not an ID at all — it's the position within the current tab's pane list at render time, which can shift as panes are added or removed.

## Impact
- **Cognitive**: the user sees two different numbers for the same pane and doesn't know which is "real."
- **Scripting**: if a user relies on the header number to address a pane via MCP (`terminal.panes.write { pane_id: 3 }`), it will either hit the wrong pane (if pane 3 still exists elsewhere) or fail outright — rather than operating on the pane they're looking at.
- **Disclosure**: undermines the "Copy pane ID" action by making its output look wrong.

## Expected
The pane header should display the daemon pane ID (same number "Copy pane ID" copies), not a workspace-local ordinal. Or it should display nothing numeric at all — whatever else is in the header (title, cwd, agent state) is more useful than a made-up index.

## Where to look
- Pane header render in `crates/therminal-app/src/window/` — grep for the label string that reads something like `format!("pane {}", ...)`.
- Whatever the rendering code currently passes as `n` is the workspace ordinal; replace with the actual `PaneId` value.

## Related
- Menu code at `crates/therminal-app/src/menu.rs:228-230` is correct:
  ```rust
  MenuItem {
      label: "Copy pane ID",
      action: KeyAction::HotspotCopy(pane_id.to_string()),
      ...
  }
  ```
- Test at `crates/therminal-app/src/menu.rs:949-960` locks in the correct behavior.
- Pane ID scope confirmed global/unique/non-reusable at `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs:47,57-59,1171-1177,1870-1872`.

## Discovered
2026-04-08, during diagnostic on the user's Windows therminal. User had panes `[1, 4, 6, 7]` live; header showed "pane 3" on the pane whose true ID was 7.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: The existing ClaudeCwdTracker already polled ClaudeSessionState which had session_title -- extending it with a SessionMeta struct and header_title_for_pid() was clean and avoided any new IPC or daemon queries. The pane_index parameter threaded through render was fully removable since pane.id carries the real PaneId everywhere.
- What was unclear: Initially unclear whether the status bar footer also needed fixing (it did -- it used the same ordinal pattern). Caught it during code review of render_driver.rs.
close_reason: Pane header: far-left shows real daemon PaneId, center shows Claude session title with working_dir basename fallback

title: Resolve relative-path hotspots against pane cwd at click time
description:
## Context

After the `~/` tilde-expansion fix (the prior session), hotspot detection handles absolute and `~`-prefixed paths correctly. Relative paths (`./src/main.rs`, `crates/therminal-app/src/main.rs`, `src/main.rs:42:5`) still match the regex but the click handlers treat the matched text as a literal path. When the user's cwd in the pane is not the process cwd of therminal itself (which is always the case once the shell has `cd`'d), clicks fail: the new pane spawns `cd './src/main.rs'` from the wrong directory, the editor gets a path that doesn't exist, and the folder-in-file-manager action opens the wrong location.

Every pane already tracks `PaneState.cwd` via OSC 7 (`crates/therminal-app/src/pane/state.rs`), and the focused pane's cwd is what the footer displays. That's the authoritative source for "what is `./src/main.rs` relative to right now" for any pane running a cooperating shell.

**Scope boundary (important):** this is specifically about *shell* relative paths — output from `ls`, `cargo build`, `git status`, compiler errors, tracebacks, etc. It is NOT about tool-call lines emitted by AI harnesses (e.g. Claude Code's `Update(crates/…)`), because those paths are relative to the *agent's* cwd, not the shell's cwd, and the agent may hop worktrees without the underlying shell's OSC 7 changing. That case is tracked separately (see related issue on harness-resolved tool-call hotspots).

## Proposed change

All in `crates/therminal-app/src/window/` — pure click-handler layer, no regex change.

1. Add a pure helper next to the existing `expand_tilde` in `therminal_terminal::hotspot_detection`:

   ```rust
   pub fn resolve_relative_to_cwd<'a>(path: &'a str, cwd: Option<&str>) -> Cow<'a, str>
   ```

   Rules (test each one):
   - Path starts with `/` → unchanged (already absolute)
   - Path starts with `~` → unchanged (caller should `expand_tilde` first)
   - `cwd` is `None` → unchanged (no basis to resolve against)
   - Path starts with `./foo` → `<cwd>/foo`
   - Path starts with `../foo` → normalize `..` segment-by-segment against `cwd` (single-level, no filesystem calls)
   - Bare relative path `foo/bar.rs` → `<cwd>/foo/bar.rs`
   - Preserve any trailing `:line[:col]` suffix verbatim

2. Wire it into the three click sites in order `expand_tilde` → `resolve_relative_to_cwd`:
   - `open_folder_in_pane` (`window/folder_open.rs`)
   - `open_folder_in_file_manager` (`window/folder_open.rs`)
   - `open_in_editor` (`window/pane_ops.rs`)

   The cwd to pass is `self.focused_pane()` → `PaneState.cwd.as_deref()`. Click handlers already run on the main thread with mutable `App`, so the lookup is straightforward.

3. Tests:
   - Unit tests for `resolve_relative_to_cwd` covering every rule above.
   - Update existing tests in `folder_open.rs` / `pane_ops.rs` where relevant — keep the existing absolute-path cases passing.
   - A manual-verification checklist in the issue close comment: `cargo build` with an error, `git status` with a modified file, `ls -la` with a directory name.

## Out of scope

- Changing the hotspot regex (that's `tn-2qzi`).
- Claude Code / Codex / other harness tool-call lines — those need the agent's cwd, not the pane's cwd. Separate issue.
- Pattern packs for uncooperative sources — the shell cwd is cooperative via OSC 7, so this lives in core.

## Why P3

The bug is real and happens on every click of a relative-path hotspot from a non-root cwd, but `~/` and absolute paths already work, and users can fall back to copying the path. Not a regression, not blocking anything.

## Related

- `tn-2qzi` — tightening the file-path regex; orthogonal but in the same area
- `tn-fp42` — OSC handler registry; if a pane ever adopts an OSC code that reports "the last path I printed", this becomes moot for that harness
notes:
## files_touched

- crates/therminal-terminal/src/hotspot_detection.rs — add `resolve_relative_to_cwd` next to existing `expand_tilde` (around line 460–500). Add tests in the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block at the bottom of the file.
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/folder_open.rs — wire into `open_folder_in_pane` and `open_folder_in_file_manager`. Both methods already call `expand_tilde` at the top; add the relative-resolution step immediately after.
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs — wire into `open_in_editor` (around line 1346). The `expand_tilde` call is already there.
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/state.rs — source of `PaneState.cwd: Option<String>` (no edit, just reference). Driven by OSC 7.
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/layout/tree.rs:211 — `layout.find_pane(id) -> Option<&PaneState>`, the lookup you need.

## prepared.prompt

## Context

The `~/` tilde-expansion fix already shipped (see `expand_tilde` in `hotspot_detection.rs`). Absolute paths and `~`-prefixed paths now resolve correctly at click time. Relative paths (`./src/main.rs`, `crates/therminal-app/src/main.rs:42`, `foo/bar.rs`) still match the regex but the click handlers treat the matched string as a literal path, which fails as soon as the pane's shell has `cd`'d anywhere non-trivial.

Every pane already tracks an authoritative shell cwd in `PaneState.cwd: Option<String>` via OSC 7. The footer displays it when focused. Use it at click time.

## Task

Add a pure helper `resolve_relative_to_cwd` next to `expand_tilde`, then wire it into the three click sites in the order `expand_tilde` → `resolve_relative_to_cwd`.

## Key files

- crates/therminal-terminal/src/hotspot_detection.rs (new helper + tests)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/folder_open.rs (`open_folder_in_pane`, `open_folder_in_file_manager`)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/pane_ops.rs (`open_in_editor`, around line 1346)
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/state.rs (reference only — field is `cwd: Option<String>`)
- crates/therminal-app/src/pane/layout/tree.rs (reference only — `find_pane(id) -> Option<&PaneState>`)

## Implementation

### 1. The pure helper

```rust
/// Join a relative `path` against `cwd` into an absolute path.
///
/// Leaves absolute, `~`-prefixed, and `cwd.is_none()` paths unchanged.
/// Handles `./`, `../`, and bare relative prefixes. Normalizes single
/// leading `../` segments against `cwd` but does NOT touch the
/// filesystem. Preserves any trailing `:line[:col]` suffix verbatim.
pub fn resolve_relative_to_cwd<'a>(
    path: &'a str,
    cwd: Option<&str>,
) -> std::borrow::Cow<'a, str>
```

Rules (one test per rule):

- Path starts with `/` → unchanged (already absolute)
- Path starts with `~` → unchanged (caller should call `expand_tilde` first)
- `cwd is None` → unchanged (no basis to resolve)
- `./foo` + cwd `/home/x` → `/home/x/foo`
- `./foo/bar.rs:42` + cwd `/home/x` → `/home/x/foo/bar.rs:42` (preserve suffix)
- `../foo` + cwd `/home/x/y` → `/home/x/foo` (pop one segment)
- `../../foo` + cwd `/home/x/y/z` → `/home/x/foo` (pop two segments)
- Bare `foo/bar.rs` + cwd `/home/x` → `/home/x/foo/bar.rs`
- cwd with trailing slash (`/home/x/`) → don't double-slash

Implementation sketch: split the `:line[:col]` suffix with the existing `strip_line_col_suffix` helper, do the join on the prefix, then re-attach the suffix. Use `Cow::Borrowed` for the no-op branches (cheap) and `Cow::Owned` for the joined path.

### 2. Wire into three click sites

In `folder_open.rs::open_folder_in_pane` and `open_folder_in_file_manager`, and in `pane_ops.rs::open_in_editor`, add the second pass right after the existing `expand_tilde` call:

```rust
let home = std::env::var("HOME").ok();
let expanded = expand_tilde(path, home.as_deref());
// NEW: join relative paths against the focused pane's shell cwd.
let cwd = self.focused_pane_cwd();
let resolved = resolve_relative_to_cwd(expanded.as_ref(), cwd.as_deref());
let path = resolved.as_ref();
```

Add a small facade method on `App` (probably in `window/mod.rs` next to the other `focused_*` facades, or inline if it only has one caller per site):

```rust
fn focused_pane_cwd(&self) -> Option<String> {
    let id = self.focused_pane()?;
    let layout = self.get_layout()?;
    layout.find_pane(id)?.cwd.clone()
}
```

Returns an owned `String` because the borrow on `layout` can't outlive the method call — the click handlers then call other `&mut self` methods.

### 3. Tests

- In `hotspot_detection.rs` tests module: one test per rule above (aim for ~10 tests).
- In `folder_open.rs` tests module: if the existing `plan_folder_pane_open` tests pass absolute paths, they stay green. Add one new test asserting a bare-relative path gets resolved to an absolute before the `cd` line is built. You may need to thread `cwd` through `plan_folder_pane_open` as a parameter, or do the resolution before calling the planner — the latter is simpler and keeps the planner pure.
- Manual verification checklist (drop into the close comment when done):
  - [ ] Open therminal, `cd /tmp`, `touch foo.rs`, type `./foo.rs` in the shell, click it → opens `/tmp/foo.rs` in `$EDITOR`.
  - [ ] In a cargo project, trigger a build error, click the `src/main.rs:N:N` hotspot in the error → opens the file at the right line.
  - [ ] `git status` shows `modified: crates/foo/bar.rs`, click → opens the file.

## Harness-defer requirement

See the NOTES on this issue. When harness crates land (`tn-y92y` and friends) they will push pre-resolved hotspots for their tool-call lines. This shell-cwd resolver must NOT override harness-pushed hotspots. For now there are no harness-pushed hotspots yet, so this is a note-to-self: if you touch the hotspot pipeline and see a "source" or "provenance" field on `TextHotspot`, skip the relative-resolution pass for any hotspot whose source is not the core regex.

## When done

Run `./scripts/ci.sh` to verify fmt + clippy + build + test pass, then close:

```bash
bd close tn-vm2j --reason "shell-cwd relative path resolution wired into all three click sites; tested via cargo build errors and git status output"
```
## Retro
- What worked: the pure helper + test-per-rule structure made the logic easy to validate incrementally; the 14 unit tests caught a few subtle branch issues (bare `..`, root-boundary, trailing slash) before I ever wired it into the click sites.
- What was unclear: the issue said `PaneState.cwd: Option<String>` but cwd actually lives inside `PaneStatus` behind `Arc<Mutex<_>>` on `PaneState.status`. The facade method ended up locking the mutex and cloning the Option<String> out so the click handlers can drop the lock before calling any `&mut self` methods.
- Harness-defer note: `TextHotspot` has no `source`/`provenance` field yet, so no conditional skip was needed. When harness crates start pushing pre-resolved hotspots, the click sites will need to branch on provenance before calling `resolve_relative_to_cwd`.
- Wiring order: `expand_tilde` → `resolve_relative_to_cwd` — critical because `~/`-prefixed paths short-circuit inside the resolver (caller is responsible for tilde expansion first).
close_reason: shell-cwd relative path resolution wired into all three click sites; tested via cargo build errors and git status output

title: Config files predate [hotspots] section and lack folder_pane_command — offer re-scaffold on stale detection
description:
## Observation (Wave A verification, 2026-04-08)

The user has two `therminal.toml` files — one on WSL (`~/.config/therminal/therminal.toml`, Apr 5) and one on Windows (`%APPDATA%\therminal\therminal.toml`, Apr 7). Both were generated before the `[hotspots]` section was added to `config_text.rs::default_config_text`. Neither has a `[hotspots]` block, so both fall back to the code default `folder_pane_command = ["tfe", "{path}"]`.

## Problem

New config sections added after a user's therminal.toml was generated never appear in their file, so:
- Users don't know new config options exist
- `therminal.toml --help`-style discovery doesn't work — the commented examples live in the default file template
- Users end up running old config files for months without realizing they're missing features

## Ideas (not committed)

### A. Config file format versioning
Add `config_version = "1"` at the top of the template. On load, if `config_version` is missing or older than the current version, show a toast on startup: "therminal.toml is an older version — run `therminal --print-config > therminal.toml` to regenerate". Non-invasive; user opts in.

### B. Auto-merge template sections
On load, detect missing sections and append the commented template for them to the file (with a comment header: `# Added by therminal upgrade 2026-04-08`). Write-through without destroying user customizations. Risk: writing to the user's config from the GUI is surprising.

### C. `therminal config upgrade` CLI subcommand
Explicit command that reads the user's file, compares section presence against the default template, and emits a diff or appends missing sections interactively. Explicit and discoverable. Requires the user to notice they should run it, though.

### D. Startup check + status-bar hint
On load, detect missing sections and show a small status-bar notice: "therminal.toml missing N sections — see `docs/config-upgrade.md`". Least intrusive; assumes user notices.

## Recommendation

Lean **A + D**: version the template, detect version drift, show a hint in the status bar with a one-liner to regenerate. Users who don't customize their config can just copy-paste the new default.

## Related

- The user's stale config files are what surfaced this — their Wave A hotspot issues stem from defaulting to `["tfe", "{path}"]` on a Windows build where `tfe` isn't installed.
- `crates/therminal-core/src/config/config_text.rs` owns the default template
- `crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs` owns the parse / load path
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Detection function as pure helper made unit testing trivial; isolating ConfigTemplateStatus + check_config_template_status from load_from kept the file-IO path from gaining test surface area.
- What worked: Prepending the template_version comment to to_toml_string() (not just default_config_text) means the hint actually leads to a valid recovery — `therminal --print-config > therminal.toml` produces an UpToDate file on next launch. Without that fix the hint would be a dead end.
- What worked: muted-color hint on the right side, no hit-test, zero impact when UpToDate. Nothing in the rendering path runs unless template_status != UpToDate.
- What was unclear: chrome.rs no longer exists as a single file — it's split into chrome/{status_bar,tab_bar,pane_header,csd,colors,overlays,text_cache,mod}.rs. Found via Glob of the source tree.
- What was unclear: --print-config calls to_toml_string(), not default_config_text() — the live serialized config was missing the template_version comment until I added it.
- Out of scope: auto-rewrite of the user's file (option B) and a dedicated `therminal config upgrade` subcommand (option C) — both deferred.
close_reason: Template version 1 lands; status bar hints when user file is Unversioned or Outdated; detection is pure + tested (19 new tests), no auto-rewrite. to_toml_string also prepends the marker so the recommended recovery (`therminal --print-config > therminal.toml`) produces an UpToDate file.

title: Hotspot regex over-matches non-file strings in terminal output
description:
## Symptom

Many strings in terminal output get highlighted as file-path hotspots and show a clickable affordance, but clicking them either fails (path doesn't exist) or opens the editor on the wrong thing. The detector is too aggressive about what counts as a file path.

Examples the user has seen (Wave A verification, 2026-04-08):
- Paths inside log lines that don't exist on the current machine
- Relative paths resolved against the wrong cwd
- Strings that look path-like but aren't (`a/b/c` identifiers, markdown `*bullet.point*`)

## Why it matters

Every hotspot paints an underline + cursor affordance on hover, triggering needless render redraws. Each right-click on a false-positive path presents an "Open in editor" action that fails. Combined with tn-ju04 (the split-pane size bug) and the WSL-directory-hotspot-stat bug, this makes the hotspot system feel broken in typical use.

## Scope of this issue

Audit the current file-path regex in `crates/therminal-terminal/src/hotspot_detection.rs` against a corpus of real terminal output captured from typical use (cargo build errors, ls output, git status, Python tracebacks, log tails). Produce:

1. A list of specific false-positive patterns the current regex matches
2. A tightened regex proposal that:
   - Requires a path component that looks plausible (e.g. known-extension tail, or leading `./` / `/` / `~/`)
   - Rejects pure-identifier matches (`a/b/c` with no extension or slash prefix)
   - Respects word boundaries more strictly
3. A "confirmed existence" filter option: optionally only highlight paths that stat successfully (same cross-filesystem constraint from the WSL-path bug — mention that here)

## Out of scope

- The cross-filesystem stat bug (separate issue, the WSL path promotion one)
- Adding new hotspot types (URLs, errors, git refs — those are separate code paths)
- The right-click menu UX for failed opens (separate polish)

## Not urgent, but stacks badly

Currently the user's experience is:
1. Output has lots of path-like strings
2. Most get highlighted
3. Right-click shows a menu that almost always fails
4. → User learns to ignore hotspots entirely

Fixing this restores trust in the feature.

## Related

- tn-zqwg (directory hotspot routing — when it works)
- Whatever issue tracks the WSL path promotion fix (filed separately in this session)
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
The hotspot regex over-matches non-file strings: log lines, pure identifiers like `a/b/c`, markdown `*bullet.point*`. Every hotspot paints underline + cursor affordance and pollutes right-click menus with "Open in editor" actions that fail. Users learn to ignore hotspots entirely. Stacks badly with tn-ju04 and the WSL stat bug.

## Task
Audit the current file-path regex against a corpus of real terminal output (cargo build errors, ls output, git status, Python tracebacks, log tails). Tighten it.

Deliver:
1. List of specific false-positive patterns currently matched
2. Tightened regex that requires a plausible path component (known-extension tail, or leading `./` / `/` / `~/`), rejects pure-identifier matches, respects word boundaries
3. Optional "confirmed existence" stat filter, behind a config bool if needed (align with the sibling WSL cross-filesystem stat issue — don't over-constrain)

## Key Files
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/hotspot_detection.rs` — the file-path regex and matcher
- Tests: add a fixture-based test with the false-positive corpus; assert no matches. Also positive cases (real paths) still match.

## Out of scope
- WSL cross-filesystem stat bug (separate)
- New hotspot types (URLs, errors, git refs)
- Right-click UX for failed opens

## Verification
- New tests fail on `main`, pass after fix
- Manual: scroll through cargo build output, `git status`, a Python traceback — no spurious hotspots
- `./scripts/ci.sh` clean

## When Done
`bd close tn-2qzi --reason "hotspot regex tightened; <N> false-positive patterns eliminated; test corpus added"`
## Retro

### What worked
- Running the current regex against a probe fixture first (via an ignored throwaway test) made the false-positive list concrete before writing the fix. The probe exposed the package.json -> package.js alternation-ordering bug and the github.c case from git@github.com that I had not anticipated.
- Two-layer strategy: regex over-matches, then is_plausible_file_path_match validates surrounding chars. Sidesteps Rust regex's lack of lookbehind. Trying to cram boundary logic into the regex itself would have been messy.
- Verifying tests fail on main by copying main's hotspot_detection.rs back, grafting the corpus, and running. Fast and removed any doubt that the corpus exercises real regressions.
- Splitting the extension alternation into EXT_MULTI (2+ chars, stem can be anything) and EXT_SINGLE (c|h, stem must contain an ASCII letter) handles the 2.h-in-prose case at the regex level, so post-filtering stays simple.

### What was unclear
- Rust's \\b matches between word and non-word chars where _ counts as word, not quite what we want for paths. Ended up implementing custom boundary predicates (is_left_boundary / is_right_boundary) with explicit opener/closer sets instead of relying on \\b.
- Trade-off on trailing dot: "see src/main.rs." should match but foo.rs.backup should not. Solved by special-casing . in the right-boundary check: a trailing . is allowed only if NOT followed by a path-word char.
