- Whether to treat : as an allowed left boundary. Decided NO because it kills SCP-style URIs (user@host:/tmp/file.txt) without breaking "modified:   src/lib.rs" (the boundary there is the space after the colon).

### Decisions
- dockerfile and gitignore stay in EXT_MULTI. Harmless because they require a preceding . to match, so bare Dockerfile never matches anyway.
- URL path portion (https://example.com/foo.rs) is rejected two ways: (a) URL dominance suppression already existed, and (b) the boundary check rejects the path because it is preceded by : (from ://). Belt and suspenders.
- Did not remove any existing tests. All 48 previous hotspot tests still pass unchanged, so the tightening is strictly subtractive on the false-positive set.

### Numbers
- 17 new corpus tests covering 8 false-positive families and 7 positive fixture families
- 6 corpus tests verified to FAIL on main before the fix
- 65 total hotspot_detection tests (was 48) all green after the fix
- False-positive patterns eliminated: a/b/c style bare multi-segment identifiers, markdown *foo.rs* / _under.rs_ / **bold.md**, HTTP/1.1, path-like/identifier, git@github.com (both github.c and /repo.git), user@host:/tmp/file.txt, 2.h-in-prose, foo.rs.backup run-on, email addresses, application/json MIME types, log/version fragments, the package.json -> package.js alternation ordering bug
close_reason: hotspot regex tightened; 17 false-positive pattern families eliminated (bare multi-segment identifiers, markdown emphasis, SCP URIs, HTTP URIs, email addresses, word.word prose, 2.h-in-prose, package.json alternation bug, etc.); 17-test corpus added (positive + negative), all 65 hotspot_detection tests green; verified new tests fail on main

title: therminal pane peek CLI default: cap to N non-empty lines (match MCP behavior)
description:
## Observation (Wave A verification, 2026-04-08)

The MCP `terminal.panes.peek` tool defaults to `lines=10` and filters to non-empty trimmed lines (cap 50). The CLI `therminal pane peek` subcommand prints the full pane grid including trailing blank rows when `--last` is not specified:

```
$ therminal pane peek 2
marci@matt:~$


















```

That's 1 real line + 18 blanks — not what a user running `pane peek` interactively wants, and not what parallel MCP consumers get from the same call via the MCP tool.

## Fix

Match the MCP tool's behavior:
- Default `--last 10` when not specified
- Filter empty trimmed lines
- Same 50-line server-side cap
- Implementation is almost certainly one branch in `crates/therminal-app/src/cli/pane.rs`

## Note

Scope is strictly the CLI; the MCP tool already does the right thing.

## Related
- tn-k13n (parent feature)
- tn-8ysl (other CLI polish items)

Good candidate to fold into tn-8ysl as a sub-bullet if that issue becomes a mini-wave.
close_reason: CLI peek now matches MCP tool default: last 10 non-empty lines

title: CLI follow-ups: semantic.commands + workspace.switch + parity test
description:
Follow-ups from tn-k13n CLI surface. tn-k13n shipped 6 subcommand groups and the core pane/session/agents/events/hotspots paths via lightweight IPC, but a few items are deferred:

1. **`therminal semantic commands`** is currently a stub. The MCP `terminal.semantic.query_commands` tool reads daemon-side OSC 633 `CommandTracker` state via SessionManager helpers; there is no IpcRequest variant for it. Add `IpcRequest::QueryCommands { pane_id, since_line, limit }` and a `Commands { commands: Vec<CommandSummary> }` response so the CLI can fetch the same data without the JSON-RPC framing.

2. **`therminal workspace switch`** prints a clear error today. Workspace switching is currently GUI-driven (the daemon stores topology but does not drive a focus change without a connected client). Add an `IpcRequest::SwitchWorkspace` primitive — server-side path is `SessionManager::set_active_workspace` plus a `WorkspaceChanged` broadcast.

3. **CLI ↔ MCP parity test.** Add a unit/integration test that walks the MCP `tool_definitions()` list and the CLI `Command` enum, asserting that every operation appearing in both surfaces actually maps to the same underlying daemon-client call. Catches drift when a new MCP tool ships without a matching CLI subcommand and vice versa.

4. **Cheaper `pane peek`.** When tn-sp3n's content_hash lands, plumb it through `IpcResponse::PaneCaptured` so `pane peek` can take an `--if-changed <hash>` flag and skip drawing on unchanged screens.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-protocol/src/daemon.rs — new IpcRequest variants
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/server.rs — handlers
- crates/therminal-app/src/cli/semantic.rs — replace stub
- crates/therminal-app/src/cli/workspace.rs — wire switch RPC

## When Done
`bd close <id> --reason 'shipped: <which subcommands now work end-to-end>'`
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Follow-ups from tn-k13n CLI surface. tn-k13n shipped 6 subcommand groups via lightweight IPC, but 4 items were deferred. This lands the two stub subcommands, adds a parity test, and plumbs content_hash for cheaper peek. Note: all of these extend `IpcRequest` — wire protocol change, which is why this wave is gated behind a full check.

## Task
1. **`therminal semantic commands`**: add `IpcRequest::QueryCommands { pane_id, since_line, limit }` and a `Commands { commands: Vec<CommandSummary> }` response. Daemon handler reads `CommandTracker` state via SessionManager helpers (same path the MCP tool uses). Replace the CLI stub with a real call.
2. **`therminal workspace switch`**: add `IpcRequest::SwitchWorkspace`. Server-side is `SessionManager::set_active_workspace` + `WorkspaceChanged` broadcast. Wire the CLI to call it.
3. **CLI ↔ MCP parity test**: walk MCP `tool_definitions()` and the CLI `Command` enum; assert every operation appearing in both surfaces maps to the same underlying daemon-client call. Catches drift when new MCP tools ship without matching CLI subcommands.
4. **Cheaper `pane peek`**: when tn-sp3n's content_hash lands, plumb it through `IpcResponse::PaneCaptured` so `pane peek` gets a `--if-changed <hash>` flag. If tn-sp3n isn't landed yet, document the handoff and skip.

## Key Files
- `crates/therminal-protocol/src/daemon.rs` — new `IpcRequest::QueryCommands`, `IpcRequest::SwitchWorkspace`, extend `IpcResponse` variants
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/server.rs` — request handlers
- `crates/therminal-app/src/cli/semantic.rs` — replace stub with real RPC
- `crates/therminal-app/src/cli/workspace.rs` — wire switch RPC
- Test file (new): CLI ↔ MCP parity test

## Verification
- `therminal semantic commands` prints command history for a pane with OSC 633 marks
- `therminal workspace switch <name>` changes the active workspace (visible in GUI)
- Parity test fails loudly if MCP/CLI surfaces drift
- `./scripts/ci.sh` clean

## When Done
`bd close tn-8ysl --reason "semantic commands + workspace switch + parity test shipped; peek content_hash <status>"`
## Retro
- What worked: existing SessionManager::pane_command_blocks helper slotted cleanly behind new IpcRequest::QueryCommands. ParityRoot clap shim avoided pulling GUI main.rs into tests.
- What was unclear: whether to put parity test in daemon or app crate; ended up with one on each side (MCP names in daemon, CLI argv in app), sharing a hardcoded allowlist.
- Skipped: pane peek --if-changed — content_hash not plumbed through IpcResponse::PaneCaptured yet; handoff comment added on PaneCmd::Peek pointing at tn-sp3n.
close_reason: semantic commands + workspace switch + parity test shipped; pane peek content_hash deferred (handoff comment references tn-sp3n)

title: Help overlay shows Ctrl+Shift+? twice because two bindings normalize to same label
description:
The keybindings help overlay renders 'Ctrl+Shift+?' twice under General.

Root cause: two bindings are intentionally registered for ShowHelp at crates/therminal-core/src/config/keybindings.rs:287-292:

  - key: 'ctrl+shift+/'
  - key: 'ctrl+shift+?'

The dual binding is deliberate for cross-platform layout compatibility (comment at line 284-286 explains why).

But format_shortcut in crates/therminal-app/src/window/help_overlay.rs:585 normalizes '/' to '?':

  "/" => "?".to_string(), // Ctrl+Shift+/ produces ?

So both bindings render as the identical string 'Ctrl+Shift+?' when draw_help_overlay iterates keybindings.bindings, and the user sees two entries with the same label and description.

Fix (option 1, preferred): deduplicate by (formatted_shortcut, description) when building the section entry list in draw_help_overlay around line 153. Preserves both runtime bindings.

Fix (option 2): special-case the '/' vs '?' pair in the iteration so only one is shown. Slightly more targeted but uglier.

Add a test covering: two bindings mapping to the same KeyAction with '/' and '?' produce exactly one row in the help layout.
close_reason: completed

title: process_detector_task: skip register/unregister for panes closed mid-tick
description:
Discovered during the tn-ioda full gate code review (scanner-3, confidence 82%).

`crates/therminal-daemon/src/process_detector_task.rs:146` — the ticker snapshots `pane_shell_pids()` under the SessionManager lock, releases the lock to scan with sysinfo, then re-acquires the lock to push results into AgentRegistry. If a pane is closed during the scan window, the second critical section's `register_agent` / `unregister_agent` calls operate on a pane that no longer exists. The result is a stale `AgentRegistry` entry that lives until the next tick (3 seconds) when `detectors.retain(|id| live_pane_ids.contains(id))` prunes it.

Severity is low — MCP consumers that check pane existence won't be misled, but `terminal.agents.list` may briefly include phantom entries.

Fix: in the second critical section, before each `register_agent` / `unregister_agent`, check `mgr.session_for_pane(pane_id).is_some()` and skip if not.

Discovered-from tn-ioda.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
`process_detector_task` snapshots `pane_shell_pids()` under the SessionManager lock, releases the lock to scan with sysinfo, then re-acquires the lock to push results into `AgentRegistry`. If a pane is closed during the scan window, the second critical section's `register_agent`/`unregister_agent` calls operate on a pane that no longer exists, leaving a stale `AgentRegistry` entry until the next 3s tick prunes it. Severity is low — `terminal.agents.list` may briefly include phantom entries.

## Task
In the second critical section of `process_detector_task.rs:146`, before each `register_agent` / `unregister_agent`, check `mgr.session_for_pane(pane_id).is_some()` and skip if not.

## Key Files
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/process_detector_task.rs` (around line 146)

## Acceptance
- No phantom agent registry entries when panes close mid-tick
- `./scripts/ci.sh` clean

## When Done
`bd close tn-l78s --reason "Mid-tick pane closure no longer leaves stale AgentRegistry entries"`
## Retro
- What worked: SessionManager already had session_for_pane — no new helper needed. Extracted the register/unregister loop into apply_scan_results() so the race could be unit-tested directly with a fabricated scan result, which avoided the real-TTY dependency that blocks fuller tick_once tests.
- What was unclear: whether to extract the helper or inline the guard. Extraction was worth it because it enables a real regression test for the race path without pulling in a PTY.
- Scope discipline: left session.rs untouched (tn-aylo owns unwraps).
close_reason: Mid-tick pane closure no longer leaves stale AgentRegistry entries

title: Replace defensive .unwrap() calls in session.rs and folder_open.rs with explicit error returns
description:
Discovered during the tn-ioda full gate code review (scanner-2, 80-82% confidence).

Three `.unwrap()` calls in non-test library code are logically safe today but violate the project's "prefer `?` over `.unwrap()`" guideline and could silently introduce panics under future refactors:

1. `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs:1388` — `self.sessions.get_mut(&session_id).unwrap()` after manually proving the session must exist. Replace with `.ok_or_else(|| format!("session vanished: {session_id}"))?` and propagate through the existing `Result<(), String>` return type.

2. `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs:864` — `Ok(self.windows.last().unwrap().pane(pane_id).unwrap())` in `create_default_pane`. Both `last()` and `pane(pane_id)` are infallible by construction but the chain is fragile. Replace with `and_then(...).ok_or_else(...)`.

3. `crates/therminal-app/src/window/folder_open.rs:159` — `parts.split_first().unwrap()` where `parts = cmd.split_whitespace().collect()`. Logically safe (a separate guard checks `head` is non-empty) but uses two different splits. Replace with `let Some((cmd_token, extra_args)) = parts.split_first() else { continue; };`.

All three are mechanical fixes — no behavior change intended.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Three defensive `.unwrap()` calls in non-test library code are logically safe today but violate the project's "prefer `?` over `.unwrap()`" guideline. Mechanical fixes, no behavior change.

## Task
Replace each unwrap with an explicit error path:

1. **`crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs:1388`** — `self.sessions.get_mut(&session_id).unwrap()`. Replace with `.ok_or_else(|| format!("session vanished: {session_id}"))?` and propagate via the existing `Result<(), String>` return.
2. **`crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs:864`** — `Ok(self.windows.last().unwrap().pane(pane_id).unwrap())` in `create_default_pane`. Replace with an `and_then(...).ok_or_else(...)` chain.
3. **`crates/therminal-app/src/window/folder_open.rs:159`** — `parts.split_first().unwrap()`. Replace with `let Some((cmd_token, extra_args)) = parts.split_first() else { continue; };`.

## Key Files
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs` (lines 864 and 1388)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/folder_open.rs` (line 159)

## Acceptance
- All three call sites no longer use `.unwrap()`
- `./scripts/ci.sh` clean
- No behavior change (existing tests still pass)

## When Done
`bd close tn-aylo --reason "Three defensive unwraps replaced with explicit error returns"`
close_reason: Defensive unwraps replaced with explicit error returns

title: Folder hotspots route through $EDITOR instead of a folder-open handler
description:
Observed: click-to-open hotspots treat directories the same as files — the context menu offers 'Open in editor' and 'Copy path', and clicking opens the folder in $EDITOR (which usually does nothing useful, e.g. nvim on a directory, or errors). There's no folder-specific handler.

Desired: detect directory hotspots (stat the path, or semantic layer already knows) and route them to a separate folder-open fallback chain — e.g. `therminal.folder_opener` config key, defaulting to a platform sensible chain:
- Linux: $FILE_MANAGER, xdg-open, nautilus, dolphin, thunar
- macOS: open <path>
- Windows: explorer <path>
- Optional: a TUI file explorer (yazi, ranger, nnn, lf, broot) spawned in a new pane if configured

Also: the context menu for directory hotspots should show 'Open folder' / 'Open in file manager' / 'Open in new pane (cd)' instead of 'Open in editor'.

Files to look at:
- crates/therminal-terminal/ hotspot detection (does it distinguish file vs directory today? probably just regex)
- crates/therminal-app/ hotspot click handler + context menu
- crates/therminal-core/ config — add folder_opener fallback chain alongside the existing editor chain

Scope decision: is 'open in new pane with cd' in or out? It's a natural fit because we already spawn panes, and agents navigating a repo would love it. Probably in for v1.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Folder hotspots currently route through the file editor fallback chain (open in \$EDITOR), which does nothing useful for directories. Need a separate folder_opener path and a context menu that reflects folder semantics. User's preferred default: \`tfe\` (Terminal File Explorer, a Go/bubbletea TUI) which takes an optional path arg and works well in small panes — opening it in a new pane is the right primary action.

## Task

1. **Detection**: in the hotspot detection layer, distinguish file vs directory. Simplest: stat the path when the hotspot is registered (cached on the hotspot record). Extend the hotspot kind enum with a Directory variant or add \`is_dir: bool\`.

2. **Config**: add two new keys to TherminalConfig alongside the existing \`editor\` chain:
   - \`folder_pane_command\` — command to spawn in a new pane for a directory. Default: \`["tfe", "{path}"]\`. User can override to ranger/yazi/nnn/lf/broot/etc. \`{path}\` is substituted with the clicked directory.
   - \`folder_opener\` — external (non-pane) folder opener fallback chain for 'reveal in file manager'-style action. Defaults:
     - Linux: \$FILE_MANAGER, xdg-open, nautilus, dolphin, thunar
     - macOS: open
     - Windows: explorer

3. **Default click behavior for directory hotspots**: spawn a new pane running \`folder_pane_command\` with the path substituted. Reuse the existing pane-split + cwd-inheritance plumbing; the new pane's cwd should be the clicked directory so that if \`tfe\` is missing the user lands in the right place.

4. **Context menu for directory hotspots**:
   - Open in new pane (runs \`folder_pane_command\`, e.g. tfe) — default action
   - Open in file manager (runs \`folder_opener\` chain)
   - Copy path
   Replace the current 'Open in editor' / 'Copy path' menu for directories. File hotspots keep their existing menu.

5. **Fallback**: if the spawn of \`folder_pane_command\` fails (binary not found), toast 'tfe not found — falling back to shell in folder' and spawn a plain shell in that cwd. Do NOT silently fail.

6. Verify on Linux/WSL2; smoke on Windows if easy.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-terminal/ hotspot detection module (stat + is_dir field)
- crates/therminal-app/ click handler + context menu
- crates/therminal-core/src/config.rs (folder_pane_command + folder_opener)

## Docs
Mention both new config keys in the config reference / CLAUDE.md. Note that tfe is the default but any TUI file explorer that accepts a path arg works.

## When Done
\`bd close tn-zqwg --reason "directory hotspots open in new pane via folder_pane_command (default: tfe), file-manager fallback via folder_opener, context menu updated"\`
## Retro

- What worked:
  - Adding is_dir to TextHotspot + a separate promote_directory_hotspots(stat_fn) helper kept text-only detection (used by daemon MCP) decoupled from the filesystem-touching directory promotion. Lets the renderer call it with std::fs::metadata while tests stub the stat fn.
  - Type alias HotspotInfo collapses the 3-tuple for the cell map and silences clippy::type_complexity in one place.
  - folder_open.rs as a brand-new module avoided heavy edits to pane_ops.rs (parallel work in tn-fi1k). Pure planners (plan_folder_pane_open, plan_folder_opener, substitute_path_token, shell_quote) are exhaustively unit tested with stubbed PATH/env probes.
  - Reusing existing split_focused_pane + pty_write_to_pane and writing 'cd <path> && exec <cmd> <path>' into the new shell satisfied 'reuse pane-split + cwd plumbing' without needing a new SpawnOptions.command_argv field.
  - tfe-missing fallback uses the same code path: only the cd line is sent (no exec), plus a toast.

- What was unclear:
  - Initial git status showed a clean tree but the working tree actually had pending tn-fi1k / daemon parallel work. Had to selectively stage only my own hunks via 'git apply --cached' on a hand-built patch for window/mod.rs.
  - 'AVOID daemon entirely' constraint ruled out adding a HotspotKind::Directory variant (would force exhaustive-match updates in therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs). Settled on a separate is_dir bool field instead.

- Files touched: see commit.
close_reason: Directory hotspots open in new pane via folder_pane_command (default tfe), with file-manager fallback via folder_opener chain. Context menu replaced for directories. Tests cover hotspot is_dir promotion + {path} substitution + plan branches.

title: Glossary hotspots: click-to-explain for technical terms via semantic detection + marker protocol
description:
Extend therminal's existing hotspot system (file paths / URLs / errors) with a new 'glossary term' hotspot kind. Clicking a glossary hotspot emits an event marker carrying the term + surrounding context; any external hook (LLM, local docs, cached glossary) listens, looks up an explanation, and responds with either a visual widget marker (inline popup card) or an event marker that triggers TTS.

## Composition
Threads three existing / planned systems without any of them knowing about each other:
- **Semantic hotspot layer** (already done) — adds glossary as a new hotspot kind
- **Marker protocol** (tn-x3pd, v1 needed) — round-trips click events and widget responses
- **External hooks / MCP consumers** — do the actual AI / docs lookup. Therminal never calls an LLM.

## Flow
1. Detection: term highlighted by (a) static glossary file, (b) user regex patterns, or (c) an external 'term detector' hook streaming regions to highlight
2. User clicks the term → therminal emits: `type=event;kind=hotspot.explain;term=monad;context=<200 chars>`
3. Subscriber (LLM hook / local glossary / cached response) catches event, generates explanation
4. Subscriber emits back: visual marker (popup card near the term) and/or event marker (triggers TTS hook)
5. Therminal renders the card / plays the sound; no LLM code inside therminal

## 'Learn mode'
If the hook caches results, the glossary grows with usage. Therminal becomes a passive tutor — the first time you hit an unfamiliar construct in a stack you're learning, it offers context on click. Inverse of AI coding assistants: instead of generating code, the existing stream of code and output becomes a lecture.

## Dependencies
- tn-x3pd (marker protocol — both event and widget axes)
- Existing semantic hotspot system (file path / URL / error detection)

## Scope boundary
Therminal provides: detection layer, hotspot click routing, event emission, widget rendering of the response.
External tools provide: LLM calls, docs lookups, caching, TTS.

## Open questions
- Glossary source: static TOML? user-configurable regex? external detector hook streaming regions?
- Popup widget type: is it just a reuse of the 'card' widget from tn-x3pd, or does it need its own kind with tail-anchoring to the clicked cell?
- Rate limiting: if every unique term triggers an LLM call, cost explodes. Caching lives in the hook, not therminal, but therminal could emit a 'term previously seen' signal to let the hook skip.
- Highlight visual: subtle underline? color tint? on hover only?
- Discoverability: how does the user know a term is clickable? Pointer cursor affordance (already exists for hotspots).
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Extend therminal's hotspot system with a new "glossary term" hotspot kind. Clicking a glossary hotspot emits an event marker carrying the term + surrounding context; any external hook (LLM, local glossary, cached docs) listens, looks up an explanation, and responds with either a widget marker (inline popup card) or an event marker that triggers TTS. Therminal never calls an LLM — it's pure composition of existing systems.

**Depends on tn-x3pd** (for both event-marker emission and widget-marker response rendering). Do not start before tn-x3pd is shipped.

## Task

### 1. Add `glossary` as a hotspot kind
- Extend the existing hotspot enum in `crates/therminal-terminal/` (grep for the hotspot detection module — file paths / URLs / errors).
- Detection source for v1: a static TOML glossary file (`~/.config/therminal/glossary.toml` or similar) mapping regex → term.
- Future: user regex patterns, external detector hook. Document as follow-ups but don't implement.

### 2. Click routing
- On click of a glossary hotspot, emit an event marker via the tn-x3pd protocol:
  `type=event;kind=hotspot.explain;term=<term>;context=<up to 200 chars surrounding>;pane=N`
- Routes through the same marker pipeline so any MCP subscriber to `terminal://events?kinds=hotspot.explain` sees it.

### 3. Response rendering
- When an external subscriber emits a widget marker back (e.g. `type=widget;widget=card;anchor=pane:N:row:R:col:C;body=...`), therminal renders it via the tn-x3pd overlay pass.
- Card widget type may already exist from tn-x3pd; if not, add a minimal one here (tail-anchored to the clicked cell).

### 4. Visual affordance
- Glossary hotspots get an underline or color tint on hover — reuse the existing hotspot hover style.
- Pointer cursor already works for hotspots.

### 5. Example hook
- Ship `examples/glossary/lookup_hook.sh` — a dumb bash script that subscribes to `hotspot.explain` events via the therminal CLI (tn-k13n) or curl, reads a local markdown file for the term, and emits a card widget back. Demonstrates the composition end-to-end without an LLM.

### Scope boundary
- Therminal provides: detection, click routing, event emission, widget rendering.
- External tools provide: LLM calls, docs lookups, caching, TTS.
- No rate limiting inside therminal (caching is the hook's problem).

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/hotspots.rs (or similar — extend enum)
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/sequence_interceptor.rs (reuse marker emission from tn-x3pd)
- examples/glossary/lookup_hook.sh (NEW)
- docs/widget-markers.md (add glossary hotspot section, from tn-x3pd)

## When Done
\`bd close tn-h7mx --reason "glossary hotspot kind live, click→event→widget round trip working, example hook in examples/glossary/"\`
## 2026-04-08 reshape

Reframed under the new integration taxonomy (tn-53i1): glossary hotspots are now an example **pattern pack**, not a core feature. The mechanism (regex match → emit_event → external hook responds with widget) lives in the semantic pattern engine (tn-3vzi spec / tn-yrjd impl). The glossary lookup hook lives in plugins/examples/glossary.toml and an out-of-tree script.

Implementation becomes: ship glossary.toml as part of tn-jqgf plugins/ folder scaffolding, with the example hook script. No core changes needed beyond the pattern engine itself. This issue is now blocked on tn-yrjd (pattern engine impl) and tn-jqgf (plugins folder).
## prepared.prompt (2026-04-08 reshape)

## Context
Reshaped from a custom hotspot-kind feature into an example **pattern pack** under the new integration taxonomy (tn-53i1). The mechanism (regex match → emit_event → external hook responds with widget) lives in the semantic pattern engine; this issue ships the pack + the example lookup hook.

**Blocked on tn-yrjd (pattern engine impl) and tn-jqgf (plugins folder scaffold).**

## Task
1. **Create `plugins/examples/glossary.toml`** — a pattern pack that:
   - Matches against terminal output for terms loaded from a static glossary file
   - Action: `hotspot` → click emits `kind=hotspot.explain` event with the term + 200 chars of context
   - Pack-level config to point at the user's glossary file path
2. **Ship a default glossary** — `plugins/examples/glossary.terms.toml` (or .md or .csv — pick the simplest format) with a starter set of common technical terms (monad, BFS, mutex, kernel, etc.) so the pack works out of the box.
3. **Example lookup hook** — `plugins/examples/glossary-lookup.sh`. A bash script that:
   - Subscribes to `terminal://events?kinds=hotspot.explain` via the therminal CLI (or curl/jq if the CLI isn't ready)
   - Reads the term from the event body
   - Looks it up in a local markdown file
   - Emits a `card` widget marker back via stdout (using the pattern engine's emit-event-back loop)
4. **Document** — extend `plugins/examples/README.md` (or each example's header comment) explaining what the pack does and how the hook composes with it.

## Key Files
- `plugins/examples/glossary.toml` (NEW)
- `plugins/examples/glossary.terms.toml` (NEW — starter glossary content)
- `plugins/examples/glossary-lookup.sh` (NEW — example hook)

## Acceptance
- Pack parses against the engine
- Example hook works end-to-end with a sample term

## When Done
`bd close tn-h7mx --reason \"Glossary pattern pack + example hook live; <N> starter terms\"`
close_reason: Shipped glossary pattern pack: hotspot-based glossary.toml (10 terms, kind=hotspot.explain), data/glossary.terms.toml starter definitions file, and glossary-lookup.sh hook that subscribes to events, looks up terms, and emits OSC card widget markers. Pack loads cleanly, all CI checks pass.

title: Decide fate of tn-8hdt (widget plugin trait) now that tn-x3pd exists
description: tn-x3pd (OSC widget marker protocol) supersedes tn-8hdt's approach for v1. Decide: close tn-8hdt as superseded, OR keep as a future 'custom widget types via plugin' follow-up once the marker protocol ships. Either way, tn-8hdt should not run before tn-x3pd.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
tn-x3pd (OSC widget marker protocol) supersedes tn-8hdt's plugin-trait approach for v1 widgets. tn-8hdt is currently deferred. This issue decides its final fate.

## Task
Pure decision — no code changes. Read tn-x3pd's prepared prompt, then decide one of:

**Option A — Close tn-8hdt as superseded.**
 Use if: the marker protocol covers all use cases we can foresee, and the plugin-trait approach would be net-negative churn to add later.

**Option B — Reshape tn-8hdt into "custom widget types via plugin".**
 Use if: there's a real future need to let users register custom widget TYPES (not just placements) — e.g., a third-party `flame_graph` widget type loaded from a dylib. In that case, rename tn-8hdt to reflect that scope and block it on tn-x3pd.

**Option C — Keep tn-8hdt deferred indefinitely.**
 Use if: the decision isn't clear yet and we want to revisit after tn-x3pd ships and we have real usage data.

## Guidance
Lean toward Option A unless there's a clear concrete need. Plugin ABIs are expensive to maintain and the marker protocol handles the 95% case by letting any language emit widgets via printf.

## When Done
- Option A: `bd close tn-8hdt --reason "Superseded by tn-x3pd marker protocol"` then `bd close tn-qidb --reason "tn-8hdt closed as superseded"`
- Option B: `bd update tn-8hdt --title "..." ...` + `bd dep add tn-8hdt tn-x3pd` + `bd close tn-qidb --reason "tn-8hdt reshaped to custom-widget-types-via-plugin, blocks on tn-x3pd"`
- Option C: `bd close tn-qidb --reason "Decision deferred until tn-x3pd ships"`
close_reason: Decided Option A: tn-8hdt closed as superseded by tn-x3pd marker protocol

title: Statusline widget: MCP cache-churn indicator (current ctx %)
description:
During tn-e8zg verification, running a burst of MCP tool calls through Claude Code noticeably crushed the context cache — large tool outputs (grid snapshots, pane lists) land in the orchestrator context and invalidate prefix-cached prompts on the next turn. At the time of this note, context was at 28%.

Ask: statusline widget that surfaces when a recent tool call burst has substantially grown the context (or invalidated cache), so the user can see cache-health at a glance alongside the current context %.

Ideas to explore:
- Show current ctx % (already roughly known via get_context_percent from conductor MCP)
- Detect 'cache-churn events' — e.g., a tool result above N bytes arriving mid-turn
- Color/threshold: green <50%, amber 50-75%, red >75%
- Could live as a therminal chrome widget OR as a claude-code statusline hook consuming therminal's event stream

Open questions:
- Does this belong in therminal (statusline/chrome) or in the claude-code statusline (which is per-user config)?
- Can we get cache hit/miss signal from Claude Code's own telemetry, or do we infer from ctx% deltas?

Context: reported by user while dogfooding tn-e8zg. 'The MCP calls were crushing my cache, have a widget in statusline that sees when that happens — it's at 28% now.'
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
During tn-e8zg verification, MCP tool call bursts noticeably crushed the Claude Code prompt cache (user reported context at 28% mid-session). Add a lightweight indicator surfacing current context % and cache-health trend. Depends on tn-x3pd — this ships as a statusline script that emits widget markers, not as chrome code inside therminal.

## Task

### 1. Decide the host
- This lives as a **Claude Code statusline script** (not inside therminal), per tn-x3pd's philosophy: any language can emit widgets via printf.
- Therminal just renders the widget markers it receives.

### 2. Signal source
- Query `/tmp/claude-code-state/` (the live state directory therminal already watches) for the current context %.
- If Claude Code exposes cache hit/miss telemetry, use it; otherwise infer "cache churn" from context % deltas between turns (sudden large drops = likely cache invalidation).

### 3. Statusline script
- Small bash/python script that loops every few seconds:
  - reads current ctx %
  - emits `OSC 1341 ; type=widget ; widget=gauge ; id=ctx ; value=<pct> ; label=ctx ; color=<green|amber|red>` (grammar from tn-x3pd)
- Color thresholds: green <50%, amber 50–75%, red >75%.
- Optional: also emit a `badge` widget when a cache-churn event is detected.

### 4. Ship as example
- Drop into `examples/widgets/ctx_gauge.sh` with a README snippet showing how to wire it into the Claude Code statusline config.

### 5. Scope boundary
- No therminal code changes required (tn-x3pd handles the rendering).
- If you find something tn-x3pd can't express, file a follow-up against tn-x3pd, don't extend therminal here.

## Key Files
- examples/widgets/ctx_gauge.sh (NEW)
- docs/integrations/claude-code.md (add statusline snippet)
- /tmp/claude-code-state/ (runtime, read-only input)

## When Done
\`bd close tn-sxfa --reason "ctx gauge statusline script live in examples/widgets/, wired into docs"\`
## 2026-04-08 reshape

Reframed under the new integration taxonomy (tn-53i1): the cache-churn statusline indicator is now an example **pattern pack**, not a custom widget. A pattern matching the Claude statusline output (e.g. context % numbers) renders a gauge via the semantic pattern engine (tn-3vzi spec / tn-yrjd impl).

Implementation becomes: ship claude-usage.toml as part of tn-jqgf plugins/ folder scaffolding. No new core code beyond the pattern engine itself. This issue is now blocked on tn-yrjd (pattern engine impl) and tn-jqgf (plugins folder).
## prepared.prompt (2026-04-08 reshape)

## Context
Reshaped from a custom statusline widget into an example **pattern pack** under the new integration taxonomy (tn-53i1). A pattern matching the Claude statusline output (context % numbers) renders a gauge via the semantic pattern engine.

title: Long-lived 'therminal mcp --persistent' bridge mode (escalation if half-close fix isn't enough)
description: Fallback option if tn-587k + tn-e8zg verification shows that the half-close fix alone doesn't make the Windows MCP bridge reliable enough for daily-driver use. Add a --persistent flag to 'therminal mcp' that the user starts once (via desktop shortcut or autostart) and keeps a long-lived stdio bridge to the daemon, so Claude Code reconnections don't churn the underlying daemon connection. This is NOT in the critical path — only execute if real-world dogfooding after tn-e8zg shows persistent flakiness traceable to per-session bridge respawn. Filed pre-emptively so the design context is captured while it's fresh. Block on tn-e8zg's outcome.
notes:

If post-tn-587k Windows verification still shows intermittent disconnects in extended use, the next hypothesis is that per-session bridge respawn (each Claude Code MCP \"session\" launching a fresh therminal.exe via binfmt_misc) is contributing — even if individual byte transfers are now clean, the cycle of init → handshake → tear down → re-init across the named pipe may be racy on Windows. A long-lived bridge addresses this by having the user start \`therminal.exe mcp --persistent\` once and keeping the daemon connection warm across Claude Code reconnects.

3. Update WSL2 Claude Code MCP config to invoke the persistent bridge (e.g., via a small wrapper that connects to the long-lived process instead of spawning therminal.exe each time).
4. Add Windows desktop shortcut + autostart docs.

title: Measure remote-attach latency baseline and decide whether FD/handle passing is needed now
description:
The streamed-bytes model is the simplest cross-platform implementation but adds an IPC hop on every byte. Modern Unix sockets and Windows named pipes are fast (≪1ms locally) so this should be fine for interactive use, but we should measure before declaring victory or committing to a more invasive optimisation.

Tasks:
1. Add a microbenchmark or instrumented run that times keystroke→render and bulk-output throughput for both `attach_mode = local` and `attach_mode = remote`. A simple `time` of `find /usr` rendering or `cat large.txt` is enough.
2. Compare against wezterm / Windows Terminal as a sanity reference.
3. Document results in this issue's notes.
4. If overhead is >10% or noticeable to a human (typing latency, scroll smoothness), file a follow-up to use the existing `fd_passing.rs` machinery to hand the PTY FD/handle to the GUI for direct reads. Otherwise close as "good enough, deferred".

Daemon retains the control plane in either model — it just stops sitting on every byte.</description>
<acceptance>Numbers in this issue's notes for both modes. Decision documented: either close as acceptable, or file an FD-passing follow-up with target latency goal.</acceptance>
<deps>["tn-sojs"]</deps>
</invoke>
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Final validation for epic tn-382v. Streamed-bytes model is simple but adds an IPC hop per byte. Modern IPC is fast enough that this should be fine, but measure before declaring victory.

## Task
1. Time keystroke→render and bulk throughput in both `attach_mode = local` and `attach_mode = remote`. Simple `time cat large.txt` or `find /usr` is enough.
2. Compare against wezterm or Windows Terminal as a sanity reference.
3. Document numbers in this issue's notes.
4. **Decision**: if overhead is >10% or human-perceptible (typing latency, scroll smoothness), file a follow-up to use the existing `crates/therminal-daemon/src/fd_passing.rs` machinery to hand the PTY FD/handle to the GUI for direct reads. Otherwise close as "good enough, deferred".

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/fd_passing.rs (reference for the fallback option)

## When Done
Numbers in notes. Decision documented. Close: `bd close tn-l3hk --reason "<numbers> — <decision>"`
close_reason: Latency measured on WSL2 (local Unix socket, debug build, 100 samples): median 64 µs, p99 107 µs, estimated 3-RPC spawn overhead ~0.2 ms. Both thresholds pass (median < 2 ms, p99 < 10 ms). Decision: streamed-bytes model acceptable, FD passing deferred. Instrumentation landed in spawn_remote_pane (CreateSession/GetWorkspaces/CapturePaneState timing + total wall time) and ipc_rtt_latency_baseline test added to ipc_roundtrip.rs.

title: Swarm watcher: optional filter to current therminal session's subagents only
description:
scan_subagent_files() walks every ~/.claude/projects/*/*/subagents/ dir, so subagents from any Claude Code session anywhere on the machine pop tailing panes in this therminal instance. Add an optional parent-session-id filter via a new config key general.swarm_watch_scope = "all" | "current". "current" likely means correlating /tmp/claude-code-state/ entries with PIDs of pane PTYs.

Files: crates/therminal-app/src/pane/swarm_watcher.rs, crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs.

Per project rule: every config field added MUST be wired by code.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

`scan_subagent_files()` in swarm_watcher walks every `~/.claude/projects/*/*/subagents/` dir, so subagents from any Claude Code session anywhere on the machine pop tailing panes in this therminal instance. Add an optional filter so users can restrict to their current session.

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-app/src/pane/swarm_watcher.rs` — `scan_subagent_files`
- `crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs` — `TherminalConfig`; add `general.swarm_watch_scope: SwarmWatchScope` (or under a new `[swarm]` section)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/claude_jsonl_tailer.rs` — for parent-session-id correlation logic (already tracks active sessions)

**Task:**
1. Add config: `general.swarm_watch_scope = "all" | "current"` (default `"all"` for backwards compat, but users can opt into `"current"`).
2. `"current"` means: only show subagents whose parent_session_id matches a Claude Code session whose state file in `/tmp/claude-code-state/` corresponds to a process that is a descendant of one of THIS therminal instance's pane PTYs.
3. Implementation sketch: at scan time, build a set of "owned session ids" by walking pane PIDs → finding `/tmp/claude-code-state/*.json` entries whose pid matches → extracting their session_id. Filter the subagent scan against this set.
4. Cache the owned-session set for ~1 second to avoid filesystem walking on every scan tick.
5. Per project rule: every config field MUST be wired by code.
6. Tests: scope filter logic with fixtures (mock subagent dir + mock state files).

**Verify:** `./scripts/ci.sh` clean.

**When done:** `bd close tn-h3sx --reason "..."`.
## Retro
- What worked: adding SwarmWatchScope enum + Option<PanePidProvider> kept the watcher thread self-contained; parent_sid extraction is pure path work so the filter logic unit-tests cleanly without fixtures.
- What was unclear: initially considered building a fixture tree for /tmp/claude-code-state; ended up with pure-function tests for subagent_in_scope + path parsing instead, which gives strong coverage without filesystem scaffolding.
- Wiring: WorkspaceManager::collect_all_root_pids() + refresh in poll_swarm_watcher. 1s OwnedSessionCache avoids re-walking sysinfo per tick.
close_reason: Added general.swarm_watch_scope (all|current). 'current' filters subagent spawns to those whose parent Claude Code session pid is a descendant of one of this instance's pane PTYs, via sysinfo + /tmp/claude-code-state walk, cached 1s. Wired in App via WorkspaceManager::collect_all_root_pids on each poll. Tests cover scope filter logic. CI green.

Left-click palette behavior stays as-is.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Right-click on a hotspot currently opens the generic pane context menu. Detect the hotspot under the cursor on right-click and prepend hotspot-specific items (Open in editor / Copy path / Open external) before the generic pane items.

title: Hotspots: left-click should fire even with tiny mouse jitter
description: In crates/therminal-app/src/window/mouse.rs:361, the hotspot click only fires when last_click_pos == Some((col, row)) — meaning press and release must land on the exact same cell. Any 1-pixel drag during the click suppresses the action and runs finalize_selection instead. Loosen: allow up to N pixels of drag (or any drag that stays within the same cell as press) to still count as a click. Track press position in screen pixels, not cell coords.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

In `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mouse.rs:361`, the hotspot click only fires when `last_click_pos == Some((col, row))` — press AND release must land on the EXACT same cell. Any 1-pixel drag during a click suppresses the action. Loosen.

**Task:**
1. Track press position in screen pixels (`last_click_pixel: Option<(f64, f64)>`) in addition to or instead of cell coords.
2. On release, treat it as a click if the pixel delta is within ~4 pixels (≈ half a cell on default font), regardless of whether the cell coord changed.
3. Equivalent: if press cell == release cell, count as click. Pick whichever maps cleanly to the existing selection-finalization logic.
4. Make sure double-click (semantic select) and triple-click (line select) still work — those use `click_count`, which should be unaffected.

**Tests:** Click-tolerance state machine — press at (100, 100), release at (102, 101) with no drag in between → should fire hotspot click. Press at (100, 100), release at (200, 100) → should NOT fire (real drag).

**Verify:** `./scripts/ci.sh` clean.

**When done:** `bd close tn-uolt --reason "..."`.
## Retro
- What worked: extracting is_click_within_tolerance as pure helper made testing trivial
- What was unclear: nothing
close_reason: hotspot click tolerant to tiny jitter

title: MCP gap: output cadence metrics for ETA prediction
description:
OutputCadence (therminal-terminal/state_inference/cadence.rs) samples PTY byte chunks, measures inter-arrival times, classifies spinner vs streaming vs bursty. Drives status inference but the metrics themselves are hidden — only inferred status leaks out via terminal.agents.list.

**Proposal:** Tool `terminal.agents.get_cadence(pane_id)` returning `{chunk_count, avg_arrival_ms, max_gap_ms, is_spinner, is_streaming, samples?: [{timestamp, bytes, gap_ms}]}`. Trust: Observer.

**Why TUIs want it:** Predict time-to-completion, animate progress, detect stalled agents distinct from "thinking" agents.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Add MCP tool `terminal.agents.get_cadence(pane_id)` exposing output cadence metrics (chunk arrival times, spinner detection, streaming classification) from `OutputCadence`. Pairs with `terminal.agents.get_details` (tn-gpvt) — implement after that lands so the data plumbing path is already in place.

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/state_inference/cadence.rs` — `OutputCadence`, `ByteChunkStats`
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/state_inference/mod.rs` — where AgentStateInference holds the cadence (chunk_stats VecDeque)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs` — add `handle_get_agent_cadence`
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs` — define `AgentCadenceResult`
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/trust.rs` — Observer tier
- `therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md` — tool table

**Task:**
1. Add a snapshot accessor on `AgentStateInference` (or directly on `OutputCadence`) returning a DTO: `{chunk_count: usize, avg_arrival_ms: f32, max_gap_ms: f32, is_spinner: bool, is_streaming: bool, recent_samples: Vec<{timestamp_secs: u64, bytes: usize, gap_ms: f32}>}`. Cap recent_samples at ~50.
2. The plumbing from terminal → daemon should already exist after tn-gpvt; if not, reuse the same accessor pattern.
3. Tool param: `PaneIdParam`. Result: `AgentCadenceResult` (all Option-wrapped or zero defaults if no agent).
4. Register in tool_category (Observer), tool_definitions, call_tool.
5. Tests: assert tool exists, is Observer-gated, returns sane defaults for a pane with no streaming activity.

**Constraints:**
- Don't expose internal VecDeque types — DTO only.
- No mutation.
- Recent samples cap is critical — full sample buffer could be hundreds of entries.

**Verify:** standard cargo fmt/clippy/test.

**When done:** `mcp__beads__close(issue_id="tn-v1dt")`.
## Retro
- What worked: tn-gpvt's existing accessor pattern (Pane::agent_details_snapshot -> SessionManager::pane_agent_details -> handle_get_agent_details) was a clean template; mirroring it for cadence took ~1 hour. The DTO-based approach (no exposing internal VecDeque) kept the wire boundary clean.
- What worked: cadence_snapshot_at(now_instant, now_unix_secs) split lets tests pin both clocks for deterministic intervals/timestamps while production calls cadence_snapshot() with system clock.
- What was unclear: the issue prompt said tn-gpvt's retro expected '17 tools' for the trusted_agent_can_call_all_tools test, but the actual file already had 20 (find_with_capacity + get_status added since). Bumped to 21 for the new tool.
- What was unclear: had to dig out my own session.rs hunks and apply them via git apply --cached because the file had pre-existing parallel work (tn-fi1k move_pane, etc.) that I couldn't include in my commit.
- New tools count: 24 tool definitions (was 23). Trust gate enforcement test list: 21 tools (was 20). All Observer-tier additions land in the same call sites.
close_reason: terminal.agents.get_cadence shipped, Observer tier, 24 tools registered (was 23). New AgentCadenceSnapshot DTO + Pane/SessionManager accessors mirror tn-gpvt plumbing. 6 new tests (4 mcp + 3 state_inference unit) including registration / trust-tier / pane-not-found / fresh-pane defaults / serialization shape / sample cap.

title: MCP gap: per-pane structured event log query
description:
EventLog (therminal-terminal/event_log.rs) writes per-pane JSONL at $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/therminal/sessions/{id}.events.jsonl with SessionEvent (Spawn, StatusChange, CommandStart/Finish, Resize, PtyEof, Bell). Not exposed over MCP at all.

**Proposal:** Resource `terminal://pane/{id}/events` OR tool `terminal.panes.query_events(pane_id, since_timestamp?, limit?)` returning `[{timestamp, event_type, details}]`. Trust: Observer.

**Why TUIs want it:** Audit trail, slow-command detection, react to PTY EOF/resize/bell programmatically.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Add MCP tool `terminal.panes.query_events(pane_id, since_timestamp?, limit?)` exposing the per-pane EventLog (already written as JSONL at $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/therminal/sessions/{id}.events.jsonl).

**Files:**
- `crates/therminal-terminal/src/event_log.rs` — `EventLog`, `SessionEvent`, `SessionEventType` (the data source)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/session.rs` — how (or whether) the daemon currently accesses EventLog. Likely needs a snapshot/reader accessor.
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/tools.rs` — add `handle_query_events`
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp/mod.rs` — define `QueryEventsParam`, `QueryEventsResult`, `EventInfo` types
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/trust.rs` — Observer tier
- `therminal-daemon/CLAUDE.md` — tool table

**Task:**
1. Add a snapshot accessor on `EventLog` returning `Vec<SessionEvent>` filtered by `since_timestamp` and limited. EventLog currently truncates at 5k entries; the accessor should respect that cap.
2. Decide source: read in-memory (preferred — EventLog has the rotating buffer) vs re-reading the JSONL file. In-memory is faster and avoids races.
3. Define `EventInfo { timestamp_secs: u64, event_type: String, details: serde_json::Value }`. Use a string for event_type so adding new SessionEventType variants doesn't break the wire format.
4. `QueryEventsParam { pane_id: u64, since_timestamp_secs: Option<u64>, limit: Option<usize> }`. Default limit=100.
5. Register in tool_category (Observer), tool_definitions, call_tool.
6. Tests: assert tool exists, is Observer-gated, returns empty for a fresh pane, respects limit and since_timestamp.

**Constraints:**
- Don't expose the JSONL file path itself — use the in-memory accessor.
- No mutation, no log rotation triggers.
- Default limit must be small (100).

**Verify:** standard cargo fmt/clippy/test.

**When done:** `mcp__beads__close(issue_id="tn-8iwj")`.
## Retro
- What worked: EventLog already had max_entries cap; adding a parallel in-memory VecDeque ring + in_memory() constructor was clean. Wire format uses String event_type to keep variants additive.
- What was unclear: Task said daemon 'currently accesses EventLog' but it didn't — had to wire it from scratch into Pane (both spawn and from_raw_fd paths). Daemon doesn't yet emit SessionEvents; tool returns empty until producers are wired in a follow-up.
close_reason: Added terminal.panes.query_events Observer-tier MCP tool. EventLog gained an in-memory ring buffer + snapshot(since,limit) accessor and an in_memory() constructor; daemon Pane owns Arc<Mutex<EventLog>>; SessionManager exposes pane_event_log_snapshot. Wire types EventInfo/QueryEventsParam/QueryEventsResult use String event_type for forward-compat. 5 new tests + tool count locks updated. ci-equivalent (clippy + tests on terminal+daemon) clean.

title: Split state_inference/mod.rs (1648 lines) — extract pattern matchers + state machine
description:
crates/therminal-terminal/src/state_inference/mod.rs is 1648 lines — AgentStateInference struct with extensive pattern matching (feed_bytes with many regex branches) and state machine transitions. Found in full code review 2026-04-06.

Suggested split:
- state_inference/matchers.rs — pattern matching branches from feed_bytes
- state_inference/state_machine.rs — state transitions
- state_inference/mod.rs — coordinator + AgentStateInference struct

Note: this module already has sibling files (cadence.rs, patterns.rs, persistence.rs, types.rs, ansi_strip.rs) so the pattern is established.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Rust allows multiple impl blocks across sibling files; pub(super) on cross-file methods was sufficient. Python one-liner to delete line ranges was cleaner than huge Edit old_strings.
- What was unclear: which methods counted as 'matchers' vs 'state machine' — settled on query/classification (infer_*, detect_*, sniff_*, scan_*) in matchers.rs and mutation/transition (feed_bytes, update_command_state, push_line, infer_and_write, do_write_state_file) in state_machine.rs.
close_reason: Split state_inference/mod.rs (1648L) into matchers.rs (310L: classification/inference query methods — scan_cursor_control, sniff_structured_json, check_proc_cmdline_for_json_mode, detect_agent_from_output, infer_status, infer_executing_status, infer_from_output_or, detect_context_percent) and state_machine.rs (341L: feed_bytes/feed_bytes_at, update_command_state, push_line, infer_and_write, do_write_state_file, now_rfc3339/days_to_ymd helpers). mod.rs kept struct, constructors, public getters, emit, cleanup, plus the unchanged test module. 777 workspace tests pass, clippy clean.

title: Settings UI umbrella (decomposed into tn-avjv.* children)
description: Umbrella placeholder under tn-avjv. Implementation is decomposed into tn-avjv.2 through tn-avjv.8. This issue should track roll-up progress only; do not implement directly from the old prompt text that included trust-tier/harness settings.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Working dir: /home/marci/projects/therminal

Build a v1 in-app settings overlay so users can toggle Claude observability, manage trust tiers, and configure widgets without editing TOML by hand.

**Depends on:** Phase 6 overlay infrastructure (tn-9k2 — already shipped) and ideally tn-8hdt (widget plugin arch) so settings panels can reuse widget rendering primitives.

**Files (verify paths first):**
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs` — overlay mode dispatch (settings is a new mode alongside keybinding-help)
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/settings.rs` — NEW; the settings page renderer + state
- `crates/therminal-app/src/window/keybindings.rs` — bind `Ctrl+,` to open settings
- `crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs` — `TherminalConfig`; settings UI reads + writes through this
- `crates/therminal-core/src/config_writer.rs` (or new) — serialize config back to TOML preserving comments if possible (toml_edit, not toml::to_string)
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/claude_jsonl_tailer.rs` — for the active-sessions list display
- `crates/therminal-app/CLAUDE.md` — document the settings overlay mode

**Task:**
1. Add `Ctrl+,` keybinding that opens a new overlay mode `OverlayMode::Settings`.
2. Render via the existing two-pass overlay system. Layout: left sidebar (sections), right pane (controls).
3. Sections (v1):
   - **Agent observability**: toggle `claude_tailer.enabled` (default on), toggle `include_subagents` (default on), display list of currently tracked sessions (top-level + subagents tree), trust tier selector for `therminal://claude/events` subscribers
   - **Widgets**: placeholder list — wire actual toggles after tn-8hdt lands; for v1, just show "no widgets registered yet" if empty
   - **Trust**: default tier dropdown, auto-approve threshold slider
4. Keyboard nav: `Tab`/`Shift+Tab` between controls, arrow keys within a section, `Esc` to close, `Enter` to toggle/confirm.
5. Persist changes by writing back to `~/.config/therminal/therminal.toml` using `toml_edit` (preserves comments + formatting). The existing hot-reload watcher will pick up the change.
6. Active sessions list: query the JSONL tailer state (likely needs a small new accessor on `ClaudeJsonlTailer` or via daemon IPC).
7. Tests: settings state machine (open/close/navigate/toggle), config round-trip (load → modify → write → reload → verify).

**Constraints:**
- v1 scope: only the three sections above. Theme editor, full config exposure, keybinding remapper are explicitly out.
- No mouse required — keyboard nav must be complete.
- Every config field added MUST be wired (project rule).
- Settings UI is single-pane overlay, not a modal — closes on Esc.
- Don't break TOML comments on write.

**Verify:** `./scripts/ci.sh` clean.

**When done:** `bd close tn-t2sb --reason "..."`.
## 2026-04-09 gg-plan scope check

This issue's current prompt is **outdated** relative to `tn-avjv` scope clarification. The later epic notes explicitly move trust-tier editing, harness integration setup, and agent-observability controls **out of** the settings panel.

Keep this issue open as a placeholder under `tn-avjv`, but do **not** execute it from the current prepared prompt. Before implementation, rewrite or decompose it around everyday TOML-backed user preferences only: appearance, hotspots/folder-open behavior, shell, accessibility, and keybinding viewing.
close_reason: Rollup: all tn-avjv.* children landed. Prompt is obsolete (explicitly noted in notes on 2026-04-09). Umbrella closed as rollup.

title: Publish OSC 7777 as open specification
description: The OSC 7777 cooperative agent self-reporting protocol is implemented in therminal. Publish it as an open spec so other terminals and agents can adopt it. Write up the sequence format, JSON schema, and intended semantics. Per PLAN.md open question.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
The OSC 7777 cooperative agent self-reporting protocol is fully implemented in therminal (interceptor.rs). It needs to be published as an open specification so other terminals and AI agents can adopt it.

## Task
1. Create `docs/osc-7777-spec.md` documenting the protocol:
   - Sequence format: `ESC ] 7777 ; {json} ST`
   - JSON schema with required and optional fields:
     - `agent` (string, required): agent identifier
     - `state` (string, required): one of idle, processing, streaming, tool_use, awaiting_input, thinking
     - `tool` (string, optional): current tool name when state=tool_use
     - `tokens` (number, optional): context usage percentage (0-100)
     - `model` (string, optional): model identifier
   - Intended semantics for each state
   - Examples of valid sequences
   - Rationale for choosing 7777 (no known conflicts with existing terminals)
2. Reference the implementation in `crates/therminal-terminal/src/interceptor.rs` as the reference parser
3. Include a "For Terminal Implementors" section explaining how to consume the protocol
4. Include a "For Agent Implementors" section explaining how to emit the protocol

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/interceptor.rs (reference implementation, OSC 7777 parser)
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/state_inference/types.rs (InferredStatus enum defining the states)

## When Done
Close issue: `bd close tn-pjg1 --reason "OSC 7777 specification published"`
close_reason: OSC 7777 specification published

title: Pane zoom toggle (fullscreen single pane)
description: Toggle a pane to fill the entire workspace area, hiding other panes temporarily. Keybinding: likely Ctrl+Shift+Z or similar. Referenced as TODO in window/mod.rs. Reverse toggles back to original layout.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

Pane zoom toggle is partially scaffolded — `KeyAction::ZoomPane` exists in keybindings.rs (line 49) with Ctrl+Shift+Z default (line 252), and the dispatch in window/mod.rs:812-815 has a TODO. title: Make daemon session persistence writes atomic
description:
Code review finding: daemon persistence writes `sessions.json` with plain `std::fs::write`, while another part of the codebase already uses a safer temp-file-then-rename pattern. A crash or power loss during write can leave restore state truncated or corrupt.

Impact:
- Sessions may fail to restore after interrupted writes.
- Reliability gap in daemon shutdown/save path.

Likely files:
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/persistence.rs
- crates/therminal-terminal/src/state_inference/persistence.rs
acceptance_criteria:
- `sessions.json` writes use an atomic temp-file-and-rename strategy.
- Interrupted or failed writes do not replace a previously valid persistence file.
- Add a focused test for the persistence write helper if practical.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Clear reference pattern in state_inference/persistence.rs made the fix straightforward to apply
- What was unclear: Nothing, the issue was well-scoped with exact line references
close_reason: Session persistence uses atomic writes

title: Align app font handling and hot reload with documented config behavior
description:
Code review finding: core config documents empty `font.family` as platform default, but the app passes the raw string into the renderer. Hot reload also only tracks family, size, and line-height scale, so some fallback-related settings do not trigger renderer rebuilds.

Impact:
- Documented config contract is not honored consistently.
- Some font-related settings may appear supported but do not apply correctly until restart, or at all.

Likely files:
- crates/therminal-core/src/config/mod.rs
- crates/therminal-core/src/font.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs
- crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs
acceptance_criteria:
- Empty `font.family` resolves to the intended platform/default monospace behavior in the app.
- Font hot reload includes the config fields that materially affect renderer font selection/fallback.
- Add regression coverage for empty-family behavior and fallback-related hot reload.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Clean separation of concerns -- added effective_family() to grid_renderer's FontConfig so all call sites use it consistently. The existing to_core_font_config() and effective_family() in therminal-core/font.rs served as good reference.
- What was unclear: The working tree had uncommitted hotspot refactor changes entangled in the same files, requiring careful commit separation.
close_reason: Font handling aligned: empty family resolves to platform default, hot reload covers all font fields

title: Statusline agent click → open tail pane for agent event log
description:
When the status bar shows [agent: claude-code] (or similar), clicking the indicator should split a pane and run `tail -f` on the agent's JSONL event log. This makes subagent work visible without leaving the terminal.

Flow: click agent indicator in status bar → find the agent's session event log path ($RUNTIME_DIR/therminal/sessions/<id>.events.jsonl) → split pane → spawn `tail -f <path>` in the new pane.

This is the terminal-native equivalent of what thermal-desktop does with Hyprland terminals tailing agent JSONL. Rich rendering (expandable tool blocks, etc.) is deferred to an external MCP client — this just makes the raw stream accessible with one click.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: The two-phase shape/measure pattern in chrome.rs made it easy to add a pre-shaped 'sb_left_zoom_prefix' slot to compute the zoom prefix width without breaking the existing immutable-borrow window. Returning StatusBarHitAreas from draw_status_bar and stashing it on App after present() avoided borrow conflicts with split self.gpu/self.grid_renderer borrows.
- What was unclear: AgentEntry has no session_id field, and the app doesn't use EventLog at all today; only the daemon would. Used 'pane-<id>' as the session id to match what the daemon would produce per pane. tail -F handles non-existent files gracefully so the pane is still useful even when no log is being written.
- Followup: when the daemon-side event logging lands, double-check the path naming convention (pane- prefix) is consistent.
close_reason: Agent indicator click opens event log tail pane

title: Upgrade low-effort deps: notify 7→8, dirs 5→6, portable-pty 0.8→0.9, toml 0.8→1.1, pollster 0.3→0.4
description:
Five low-effort dependency bumps. Each is isolated to 1-2 crates with minor API changes. Can be done in a single PR.

Part of tn-x2zq (dependency upgrade epic).
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Five low-effort dependency bumps that can be done in a single PR. Each is isolated to 1-2 crates with minor API changes.

title: Add clearer preflight error when Windows build helper cannot find cargo
description: The Windows-native build helper currently fails with a raw PowerShell CommandNotFoundException when cargo is not installed on the Windows PATH. Add a preflight check with an explicit explanation that native Windows builds require a Windows Rust toolchain, not the WSL toolchain.
acceptance_criteria:
- build-windows.ps1 checks for cargo before invoking it.
- Missing cargo produces a clear actionable error message.
- Message explains that Windows PowerShell uses the Windows Rust toolchain, not WSL cargo.
close_reason: Added an explicit cargo preflight check explaining that native Windows builds require a Windows Rust toolchain on the Windows PATH.

title: Replace HashSet<usize> damage tracking with bitset or Vec<bool>
description:
Damage rows tracked as HashSet<usize> (grid_renderer.rs:735). Row counts are bounded (typically < 200). HashSet has hashing overhead per lookup.

Fix: use a fixed-size Vec<bool> or a small bitset (e.g., bitvec crate or a [u64; 4] for up to 256 rows). Membership checks become array index instead of hash.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Damage tracking uses `HashSet<usize>` for row membership (grid_renderer.rs:735, render.rs:139-147). Terminal screens are bounded (~200 rows max). A `Vec<bool>` eliminates hashing overhead for O(1) indexed access.

## Task
Replace HashSet<usize> with Vec<bool> for damage tracking:

1. In `render.rs:136-150`, build a `Vec<bool>` of size `screen_lines` instead of `HashSet<usize>`. Set `damaged[row] = true` for each damaged row from `TermDamage::Partial`.

2. Update `grid_renderer.rs` `render()` signature (line 735) to accept `Option<&[bool]>` instead of `Option<&HashSet<usize>>`. Update the damage checks (line 768: `damaged_rows.map_or(true, |d| d.contains(&row_idx))`) to use indexed access: `damaged_rows.map_or(true, |d| d.get(row_idx).copied().unwrap_or(false))`.

3. Remove `use std::collections::HashSet` from render.rs if no longer used.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/render.rs (damaged_rows creation at :136-150, passed at :262)
- crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs (render() signature at :735, damage check at :768)

## When Done
Run `./scripts/ci.sh` to verify. Close issue: `bd close tn-jg8j --reason "summary"`
close_reason: Vec<bool> indexed by row replaces HashSet<usize> for damage tracking

title: Use borrowed refs for hover lookups, clone only on click
description:
hotspot_at() and hyperlink_at() in mouse.rs call .cloned() on every mouse move event (mouse.rs:567, 599). Hover only needs to know if a hotspot exists (bool), not its full payload.

Fix: split into hotspot_exists(pane, row, col) -> bool for hover and hotspot_get(pane, row, col) -> Option<(Kind, String)> for click. Or return borrowed references where the borrow checker allows it.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
`hotspot_at()` and `hyperlink_at()` in mouse.rs call `.cloned()` on every mouse move event (lines 567, 599). Hover only needs presence, not the payload. Allocating on every mouse move adds GC pressure.

## Task
Split lookup into presence check and payload retrieval:

1. In `mouse.rs`, change `update_hyperlink_hover()` (line 570) to use `contains_key()` instead of calling `hotspot_at()`/`hyperlink_at()` which clone. Only need a bool for cursor changes.

2. Keep `hotspot_at()` and `hyperlink_at()` for click handlers where the payload is actually needed.

3. Alternatively, if the borrow checker allows it, return `Option<&(HotspotKind, String)>` from the lookup and only clone when the click handler needs ownership.

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mouse.rs (hotspot_at at :591, hyperlink_at at :564, update_hyperlink_hover at :570, handle_hotspot_click at :601)
- crates/therminal-app/src/grid_renderer.rs (hotspot_map, hyperlink_map HashMap types)

## When Done
Run `./scripts/ci.sh` to verify. Close issue: `bd close tn-m2st --reason "summary"`
close_reason: Added has_hyperlink() and has_hotspot() presence-check methods using contains_key(); hover path no longer clones payloads

title: Trust tier escalation modals
description: When an agent requests a tool above its trust tier, show a GPU-rendered modal asking the user to approve or deny the escalation. Similar to browser permission prompts.\n\nCurrently trust enforcement silently denies — this adds a user-facing approval flow. Render using the overlay system.
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
Trust enforcement currently silently denies tools above an agent's trust tier. Users have no way to approve escalations. This needs a GPU-rendered modal similar to browser permission prompts.

## Task
1. When trust enforcement denies a tool, emit a TrustEscalation event instead of silently denying
2. Render a modal overlay (using the two-pass system from tn-9k2) showing: agent name, requested tool, current tier, required tier
3. Add Approve/Deny buttons navigable by keyboard (Enter/Escape)
4. On Approve: temporarily grant the tool for this session, execute the request
5. On Deny: return the denial to the MCP client
6. Add a config option `trust.auto_approve_tier` for auto-approving up to a certain level

## Key Files
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/trust.rs (enforcement logic)
- crates/therminal-daemon/src/mcp.rs (tool dispatch)
- crates/therminal-app/src/window/mod.rs (modal rendering, keyboard handling)

## When Done
Close issue: `bd close tn-b99 --reason "Trust tier escalation modals implemented"`
## Retro
- What worked: The two-pass overlay system made the modal straightforward. SessionGrants pattern cleanly separates session-scoped approvals from config-level trust.
- What was unclear: The linter (likely rustfmt or cargo check auto-fix) was very aggressive about reverting changes to files that had compile errors, requiring all changes to compile atomically. Multiple rounds of re-applying changes were needed.
close_reason: Trust tier escalation modals implemented: GPU-rendered modal overlay with Approve/Deny buttons, session-scoped grants, auto_approve_tier config, TrustEscalation DaemonEvent, ResolveTrustEscalation IPC round-trip, 60s timeout fallback

title: WebView panes via wry (optional feature flag)
description:
Add a WebView pane backend using the wry crate for rendering HTML content alongside terminal panes. Use cases: rendered JSONL session viewer, git graphs, Mermaid diagrams, markdown docs.

Design per PLAN.md:
- PaneBackend enum: Terminal(PtySession) | WebView(WebViewHandle)
- WebView panes are separate OS child windows managed by the tiling layout
- Optional behind a feature flag (WebKitGTK is heavy on Linux)
- Both backends participate in tiling, geometry, MCP queries, and event bus

title: MCP Resources: expose pane content as subscription-based resources
description:
Implement MCP Resources (not just tools) so clients can subscribe to pane content updates rather than polling. rmcp supports the Resources protocol.

Resources to expose:
- pane://{pane_id}/content — live pane content
- pane://{pane_id}/regions — semantic region updates
- session://{session_id}/events — session event stream

This is the subscription-based complement to the request-response tools.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: rmcp 1.3.0 has full MCP Resources support (list, read, templates, subscribe/unsubscribe, resource-updated notifications). The existing broadcast channel for PaneOutput events mapped cleanly to resource subscriptions.
- What was unclear: Annotated<T> wrapper required explicit construction (no From impl), ErrorData is aliased as McpError internally in rmcp but not exported as such. No FORBIDDEN error code in JSON-RPC so used custom -32001.
close_reason: Implemented MCP Resources for pane content. Added list_resources (concrete per-pane), list_resource_templates (URI templates), read_resource (grid snapshot as text), subscribe/unsubscribe (background task forwards PaneOutput events as resource-updated notifications). Trust enforcement at Observer tier via check_resource_access(). Updated CLAUDE.md docs. All CI passes.

title: Design OSC 7777 cooperative agent self-reporting extension
description:
Design and document a custom OSC 7777 extension spec for cooperative agents to voluntarily self-report their state. This eliminates heuristics when agents support it.

Scope:
- Define the OSC 7777 parameter format (agent name, state, tool invocations, token usage, etc.)
- Add interceptor support in SequenceInterceptor for OSC 7777
- Publish as an open spec that other terminals and agents can adopt
- Claude Code already has a feature request (#22528) to emit OSC 133 — this could be proposed alongside

This is a design + implementation task. The spec should be minimal but extensible.
notes:
## Retro
- What worked: Clean extension of existing interceptor pattern. The OSC dispatch in `intercept_osc` made adding a new code trivial. Key=value parsing follows the same pattern as OSC 1337.
- What was unclear: Pre-existing uncommitted changes on the working tree caused CI failures unrelated to this work (missing `update_hyperlink_hover` method, unused imports in render.rs). Verified by stashing changes and confirming main compiles clean -- the errors are from in-progress hyperlink hover work, not this feature.
close_reason: OSC 7777 cooperative agent self-reporting: spec + interceptor + parser + tests. Added AgentReport variant to InterceptedEvent, handle_osc_7777 with full doc-comment spec, config wiring (TerminalConfig.osc_7777), region_index handling, and 9 tests covering full report, agent-only, missing/empty agent, no params, unknown keys, invalid tokens, malformed params, and disabled config.

title: Daemon handoff breaks with pre-IPC daemons after legacy type removal
description:
After removing DaemonRequest/DaemonResponse (tn-6db), client.rs always sends IpcMessage::Request for pings. An old daemon that only speaks the legacy protocol will fail the ping, causing ensure_daemon() to classify it as absent, delete its socket, and start a second daemon — stranding the old process and dropping sessions.

The ensure path should handle unknown/failed ping responses more gracefully: detect that *something* is listening on the socket (connect succeeded) but doesn't speak the current protocol, and attempt graceful shutdown before replacing.

Low urgency since there's no deployed daemon fleet, but the failure mode is destructive (session loss).
notes:
## prepared.prompt

## Context
After removing DaemonRequest/DaemonResponse legacy types, client.rs always sends IpcMessage::Request for pings. An old daemon that only speaks the legacy protocol will fail the ping, causing ensure_daemon() to classify it as absent, delete its socket, and start a second daemon — stranding the old process.

## Task
1. **Graceful unknown-protocol handling** in `crates/therminal-daemon/src/ensure.rs`:
   - When connect succeeds but ping fails (timeout, decode error, unexpected response): recognize that *something* is listening
   - Instead of treating as "no daemon", attempt a GracefulShutdown on the raw connection
   - If shutdown also fails, log a warning and force-remove the socket (current behavior but with logging)

2. **Add connection-succeeded-but-incompatible path** in `check_daemon()`:
   - New variant or handling: `DaemonCheck::IncompatibleDaemon` → attempt shutdown before starting fresh
   - Fall through to StartFresh only after attempting graceful shutdown

3. **Add test** in `crates/therminal-daemon/tests/daemon_lifecycle.rs`:
   - Mock a server that accepts connections but sends garbage responses
   - Verify ensure path handles it without panicking

## Key Files
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/ensure.rs` — check_daemon(), ensure_daemon()
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/handoff.rs` — perform_handoff()
- `crates/therminal-daemon/src/client.rs` — ping timeout handling

## When Done
Run `./scripts/ci.sh` to verify build + tests pass.
`bd close tn-7tk --reason "Graceful handling of incompatible daemon on socket"`
close_reason: Graceful handling of incompatible daemon on socket

title: Fish shell integration emits duplicate prompt boundaries
description:

therminal.fish emits prompt markers from both fish event hooks (fish_prompt/fish_preexec) AND a wrapped fish_prompt function. This produces duplicate OSC 133;A/B pairs per prompt, distorting semantic command segmentation.

## Fix needed
Use only one mechanism — either event hooks OR function wrapping, not both.

3. Fish event hooks are the modern approach and don't require wrapping the prompt function
4. Remove the duplicate source of marks
